Many of these narratives are grounded in rigorous qualitative research, illuminating the diverse ways identity is shaped by social positionality, lived experience, the pursuit of individuation, and a willingness to embrace life as a dynamic learning process."
— Jared D. Kass, Lesley University, Author of *A Person-Centered Approach to Psychospiritual Maturation: Mentoring Psychological Resilience and Inclusive Community in Higher Education*

Name: Communicating Ethnic and Cultural Identity
Description:
This intercultural communication text reader brings together the many dimensions of ethnic and cultural identity and shows how they are communicated in everyday life. Introducing and applying key concepts, theories, and approaches--from empirical to ethnographic--a wide variety of essays look at the experiences of African Americans, Asians, Asian Americans, Latino/as, and Native Americans, as well as many cultural groups. The authors also explore issues such as gender, race, class, spirituality, alternative lifestyles, and inter- and intra-ethnic identity. Sites of analysis range from movies and photo albums to beauty salons and Deadhead concerts. Visit our website for sample chapters!

Name: Esperanza Rising
Description:
A modern classic for our time and for all time-this beloved, award-winning bestseller resonates with fresh meaning for each new generation. Perfect for fans of Kate DiCamillo, Christopher Paul Curtis, and Rita Williams-Garcia. Pura Belpre Award Winner * "Readers will be swept up." -Publishers Weekly, starred review Esperanza thought she'd always live a privileged life on her family's ranch in Mexico. She'd always have fancy dresses, a beautiful home filled with servants, and Mama, Papa, and Abuelita to care for her. But a sudden tragedy forces Esperanza and Mama to flee to California and settle in a Mexican farm labor camp. Esperanza isn't ready for the hard work, financial struggles brought on by the Great Depression, or lack of acceptance she now faces. When Mama gets sick and a strike for better working conditions threatens to uproot their new life, Esperanza must find a way to rise above her difficult circumstances--because Mama's life, and her own, depend on it.

Name: Questions of Cultural Identity
Description:
Why and how do contemporary questions of culture so readily become highly charged questions of identity? The question of cultural identity lies at the heart of current debates in cultural studies and social theory. At issue is whether those identities which defined the social and cultural world of modern societies for so long - distinctive identities of gender, sexuality, race, class and nationality - are in decline, giving rise to new forms of identification and fragmenting the modern individual as a unified subject. Questions of Cultural Identity offers a wide-ranging exploration of this issue. Stuart Hall firstly outlines the reasons why the question of identity is so compelling and yet so problematic. The cast of outstanding contributors then interrogate different dimensions of the crisis of identity; in so doing, they provide both theoretical and substantive insights into different approaches to understanding identity.

## Understanding Identity: A Framework for Exploration

Identity is not static; it's a dynamic construct shaped by our interactions, life experiences, historical context, and cultural norms. The degree of self-autonomy we experience is often influenced by the power dynamics within our relationships.

### Introducing Identity Structure Analysis (ISA)

To systematically explore this complex concept, Identity Structure Analysis (ISA) offers a valuable framework. Drawing from psychology, sociology, and social anthropology, ISA provides a set of concepts to understand identity's nature and development. These concepts are applicable across clinical, societal, and cultural settings.

### Key Applications of ISA

ISA can be applied to various areas, including:

*   **Multicultural Contexts:** Analyzing national and ethnic identification in diverse societies.
*   **Social and Urban Environments:** Understanding how gender identity is influenced by social and urban settings.
*   **Psychological Distress:** Investigating identity processes related to mental health challenges.

### Clinical Applications and the Ipseus Tool

The framework extends to clinical applications, examining identity processes linked to psychological distress, such as anorexia nervosa and vicarious traumatization in counselors.

A significant contribution of this work is its integrated approach to self and identity, made practical through the **Ipseus** software.

#### The Ipseus Software Advantage

Ipseus is a tool designed to aid practitioners in conducting ISA. It helps professionals:

*   Develop their skills.
*   Understand clients' self-perceptions within their social world.

The software is effective across all age groups, from early childhood to adulthood, and is designed for intercultural use, allowing for the creation of customized identity instruments. Visit www.identityexploration.com for more information.

### Target Audience

This resource is beneficial for academics and professionals in developmental, social, cross-cultural, clinical, and educational psychology, as well as psychotherapists, sociologists, political scientists, and those in gender studies, ethnic studies, and social policy.

Name: The Conjured Chest
Description:
The authoritative true story of the notorious deadly chest and the victims of the curse placed on it around 1830. The original letter by Virginia Cary Hudson is researched and published by Dr. Beverly Mayne Kienzle, Virginia's granddaughter and retired Harvard professor.

Name: The Pirate's Curse
Description:
The Pirate's Curse: A Swashbuckling Adventure is an epic tale of adventure, treasure, and danger on the high seas. Captain Blackbeard and his crew are the most feared pirates on the Caribbean, sailing the seas in search of treasure and adventure. But when they come across an ancient treasure map that is said to be cursed, they realize that they are in for their greatest challenge yet. As they set sail to find the treasure, they must navigate treacherous waters, face deadly sea monsters, and confront their own inner demons. Along the way, they encounter rival pirate crews, mysterious islands, and haunted caverns, each presenting a new challenge to their quest. But their biggest challenge comes in the form of a powerful pirate queen who seeks revenge against Captain Blackbeard and his crew. With her cunning and ruthless tactics, she threatens to destroy everything they hold dear. As the final showdown approaches, Captain Blackbeard and his crew must use all of their courage, determination, and skill to emerge victorious. They will face their greatest challenge yet, confronting their own fears and the forces of nature itself, in order to claim the treasure that they seek. Full of swashbuckling adventure, daring escapades, and thrilling twists and turns, The Pirate's Curse is a must-read for anyone who loves epic tales of adventure on the high seas. With vivid descriptions, memorable characters, and a gripping plot, this book will keep you on the edge of your seat until the very last page.

The disappearance of the Roanoke Colony remains one of America's most enduring historical mysteries. In the late 16th century, English settlers established a colony on Roanoke Island, only to vanish without a trace. The sole clue left behind was the word "CROATOAN" carved into a post, a cryptic message that has fueled speculation for centuries.

This captivating enigma is explored in the fictional narrative, "The Lost Colony: Shadows of Roanoke." The story follows Dr. Emily Clarke, an archaeologist whose personal connection to the colony's history drives her to seek answers. With a family legacy intertwined with the vanished settlers, Emily arrives on Roanoke Island determined to solve a puzzle that has baffled scholars for over four hundred years.

As Emily and her team excavate the island's past, their investigation uncovers more than just historical relics. They awaken an ancient, malevolent force, leading to a terrifying confrontation. The island itself seems to hold dark secrets, manifesting in strange occurrences, ghostly apparitions, and the emergence of a sinister spirit known as Croatoan, posing a grave threat to the living. The island's very essence appears to conspire against those who seek its hidden truths.

Name: Curse the Dark
Description:
Return to the magical world of the Retrievers and the Cosa Nostradamus, in book 2 of this popular series by Laura Anne Gilman. Once more Wren Valere’s game plan has taken an unexpected direction. She made a bargain with a supersecret magic-watching outfit to protect her and her partner on their last job, but now the Silence is trying to wedge them apart. And ever since she and Sergei began to talk about their "relationship," things have been tricky. Still…they’re a team, and a force to be reckoned with when danger is near. So now they are off to Italy in search of a missing artifact, without any information other than that it’s very old, very dangerous and everyone who gets too close disappears. Compared with what’s going on at home (lonejacks banding together, a jealous demon, tracking bugs needing fumigation…) maybe disappearing wouldn’t be so bad! Originally published in 2005

## Unchronicled: A Curse Unleashed

Auron Sypher, a twenty-five-year-old game developer, leads a seemingly ordinary life alongside his girlfriend, Angie Maldano. Following a demanding work week, Auron, Angie, and a circle of their cherished friends opt for a tranquil evening out in the vibrant city of Seattle. Their night unfolds with its customary enjoyment, but the journey home abruptly shatters their peaceful existence, plunging them into a bewildering and desperate situation.

### The Onset of the Curse

Shortly after narrowly escaping a potentially fatal car accident, the group discovers that a sinister curse has begun to permeate their very beings. The only viable path to breaking free from this affliction lies in meticulously investigating the life and untimely demise of the very individual whose truck nearly ended their lives. The central mystery revolves around the identity of this enigmatic man and his profound connection to the inexplicable curse that now grips them.

### Auron's Unforeseen Transformation

Unbeknownst to Auron, his own true nature remains hidden until an event of unimaginable magnitude forces it to the surface. The narrative then delves into the profound questions of how far he will venture to protect his friends and the woman he loves. As he confronts his deepest fears, Auron is compelled to uncover startling truths about himself and his own capabilities.

Name: Above the Curse
Description:
She's just trying to stop her friend from marrying a blood-sucking leech, and from the way everyone is treating her, you would think she was the bad guy! Phaedra doesn't like leeches, so when one from her past shows up unexpectedly, she's got her stake at the ready. But he's fast, he's slick, and he's got a taste for her blood. Can she escape him when he seems to have all the advantages? Maybe, just maybe, but only with the help of a necromancer and his ghost ship, and a little diversion care of a fire-breathing dragon… Fae Wilds Series Twist & Turns Curse of the Fae Force the Truth Crown & Glory Enemy & Rivals Light in the Dark Dusk and Shadows Called by Midnight Dark Memories Above the Curse Myths and Muses Chase and Hunt USA Today Bestselling Author, W.J. May creates a new paranormal series with a world of Fae and Magic. Escape into a realm of fantasy creatures, love and deception, betrayal and jealousy. Search terms: Fae, fantasy witches, fantasy new adult, paranormal shifter romance, shifter romance, coming of age, vampires, vampires and witches, shifters, shifter, dark fantasy, superhero fantasy ebooks, witches, superhero, paranormal fantasy, paranormal romance, New Adult & College Romance Paranormal, Fairy, new adult, new adult and college, New Adult & College Romance, w.j. may, chronicles of kerrigan, supernatural, romance, mystery, superpowers, paranormal, series, magic, fairytale, fairy tale, sequel series, sequel, Arthurian, dragons

Name: Cursed Objects
Description:
Beware...this book is cursed! These strange but true stories of the world’s most infamous items will appeal to true believers as well as history buffs, horror fans, and anyone who loves a good spine-tingling tale. They’re lurking in museums, graveyards, and private homes. Their often tragic and always bizarre stories have inspired countless horror movies, reality TV shows, novels, and campfire tales. They’re cursed objects, and all they need to unleash a wave of misfortune is . . . you. Many of these unfortunate items have intersected with some of the most notable events and people in history, leaving death and destruction in their wake. But never before have the true stories of these eerie oddities been compiled into a fascinating and chilling volume. Inside, readers will learn about: • Annabelle the Doll, a Raggedy Ann doll that featured in the horror franchise The Conjuring • The Unlucky Mummy, which is rumored to have sunk the Titanic and kick-started World War I • The Dybbuk box, which was sold on eBay and spawned the horror film The Possession • The Conjured Chest, which has been blamed for fifteen deaths within a single family • The Ring of Silvianus, a Roman artifact believed to have inspired J. R. R. Tolkien’s The Hobbit • And many more!

Name: Cursed Artifacts
Description:
Cursed Artifacts explores the captivating world of objects believed to carry misfortune, blending historical context, cultural beliefs, and psychological factors to understand our fascination with cursed objects. It examines how societies throughout history have imbued objects with spiritual significance, sometimes attributing malevolent power to them. The book argues that the perception of an object as cursed arises from a complex interplay of historical circumstances, cultural narratives, and individual psychological suggestion, not necessarily from inherent evil within the object itself. The book progresses from introducing the concept of cursed artifacts to detailed case studies, like the Hope Diamond and Tutankhamun's Tomb, analyzing how legends and documented events contribute to their cursed reputations. It further delves into psychological phenomena such as confirmation bias and the nocebo effect, illustrating how our minds can shape our experiences. By adopting a balanced and analytical approach, Cursed Artifacts provides a thought-provoking exploration of the human relationship with objects and the stories we tell about them.

Name: The Serpent's Curse
Description:
"Esta and Harte race through time and across the country to steal back the remaining elemental stones needed to bind the book's power, stop the Order, and save the future of the Mageus"--Provided by publisher.

Name: Malice
Description:
An explosive new Conspiracy 365 adventure! Cal, Winter, Ryan, and Boges lived through the 365 days it took to solve the Ormond Singularity, and then barely escaped from a lunatic bent on revenge. Now, a secret from Winter's mysterious past has come back to haunt them . . . With Cal away at flight school and a dangerous mystery to solve, will Winter be able to survive her own 30-day countdown? The clock is ticking, and any second could be her last.

## Fallen Gods: An Empire's Collapse and a Shepherd's Fight

The sprawling empire of Fallen Gods is crumbling under a hidden threat. Corruption has seeped into its political and military foundations, allowing a pervasive darkness to spread from the kingdom's core. Both citizens and leaders are losing their way, their moral compasses broken, making it impossible to distinguish right from wrong. This decay is advancing like a disease, met with little opposition.

In a remote village, a young shepherd lives a simple life, tending his flock and helping his family on their struggling farm. His grandfather's death has left him with the heavy burden of protecting their legacy.

A routine trip to a nearby village turns dangerous when the shepherd encounters a disturbing darkness. This event sparks a quest that will become a fight for survival, a story of enduring love, and a profound sacrifice.

As the shepherd answers the call to action, his innocence fades. His mission becomes clear: save his country and awaken his people from their moral apathy. The empire's future rests on his shoulders as he races against time.

Name: ArtCurious
Description:
A wildly entertaining and surprisingly educational dive into art history as you've never seen it before, from the host of the beloved ArtCurious podcast We're all familiar with the works of Claude Monet, thanks in no small part to the ubiquitous reproductions of his water lilies on umbrellas, handbags, scarves, and dorm-room posters. But did you also know that Monet and his cohort were trailblazing rebels whose works were originally deemed unbelievably ugly and vulgar? And while you probably know the tale of Vincent van Gogh's suicide, you may not be aware that there's pretty compelling evidence that the artist didn't die by his own hand but was accidentally killed--or even murdered. Or how about the fact that one of Andy Warhol's most enduring legacies involves Caroline Kennedy's moldy birthday cake and a collection of toenail clippings? ArtCurious is a colorful look at the world of art history, revealing some of the strangest, funniest, and most fascinating stories behind the world's great artists and masterpieces. Through these and other incredible, weird, and wonderful tales, ArtCurious presents an engaging look at why art history is, and continues to be, a riveting and relevant world to explore.

Name: Awoken by Revenge
Description:
Get swept up in this dramatic enemies-to-lovers romance novel. All he wants is revenge…until he wants her. Claiming Easton House will be billionaire Matteo Bainbridge’s revenge on the family that rejected him. Evicting the home’s sole occupant? A minor detail. Then he meets Louisa Cameron and realizes his hunger for her could be the chink in his ruthless plan for vengeance. Hidden away on the grand estate, innocent Louisa escapes her traumatic childhood by illustrating beautiful fairy-tale realms. Then the outside world intrudes in the form of tycoon Matteo and the astonishing passion he awakens in her. Will surrendering to him destroy her—or set this Cinderella free? From Harlequin Presents: Escape to exotic locations where passion knows no bounds.

Name: Secret Houses of the Cotswolds
Description:
Secret Houses of the Cotswolds is a private tour of 20 of the most beguiling castles, estates, palaces and manor houses in this much-loved region including Asthall Manor, Broughton Castle, Stanway, Sudeley Castle and Hilles House.

Name: A Study Guide to Edgar Allan Poe's Fall of the House of Usher
Description:
A Study Guide to Edgar Allan Poe's Fall of the House of Usher, excerpted from Gale's acclaimed Short Stories for Students series. This concise study guide includes plot summary; character analysis; author biography; study questions; historical context; suggestions for further reading; and much more. For any literature project, trust Short Stories for Students for all of your research needs.

Name: The Amityville Horror
Description:
“A fascinating and frightening book” (Los Angeles Times)—the bestselling true story about a house possessed by evil spirits, haunted by psychic phenomena almost too terrible to describe. In December 1975, the Lutz family moved into their new home on suburban Long Island. George and Kathleen Lutz knew that, one year earlier, Ronald DeFeo had murdered his parents, brothers, and sisters in the house, but the property—complete with boathouse and swimming pool—and the price had been too good to pass up. Twenty-eight days later, the entire Lutz family fled in terror. This is the spellbinding, shocking true story that gripped the nation about an American dream that turned into a nightmare beyond imagining—“this book will scare the hell out of you” (Kansas City Star).

**Unveiling the World's Most Infamous Cursed Objects**

Explore the chilling realm of cursed objects and their unsettling histories. This collection delves into the true, strange tales of notorious artifacts, captivating those fascinated by the paranormal, history, horror, or simply a good scare. These items, often steeped in tragedy and peculiarity, are found in museums, cemeteries, and private collections worldwide. Their dark legacies have inspired countless horror films, TV shows, novels, and campfire stories, proving that some objects carry a potent, misfortune-laden aura.

These ill-fated artifacts have not only witnessed but sometimes influenced significant historical events, leaving a trail of death and destruction. This volume meticulously compiles the authentic narratives behind these eerie oddities, offering a fascinating and chilling exploration of their enduring mystique.

**Discover the Stories of Notorious Artifacts:**

Within this captivating book, you'll uncover the secrets of:

*   **Annabelle the Doll:** The infamous Raggedy Ann doll that inspired *The Conjuring* film series.
*   **The Unlucky Mummy:** An artifact rumored to be connected to the sinking of the Titanic and the start of World War I.
*   **The Dybbuk Box:** An item once sold on eBay that became the basis for the horror film *The Possession*.
*   **The Conjured Chest:** An object linked to fifteen deaths within a single family.
*   **The Ring of Silvianus:** A Roman artifact believed to have inspired J. R. R. Tolkien's *The Hobbit*.
*   **And many more!**

These objects, with their often tragic and always bizarre histories, have left an indelible mark on both history and popular culture. This collection provides a unique opportunity to explore the true stories behind these unsettling relics.

Name: A Family Cursed
Description:
Chronicles the case of the brutal murders of two brothers--Robert Kissel, drugged and beaten to death, a crime for which his wife was convicted, and Andrew Kissel, found stabbed to death in his mansion more than two years later.

Name: The Poe Estate
Description:
Sukie braves the twists and turns of the spooky Poe Annex at the New-York Circulating Material Repository to untangle ancient family secrets, find hidden treasure, and help the ghosts who are haunting her house.

Name: Once Upon a Darkened Night 7-9
Description:
Enjoy this collection of twisted fairy tale retellings by USA Today bestselling author Nicole Zoltack where classical fairytale heroes are now the villians. What if Hansel and Gretel, Alice of Wonderland, Dorothy were the villains? Find out in this collection of twisted fairy tales. Included is an exclusive short story, the Cost of Adoration, a retelling of Thumbelina. KEYWORDS: Hansel and Gretel, Alice of Wonderland, Dorothy, Oz, fairytale fantasy, fantasy romance, romantic fantasy, slow burn romance, supernatural powers, magic, come into powers, dark fantasy romance, clean fantasy, king, prince, royal, historical fantasy, Free Royal, Raven Kennedy, Kelly St. Clare, Caroline Peckham, Susanne Valenti, C.N Crawford, Elise Kova, Robin D. Mahle, Elle Madison, D.K. Holmberg, Cordelia Castel, Kay L Moody, Alisha Klaphe

Name: Once Upon a Darkened Night 10-12
Description:
Enjoy this collection of twisted fairy tale retellings by USA Today bestselling author Nicole Zoltack where classic fairytale heroes are now the villians. What if the Three Little Pigs, Goldilocks, and Jack and the Beanstalk were the villains? Find out in this collection of twisted fairy tales. Included is an exclusive short story, the Cost of Thievery, a retelling of Little Red Riding Hood. KEYWORDS: Three Little Pigs, Goldilocks, and Jack and the Beanstalk, fairytale fantasy, fantasy romance, romantic fantasy, slow burn romance, supernatural powers, magic, come into powers, dark fantasy romance, clean fantasy, king, prince, royal, historical fantasy, Free Royal, Raven Kennedy, Kelly St. Clare, Caroline Peckham, Susanne Valenti, C.N Crawford, Elise Kova, Robin D. Mahle, Elle Madison, D.K. Holmberg, Cordelia Castel, Kay L Moody, Alisha Klaphe

Name: Once Upon a Darkened Night 1-3
Description:
Enjoy this collection of twisted fairy tale retellings by USA Today bestselling author Nicole Zoltack where Cinderella, Sleeping Beauty, and Beauty and the Beast all feature their villains as the heroes. What if the evil stepmother had a reason for locking Cinderella away? What if Sleeping Beauty was cursed to protect her kingdom? What if the beast truly was a beast? Find out in this collection of twisted fairy tales. Included is a never before published, brand new twisted short story, the Cost of Intemperance, a twisted retelling of Twelve Dancing Princess. KEYWORDS: Cinderella, Sleeping Beauty, and Beauty and the Beast, fairytale fantasy, fantasy romance, romantic fantasy, slow burn romance, supernatural powers, magic, come into powers, dark fantasy romance, clean fantasy, king, prince, royal, historical fantasy, Free Royal, Raven Kennedy, Kelly St. Clare, Caroline Peckham, Susanne Valenti, C.N Crawford, Elise Kova, Robin D. Mahle, Elle Madison, D.K. Holmberg, Cordelia Castel, Kay L Moody, Alisha Klaphe

Name: Grimm's Fairy Tales
Description:
Once Upon a Time...Grimm's Fairy Tales have brought magic and delight to millions, throughout the generations all over the world. They collected the oral folktales of their time, which are slightly dark in origin, different from the fairy tales you are used to hearing. In this book, there are timeless classics such as: Little Red-Cap (Little Red Riding Hood) Briar Rose (Sleeping Beauty) Beauty and the Beast Hansel and Grethel Rumpelstiltskin Snow White Tom ThumbCinderellaRapunzel and 55 others...Collected here are 64 of the most popular stories by the Brothers Grimm with 55 black-and-white illustrations and photos, and a free online link to 62 audio files.

Name: The Complete Grimm's Fairy Tales
Description:
Once upon a time in a fairy tale world, There were magical mirrors and golden slippers;Castles and fields and mountains of glass,Houses of bread and windows of sugar.Frogs transformed into handsome Princes,And big bad wolves into innocent grandmothers.There were evil queens and wicked stepmothers;Sweethearts, true brides, and secret lovers. In the same fairy world, A poor boy has found a golden key and an iron chest, and " We must wait until he has quite unlocked it and opened the lid . . ." A classic collection of timeless folk tales by Grimm Brothers, Grimm' s Fairy Tales are not only enchanting, mysterious, and amusing, but also frightening and intriguing. Delighting children and adults alike, these tales have undergone several adaptations over the decades. This edition with black-and-white illustrations is a translation by Margaret Hunt.

Name: Dark Fairy Tales of Fearless Women
Description:
Enter a world in which magic exists, hope wins and every woman's heart is alive with courage! This global feast of ancient tales features valiant women overcoming every kind of obstacle and danger to fulfil their destinies. Travel through Africa, Asia, Europe, the Middle East, North America and the Pacific. Shudder, cheer and laugh out loud as the heroines deal with trolls, faeries, dragons and ghosts; admire their knowledge, wit and cunning; marvel at shapeshifting and other manifestations of the supernatural. A rich collection of fairy tales, beautifully illustrated throughout with Joe McClaren woodcuts, this book is stitched together like a series of Scheherazade stories. It is story-telling at its best, pitch perfect fairy tales of fearless women for readers everywhere.

Name: A Tale Dark & Grimm
Description:
In this mischievous and utterly original debut, Hansel and Gretel walk out of their own story and into eight other classic Grimm-inspired tales. As readers follow the siblings through a forest brimming with menacing foes, they learn the true story behind (and beyond) the bread crumbs, edible houses, and outwitted witches. Fairy tales have never been more irreverent or subversive as Hansel and Gretel learn to take charge of their destinies and become the clever architects of their own happily ever after.

Name: Dark Fairy Tales
Description:
Discover the original macabre stories of your most beloved classics in this collection of disturbingly dark fairy tales. ‘Story-telling is one of the most ancient and universal of arts.’ – Laura F. Kready, A Study of Fairy Tales, 1916 ‘The instruments of darkness tell us truths.’ – William Shakespeare, Macbeth, 1623 This twisted treasury presents over forty of the world’s most wicked fairy tales, including the original versions of stories you thought you knew, such as ‘Cinderella’, ‘Sleeping Beauty’, and ‘Snow White’. Relish in the morbid darkness of these early classics, alongside many other tales you haven't heard before. From renowned storytellers of the fairy tale genre, this collection sources works from the Brothers Grimm, Charles Perrault, Hans Christian Andersen, and many more. Dark Fairy Tales is woven with themes of lost innocence, beastly bonds, and haunted hearts, aiming to inspire, caution, and illuminate valuable aspects of the human experience. Be warned, for these stories are not for the faint of heart and happy endings cannot be guaranteed.

Name: Once Upon a Darkened Night 13-15
Description:
Enjoy this collection of twisted fairy tale retellings by USA Today bestselling author Nicole Zoltack where Aladdin, the Little Mermaid, and Peter Pan all feature their villains as the heroes. What if Aladdin didn't have a heart of gold? What if the Little Mermaid and her father were the villains? What if Peter Pan was the one to bring terror to the high seas of Neverland? Find out in this collection of twisted fairy tales. Included is an exclusive twisted short story, the Cost of Judgment, a twisted retelling of The Ugly Duckling. KEYWORDS: Aladdin, the Little Mermaid, Peter Pan, fairytale fantasy, fantasy romance, romantic fantasy, slow burn romance, supernatural powers, magic, come into powers, dark fantasy romance, clean fantasy, king, prince, royal, historical fantasy, Free Royal, Raven Kennedy, Kelly St. Clare, Caroline Peckham, Susanne Valenti, C.N Crawford, Elise Kova, Robin D. Mahle, Elle Madison, D.K. Holmberg, Cordelia Castel, Kay L Moody, Alisha Klaphe

"Watch Me" is a chilling psychological thriller that explores the dark depths of obsession. As a creative writing professor at Blackwood College, Kate Youngblood finds her life unraveling. Her career is stalled after a disappointing second novel, her marriage has ended, and she feels her vibrant spirit fading with age. She fears becoming invisible, her unique spark dimming until no one truly sees her anymore.

However, one person does see her: Sam Grist, her most talented student. Sam is a gifted writer, drawn to dark and complex narratives. Kate recognizes his raw potential and wants to guide him toward literary success. But Sam's interest in Kate goes beyond mentorship. He has been observing her for years, harboring a deep desire and meticulously planning to enter her life.

As Sam insinuates himself into Kate's world, they become entangled in a dangerous game of deception and forbidden attraction. The novel delves into the extreme lengths of Sam's fixation and the boundaries Kate will allow him to cross. "Watch Me" is a gripping exploration of intense obsession and illicit desire, a testament to the idea that what we crave most can also be our greatest peril. Jody Gehrman masterfully crafts a narrative where the pursuit of desire leads to a perilous precipice.

Name: Just Like You
Description:
You have everything she ever wanted. How far will she go to take it? When swimming instructor Isabel meets up-and-coming actor Iain, the attraction is instantaneous. The pair fall hard for one another and, just a few months later, they are starting to imagine a future together. But everyone has a past... Heartbroken Nicky is convinced that Isabel has stolen the life she was meant to lead. Soon Nicky finds herself becoming fixated on Iain's new girlfriend, and envy quickly turns into a dark, dangerous obsession, with devastating consequences. A chilling psychological thriller that explores love, jealousy and obsession, perfect for fans of Karin Slaughter, Lisa Gray and Robert Dugoni. ********** 'A taut psychological thriller that will keep you on the edge of your seat. A delicious sense of uneasiness settles in and refuses to be shaken. This one will give you chills' Netgalley reviewer 'WOW! What a twisty thriller!' Netgalley reviewer 'I loved this book . . . keeps you on the edge of your seat' Netgalley reviewer

Name: The Edge of Sanity
Description:
A new drug will give you your best—and last—high in this “amazing follow up to Enter the Dark . . . Fast-paced, gruesome and very dark” (The Book Magnet). In a derelict squat, the Smart Man watches as the new narcotic developed by his shadowy organisation wreaks havoc on its unsuspecting victims. The drug is now ready for sale on their exclusive darknet marketplace. Meanwhile, former detective Pete Harris had started a new life away from the Cyber Crimes Unit with his daughter and begins to rekindle his relationship with a former colleague. Unknown to Pete, the retired head of the unit has sought out crime boss Curtis Slater and offered him information—for a price. With his life seemingly complete, Pete’s world comes crashing down as he is drawn into Slater’s game with fatal consequences. He must join forces with his old enemies in a race against time. But can Pete save his loved ones from the clutches of Slater, the Smart Man . . . and a sinister ringmaster? “An action-packed story! Interesting plot threads cleverly woven together, with plenty to keep the reader turning the pages.” —Bibliophile Book Club

Name: BORN EVIL
Description:
WHAT WOULD YOU DO IF YOU FOUND OUT YOUR CHILD IS A PSYCHOPATH? One night when Jennifer Hanson returns home, she catches her 12-year-old son Shane stabbing their cat to death. Thankfully, he doesn't see her. Having long known Shane has a brain with psychopathic tendencies, her worst fears may be confirmed. Ever since he accidentally shot his father to death at age six, she has done everything she can to ensure Shane doesn't become a full-blown psychopath. He has behaved normally up until now. Have her efforts been in vain? She can tell he's changing, becoming increasingly dangerous, but she can't stomach putting him in a psych ward. But then she finds pics of a dead girl in his phone, a girl everyone thought had died accidentally... NOTE: BORN EVIL is a stand-alone psychological thriller with no sex and little violence. CRIME THRILLER, TWIST, GONE GIRL FANS, GILLIAN FLYNN FANS, DARK, TRILOGY, SUSPENSE, SERIAL KILLER, MURDER, MYSTERY

## The Final Act: A Twisted Legacy in "Bad Games"

The "Bad Games" series culminates in its fifth and final installment, a chilling conclusion that revisits the Lambert family a decade after their initial harrowing experiences. What appears to be a thriving family on the surface is a carefully constructed facade. Amy Lambert, a successful entrepreneur, navigates a promising relationship with Allan Brown. Caleb Lambert has achieved respect within the United States Marine Corps, and Carrie Lambert is a flourishing college student. However, this idyllic picture shatters under the weight of harsh realities.

### The Unraveling of the Lamberts

Caleb's once-promising military career is derailed by an increasingly violent temper, leading to his discharge. Carrie finds herself struggling, failing in her second attempt at college. Amy's relationship with Allan is far from functional, revealing the cruelest game of all: the aftermath of trauma.

### A New Threat Emerges

Amidst the family's personal turmoil, Amy Lambert's public success, amplified by a bestseller detailing their past ordeals, attracts dangerous attention. Two aspiring killers, fervent admirers of the infamous serial killers Arty and Jim Fannelli, have been watching. Driven by a twisted desire to emulate their idols, they are eager to demonstrate their devotion to the Lamberts, potentially with the direct involvement of Arty and Jim themselves.

### Prepare for the Ultimate Game

Discover how this horrifying scenario unfolds in the gripping finale of the "Bad Games" series. This final book promises to be the game to end all games, a testament to the enduring terror crafted by author Jeff Menapace.

### What Readers Are Saying:

*   "Will scare you to death... Menapace will become a household name along with Stephen King, Dean Koontz, and Richard Laymon." ★★★★★
*   "Outstanding... I have read horror before like John Saul, Dean Koontz and Stephen King, and they never bothered me as Bad Games did." ★★★★★
*   "Jeffery Deaver look out... this guy plays with your head." ★★★★★
*   "I have found a new author to add to my list of greats." ★★★★★
*   "If any of these appeal to you, I strongly suggest reading this RIGHT NOW!!" ★★★★★
*   "If you like Stephen King, Blake Crouch, or Dean Koontz, then you'll love Jeff Menapace." ★★★★★
*   "I had to keep reading even though I knew it was going to get more twisted. Jeff Menapace is right up there with Stephen King." ★★★★★
*   "If you love J.A. Konrath, Jack Kilborn, and Blake Crouch, you'll love Jeff Menapace." ★★★★★
*   "Compares to my favorite writers Stephen King, Dean Koontz, and John Saul." ★★★★★
*   "A great page-turner with shades of Konrath and King!" ★★★★★

The "Bad Games" series is an ideal choice for fans of dark fiction authors such as Stephen King, Dean Koontz, James Patterson, Karin Slaughter, Lisa Gardner, Jeffery Deaver, John Sandford, L.T. Vargus, Tim McBain, Jack Ketchum, Richard Laymon, John Saul, J. A. Konrath, Blake Crouch, Darcy Coates, Patrick Logan, Willow Rose, and many more. Happy Reading!

Name: What Lies in the Dark
Description:
Suspicion and paranoia engulf a community while a police detective hunts for a serial killer. One murder can make a town nervous. Two brings fear. Add three, four, or even more, and neighbors will start to turn on one another . . . Victoria Bullrush is a stickler for rules and tries to maintain a faultless investigation. But with each grisly killing, the urgency grows greater, and it soon becomes impossible to ignore the public’s growing anger . . . Who is behind the killings? And why are they targeting this community? What Lies in the Dark is a breathtaking debut crime thriller.

**Mother: A Gripping Psychological Thriller**

Discover the dark secrets hidden within a family's past in S.E. Lynes' chilling novel, *Mother*. This psychological drama plunges readers into the life of Christopher Harris, a boy who has always felt like an outsider. His sense of isolation is profound, a constant ache of something missing.

**A Life-Altering Discovery**

Christopher's world is turned upside down when he stumbles upon a hidden suitcase in his family's attic. This discovery unearths a shocking secret about his mother, a revelation that will irrevocably change his understanding of his family and himself.

**What Readers Are Saying:**

*   "An intelligent psychological drama with a gasp-making twist." - Good Housekeeping
*   "Up there with the big hitters. Move over Gone Girl you've got company on the top shelf." - Read & Rated Book Reviews
*   "Breathlessly exciting." - Linda's Book Bag
*   "A sinister, deliciously wicked foray into the dark recesses of our personality. Lynes is a promising addition to the prolific list of stellar writers of psychological thrillers..." - NB Magazine
*   "Sent chills down my spine... completely enticing." - Damp Pebbles
*   "Oozes with atmospheric tones, vivid descriptions and pure, haunting emotions." - Reviewed the Book
*   "S.E. Lynes has raised the bar when it comes to phenomenal story telling." - Between Dreams I Read

**Perfect for Fans Of:**

*   *The Girl on the Train*
*   *The Sister*
*   *Apple Tree Yard*

*Mother* promises to keep you on the edge of your seat until the very last page, leaving you with a lingering sense of unease. Prepare to be captivated by S.E. Lynes' masterful storytelling.

Name: Isolation
Description:
As a deadly epidemic sends the nation into quarantine, one family must face a killer in their home in this tense and timely mystery thriller. Seemingly out of nowhere, a vicious virus puts all of America on lockdown. In their opulent mansion, the wealthy Stone family is better off than most. But they’re not without their problems. Mark, a former tech industry giant, is now an invalid close to death. His wife Brenna is unravelling more each day. And the children are terrified by the constantly shifting news. Then a member of the household staff starts showing symptoms. As paranoia builds, the family’s fragile bonds are put to the test. But the virus is the least of their worries. Because someone has chosen to use this crisis to take the ultimate revenge . . .

Name: Over My Shoulder
Description:
Falling in love with the perfect man leads a young woman into a terrifying trap in this “addictive and intriguing” psychological thriller (Linda Huber, bestselling author of Baby Dear). When Freya meets Kane, he charms her off her feet and whisks her away into his lavish life. Falling in love with him is a dream come true. But as time goes by, his subtle manipulations escalate into something far worse. And as her new life becomes increasingly intolerable, Freya realises escape is out of reach. Kane is deeply controlling, dangerous, and unpredictable. And he’s convinced that Freya has done him wrong. She knows that when she least expects it, he will make good his promise to exact revenge. Freya is no fool, but will she ever be free?

## Discovering the Sixth Stage of Grief: Finding Meaning

David Kessler, a renowned expert in grief and co-author of the seminal work *On Grief and Grieving* with Elisabeth Kübler-Ross, presents a profound new perspective on loss. Building upon the foundational understanding of grief, Kessler introduces a critical sixth stage: **meaning**. This groundbreaking concept moves beyond the commonly recognized stages of denial, anger, bargaining, depression, and acceptance, offering a transformative path toward healing.

### The Evolution of Grief Understanding

In 1969, Elisabeth Kübler-Ross revolutionized our understanding of mortality with her book *On Death and Dying*, identifying the five stages of dying. Years later, she and David Kessler collaborated on *On Grief and Grieving*, extending this compassionate and pragmatic framework to the stages of grief. Now, drawing from extensive personal experience and decades of working with those who are grieving, Kessler unveils a crucial addition to this established model.

### Beyond Closure: The Power of Meaning

Many individuals seek "closure" after experiencing a loss. However, Kessler posits that true transformation lies not in simply moving through the familiar stages of grief, but in discovering meaning beyond them. This discovery can shift the experience of grief from one dominated by pain to one characterized by peace and hope.

### A Roadmap for Honoring Loved Ones

In his latest work, Kessler provides readers with a comprehensive guide to remembering those who have passed with more love than pain. He offers practical strategies for moving forward in a way that deeply honors the memory of loved ones. This insight is both professionally informed and intensely personal.

### A Personal Journey Through Profound Loss

Kessler's own journey with grief began in childhood, marked by witnessing a mass shooting concurrently with his mother's terminal illness. Throughout his career, he has dedicated himself to educating physicians, nurses, counselors, police officers, and first responders on end-of-life care, trauma, and grief. He has also led numerous talks and retreats for individuals navigating their own grief.

Despite his profound professional knowledge, Kessler's life was irrevocably altered by the sudden death of his twenty-one-year-old son. Faced with such an unexpected and devastating loss, the grief expert himself had to find a way to navigate this profound sorrow. This personal quest led him to discover the essence of the sixth stage of grief: finding meaning.

### Essential Tools for Healing

In *Finding Meaning*, Kessler generously shares the insights, collective wisdom, and powerful tools that have proven invaluable to those experiencing loss. This book is an essential contribution to grief literature, serving as a vital guide for healing from immense sorrow. It is an inspiring and deeply intelligent read for anyone seeking to move beyond suffering, navigate loss, and ultimately discover a path toward meaning.

**Exploring Life's Profound Questions: A Philosophical Journey**

The fundamental questions of existence—whether life possesses inherent meaning, the ethics of procreation, the nature of death, the morality of suicide, the desirability of immortality, and the merits of optimism versus pessimism—lie at the heart of philosophical inquiry. David Benatar's anthology, *Life, Death, and Meaning: Key Philosophical Readings on the Big Questions*, offers a compelling exploration of these profound themes. This collection has garnered significant acclaim, becoming a favored resource in both introductory and advanced philosophy courses.

While existentialist philosophers, often associated with the "continental tradition," have extensively addressed these existential quandaries with considerable popular resonance, English-speaking philosophers have historically engaged with these topics less frequently. However, the rigorous analytical methods characteristic of English-speaking philosophy can be effectively applied to these "existential" questions, as demonstrated within this volume.

*Life, Death, and Meaning* thoughtfully curates a selection of primarily English-speaking philosophers' perspectives on these pivotal life questions. The anthology is meticulously organized into six distinct sections: the meaning of life, the creation of new individuals, the concept of death, the act of suicide, the prospect of immortality, and the philosophical stances of optimism and pessimism. Each key reading is enhanced by insightful introductions, thought-provoking study questions, and recommendations for further exploration, ensuring the material is both accessible and engaging for students. Ultimately, this book serves as a unique and valuable introduction to how philosophical thought grapples with the fundamental questions that naturally arise in our lives.

Name: The Death of God and the Meaning of Life
Description:
In our modern, secular, and scientifically-driven world, the question of life's meaning has become a central concern. This profound inquiry, however, is not new; it has occupied the minds of many influential philosophers throughout history. Julian Young masterfully explores this enduring philosophical quest in the engaging second edition of *The Death of God and the Meaning of Life*.

### Historical Perspectives on Meaning

The book begins by tracing the historical lineage of thought on life's purpose. Part 1 delves into the ideas of philosophers from Plato to Marx, examining their beliefs in a meaningful existence, whether rooted in an otherworldly realm or a future-oriented vision of this world.

### The Erosion of Traditional Meaning

Part 2 shifts focus to the consequences when established frameworks that once provided life with meaning began to crumble. In the absence of replacements, this erosion left a void, opening the door to nihilism and the unsettling notion that life might be inherently meaningless.

### Philosophical Responses to Nihilism

Young then examines how prominent thinkers grappled with this existential threat. Chapters are dedicated to the responses of Nietzsche, Heidegger, Sartre, Camus, Foucault, and Derrida, showcasing their diverse approaches to confronting the apparent meaninglessness of existence.

### New Explorations of Meaning

This revised edition introduces three new chapters that expand the scope of the discussion. These explore Søren Kierkegaard's efforts to uphold a Christian perspective on life's meaning, Karl Marx's attempt to reframe this answer in naturalistic and atheistic terms, and Sigmund Freud's profound skepticism regarding the possibility of any such overarching meaning.

Fully updated and revised, this captivating exploration of fundamental questions will resonate with anyone who has pondered the ultimate source of life's significance. It also serves as an excellent historical introduction to philosophy, particularly the continental tradition.

Name: Determinism, Death, and Meaning
Description:
This book offers new arguments for determinism. It draws novel and surprising consequences from determinism for our attitudes toward such things as death, regret, grief, and the meaning of life. The book argues that rationalism is the right attitude to take toward reality. It then shows that rationalism implies determinism and that determinism has surprising and far-reaching consequences. The author contends that the existence of all of humanity almost certainly depends on the precise time and manner of your death and mine; that purely retrospective regret, relief, gratitude, and grief are irrational for all but those who hold extreme values; and that everyone’s life has an unending impact on the future and thereby achieves the strongest kind of meaning that it makes sense to desire. Written in a direct and accessible style, Determinism, Death, and Meaning will be of interest to researchers and advanced students working in metaphysics, philosophy of religion, and value theory, as well as general readers with a serious interest in these topics.

Name: The Meaning of Life and Death
Description:
What is the point of living? If we are all going to die anyway, if nothing will remain of whatever we achieve in this life, why should we bother trying to achieve anything in the first place? Can we be mortal and still live a meaningful life? Questions such as these have been asked for a long time, but nobody has found a conclusive answer yet. The connection between death and meaning, however, has taken centre stage in the philosophical and literary work of some of the world's greatest writers: Fyodor Dostoyevsky, Leo Tolstoy, Soren Kierkegaard, Arthur Schopenhauer, Herman Melville, Friedrich Nietzsche, William James, Ludwig Wittgenstein, Marcel Proust, and Albert Camus. This book explores their ideas, weaving a rich tapestry of concepts, voices and images, helping the reader to understand the concerns at the heart of those writers' work and uncovering common themes and stark contrasts in their understanding of what kind of world we live in and what really matters in life.

## Kierkegaard and Heidegger on Death: A New Perspective

Death is a universal human experience, prompting deep philosophical inquiry throughout history. Philosophers have grappled with its meaning and implications, leading to a rich tapestry of thought. In his work, Adam Buben presents a novel framework for understanding these diverse philosophical perspectives on death.

### Ancient Dichotomies: Plato and Epicurus

Buben begins by exploring two foundational approaches to death in Western philosophy: the Platonic and the Epicurean. These ancient viewpoints offer contrasting ways of conceptualizing our mortality.

### A Modern Synthesis: Kierkegaard and Heidegger

Moving beyond these traditional views, Buben then examines the contributions of Søren Kierkegaard and Martin Heidegger. Their work offers a distinct approach to confronting death, often overshadowing Kierkegaard's own significant insights despite Heidegger's acknowledged debt to the Danish philosopher.

Buben posits that Kierkegaard and Heidegger propose a unique way of coexisting with our mortality. Their philosophy suggests a middle ground, a compromise that bridges the gap between the Platonic and Epicurean traditions. This approach encourages a particular way of living in light of our inevitable end.

## Navigating Spiritual Concerns at Life's End

Facing terminal illness or profound loss often brings spiritual questions to the forefront, regardless of societal secularism. Clinicians and counselors frequently encounter individuals grappling with deeply personal beliefs and values during these critical times.

### Diverse Spiritual Journeys

Consider the varied experiences: an elderly Chinese immigrant requesting to burn incense during hospitalization for a terminal disease. Contrast this with a 30-year-old Roman Catholic gay man dying of AIDS, burdened by moral guilt and questioning long-held beliefs. A mother, grieving her teenage son's death from an aneurysm, feels anger towards God but fears expressing it. Another, a young widower, struggles to articulate his grief, viewing it as a sign of weak faith. These examples highlight the profound spiritual dimensions that emerge when confronting mortality.

### The Enduring Power of Faith

Despite North America's reputation for secularism, spiritual concerns and religious values remain potent forces on an individual level. Surveys consistently show that a significant majority of North Americans believe in God and consider religion important in their lives. This significance is amplified during crises like dying or bereavement, where faith offers solace and support when secular explanations fall short.

### Supporting Caregivers and Clergy

Recognizing this, educators and clinicians have long acknowledged the importance of religious and spiritual themes in counseling the dying and bereaved. However, in religiously diverse cultures like the U.S. and Canada, caregivers and educators may feel ill-equipped to address these complex needs.

"Death and Spirituality" aims to bridge this gap. The book serves two primary audiences.

#### For Counselors and Educators

It provides death-related counselors and educators with an understanding of religious traditions and the spiritual issues that can arise during illness and bereavement. It offers practical interventions, approaches, and resources to help clients explore and resolve these concerns.

#### For Clergy

The book also addresses the needs of clergy, offering guidance on how to support parishioners and others navigating the spiritual and psychological challenges of dying and grief.

Name: Supernatural
Description:
Humans--even those who consider themselves secular or atheists--are utterly seduced by supernatural beliefs. Clay Routledge, an experimental social psychologist who grew up in a deeply religious environment, argues that belief or trust in forces beyond our understanding is natural and rooted in our fears of death. In Supernatural: Death, Meaning, and the Power of the Invisible World, Routledge argues that supernatural thinking is adaptive, even healthy, and that it should unite and not divide us.

**Beware the Night: An NYPD Detective's Encounters with the Demonic**

Ralph Sarchie, a seasoned NYPD detective with sixteen years on the force, dedicates his life to two demanding roles. While he serves the 46th Precinct in New York's South Bronx, his true calling lies in "the Work": investigating cases of demonic possession and aiding in the exorcism of humanity's most ancient and dangerous adversaries.

**Unexplained Crimes and Ancient Evils**

For the first time, Sarchie reveals his investigations into chilling true crimes. These are cases of inhuman evil that defied conventional explanation, remaining unsolved and misunderstood by all but Sarchie and his partner.

**A Chronicle of the Inexplicable**

Trained in exorcism rituals and a firsthand witness to the reality of demonic possession, Ralph Sarchie presents a gripping account of the inexplicable. His experiences reshape our understanding of the darkness that lurks in the shadows.

**Into the Heart of Hell on Earth**

In *Beware the Night*, Sarchie guides readers into the very hierarchy of a hellish existence. He exposes:

*   **Grisly Rituals:** The dark practices of a Palo Mayombe priest.
*   **An Incubus's Victim:** A young girl whose innocence is shattered by a demonic entity.
*   **A Haunted Home:** A house invaded by the malevolent spirit of a murdered 19th-century bride.
*   **Neighbors from Hell:** The disturbing reality of a couple whose true nature was terrifyingly sinister.

**The Supernatural Meets True Crime**

Ralph Sarchie's revelations from his NYPD career forge a powerful and disturbing connection. They link the stark realities of true crime with the bone-chilling grip of supernatural terror.

Name: My Best Friend's Exorcism
Description:
Soon to be a major motion picture. This ENHANCED DIGITAL EDITION features TONS of TOTALLY AWESOME ’80s bonus materials—including Satanic Panic educational pamphlets, a do-it-yourself exorcism cheat sheet, a Spotify playlist of awesome ’80s tunes, animated cover artwork, and much more! From the New York Times best-selling author of The Southern Book Club's Guide to Slaying Vampires, this unholy hybrid of Beaches and The Exorcist blends teen angst and unspeakable horrors into a pulse-pounding supernatural thriller. The year is 1988. High school sophomores Abby and Gretchen have been best friends since fourth grade. But after an evening of skinny-dipping goes disastrously wrong, Gretchen begins to act…different. She’s moody. She’s irritable. And bizarre incidents keep happening whenever she’s nearby. Abby’s investigation leads her to some startling discoveries—and by the time their story reaches its terrifying conclusion, the fate of Abby and Gretchen will be determined by a single question: Is their friendship powerful enough to beat the devil?

Name: Possessed
Description:
"The Exorcist", a 1973 movie about a twelve-year-old girl possessed by the Devil, frightened people more than any horror film ever did. Many moviegoers sought therapy to rid themselves of fears they could not explain. Psychiatrists coined the term "cinematic neurosis" for patients who left the movie feeling a terrifying presence of demons. At the Washington premiere, a young woman stood outside the theater, trembling. "I come out here in the sunlight," she said, "and I see people's eyes, and they frighten me." Among the few moviegoers unmoved by the horror were two priests, Father William S. Bowdern and Father Walter Halloran, members of the Jesuit community at St. Louis University. "Billy came out shaking his head about the little girl bouncing on the bed and urinating on the crucifix," Halloran remembers. "He was kind of angry. 'There is a good message that can be given by this thing,' he said. The message was the fact that evil spirits operate in our world." Bowdern and Halloran knew that the movie was fictional veneer masking a terrible reality. Night after night in March and April 1949, Bowdern had been an exorcist, with Halloran assisting. Bowdern fervently believed that he had driven a demon from a tormented soul. The victim had been a thirteen-year-old boy strangely lured to St. Louis from a Maryland suburb of Washington. Bowdern's exorcism had been the inspiration for the movie. The true story of this possession, told in Possessed, is based on a diary kept by a Jesuit priest assisting Father Bowdern. The diary, the most complete account of an exorcism since the Middle Ages, is published for the first time in this revised edition of Possessed.

**Demonic Possession: The Holy Water Incident**

This is the true story of William Dorian and his daughter Brittany, a chilling account of demonic possession. Brittany's ordeal began at fifteen, plunging her family into a nightmare of torment and suffering. The story shows the immense trauma malevolent entities can inflict, destroying an ordinary family's peace.

**Facing Disbelief and Isolation**

As Brittany's possession worsened, her family felt lost and alone. Local religious leaders offered little help. Doctors recommended only one thing: a psychiatric unit. With minimal support from ministers and doctors, Brittany and her father faced a grueling spiritual battle. Their resolve was tested, but they persevered.

**The Descent into Spiritual Warfare**

The terrifying ordeal started innocently with a spirit communication board session. However, the situation quickly escalated. Multiple demonic entities took control of the fifteen-year-old's life. This gripping narrative reveals the profound trauma possession can cause. It highlights the extreme measures a desperate family will take to save their daughter from malevolent forces. The spiritual warfare that followed changed their lives forever.

Name: The Devil in Connecticut
Description:
The true story that is the basis for The Conjuring 3: The Devil Made Me Do It opening in theaters June 4, 2021. It began with the lust of demons and the corruption of a young boy named David Glatzel. It ended with a murder trial that made headlines across the country--the trial of Arne Cheyenne Johnson, accused of brutally knifing a friend to death. Johnson's defense startled the nation: not guilty, by reason of demonic possession. Here is the horrifying true story of what happened on terrible summer in the sleepy town of Brookfield, Connecticut. How the Glatzels, an average suburban family, came under a terrifying demonic attack that changed their lives. How their eleven-year-old son, David, suffered monstrous visitations by an entity he could only identify as "the Beast." How a close friend, Arne Cheyenne Johnson, became the tragic victim of forces beyond anyone's control...and how the Glatzels' ordeal has still not ended.

Name: The Last Days of Jack Sparks
Description:
"Ingenious and funny . . . Magnificent." -- Alan Moore, creator of Watchmen and V for Vendetta Jack Sparks died while writing this book. It was no secret that journalist Jack Sparks had been researching the occult for his new book. No stranger to controversy, he'd already triggered a furious Twitter storm by mocking an exorcism he witnessed. Then there was that video: forty seconds of chilling footage that Jack repeatedly claimed was not of his making, yet was posted from his own YouTube account. Nobody knew what happened to Jack in the days that followed -- until now. "Wow. Seriously hard to put down." -- M. R. Carey, author of The Girl With All the Gifts

**Explore the chilling reality of spirit possession with "The Penguin Book of Exorcisms."**

This collection delves into centuries of real-life exorcism accounts from across the globe. Discover how phenomena like levitation, superhuman strength, and unearthly stares have been interpreted as signs of spirit possession for millennia. Even in the 21st century, the Vatican held a weeklong seminar in 2019, gathering 250 priests from 50 countries to discuss the practice of exorcism.

"The Penguin Book of Exorcisms" presents a fascinating array of documented cases. Witness Saint Anthony's battles with demonic manifestations, the Prophet Muhammad's expulsion of an evil spirit from a boy, and the intriguing tales of fox spirits in medieval China and Japan. The book also recounts a headless bear's assault in 16th-century England, the mass possession of Ursuline nuns in Loudun, France, and a Zulu woman's daily levitation.

Further accounts include a previously unpublished 1928 exorcism in Earling, Iowa, which inspired the film "The Exorcist." You'll also find the 1949 Maryland poltergeist case that formed the basis for William Peter Blatty's novel, a Filipina girl's encounter with "devils," and a rare letter from a priest seeking a bishop's permission for an exorcism after observing a boy walk backward up a wall. With 57% of Americans believing in demonic possession, this book offers compelling narratives that might just sway your own beliefs. As a Penguin Classic, it joins a distinguished series known for its authoritative texts and scholarly introductions.

Name: The Devil Within
Description:
A fascinating, wide-ranging survey of the history of demon possession and exorcism through the ages. In the sixteenth and seventeenth centuries, the era of the Reformation, thousands of Europeans were thought to be possessed by demons. In response to their horrifying symptoms—violent convulsions, displays of preternatural strength, vomiting of foreign objects, displaying contempt for sacred objects, and others—exorcists were summoned to expel the evil spirits from victims’ bodies. This compelling book focuses on possession and exorcism in the Reformation period, but also reaches back to the fifteenth century and forward to our own times. Entire convents of nuns in French, Italian, and Spanish towns, thirty boys in an Amsterdam orphanage, a small group of young girls in Salem, Massachusetts—these are among the instances of demon possession in the United States and throughout Europe that Brian Levack closely examines, taking into account the diverse interpretations of generations of theologians, biblical scholars, pastors, physicians, anthropologists, psychiatrists, and historians. Challenging the commonly held belief that possession signals physical or mental illness, the author argues that demoniacs and exorcists—consciously or not—are following their various religious cultures, and their performances can only be understood in those contexts. “Riveting [and] readable . . . must-reading for students of history, psychology and religion.” —Publishers Weekly “Levak, a distinguished historian of early modern witchcraft, now sets exorcism in a long historical perspective, providing the most comprehensive and scholarly overview of the theme yet published.” —Peter Marshall, Times Literary Supplement

**Demonic Foes: A Psychiatrist's Journey from Skepticism to Belief**

Dr. Richard Gallagher, a leading psychiatric authority on demonic possession, shares his extraordinary twenty-five-year career, detailing his transformation from a staunch skeptic to a firm believer. His journey began with a perplexing case presented by a pragmatic Catholic priest: a woman exhibiting behaviors that defied rational explanation.

Gallagher, a successful New York psychiatrist, was initially intrigued but doubtful when asked to assess a woman for a potential exorcism. Upon meeting her, he was astounded. The woman displayed abilities that seemed impossible. She could instantly discern individuals' hidden vulnerabilities and knew intimate details about the deaths of strangers, including the passing of Gallagher's own mother. Crucially, she spoke multiple languages, including Latin, but only when in a trance-like state. Gallagher concluded that this was not a manifestation of psychosis, but rather what he scientifically identified as paranormal ability. He determined the woman was not mentally ill, but demonically possessed.

This pivotal case marked the beginning of Gallagher's extensive involvement with demonic possession. Today, he is sought after by religious leaders across faiths—ministers, priests, rabbis, and imams. For a quarter-century, Gallagher has dedicated himself to studying historical accounts of demonic activity and exorcisms, accumulating more firsthand experience than any other psychiatrist globally.

In his compelling book, *Demonic Foes*, Gallagher recounts some of his most notable cases for the first time. These include:

*   A professional woman who believed her spiritualist mother had "assigned" her a spirit that subsequently turned against her.
*   A frail woman, weighing only 90 pounds, who violently hurled a 200-pound Lutheran deacon across a room, terrifying witnesses.
*   The case of "Julia," a self-proclaimed witch and "Satanic queen," whose possession Gallagher describes as the most harrowing he has ever encountered, a "once-in-a-century" event.

*Demonic Foes* offers a profound exploration of true demonic possession, moving beyond fictional portrayals to reveal the chilling realities of these extraordinary cases.

"The Seven Visitations of Sydney Burgess" is a chilling new horror novel that explores the terrifying aftermath of a home invasion. The story centers on Sydney, a woman who has worked hard to build a stable life for herself and her young son, escaping a troubled past. She has a good job, a comfortable home, and a supportive boyfriend.

However, her carefully constructed world shatters when a masked intruder breaks into her house. Sydney manages to escape, but the police present a different account of events. The intruder is found dead in her guest room, murdered in a way that suggests a deeply personal motive. Sydney has no memory of killing him, and no one believes her.

As Sydney grapples with fragmented memories and the unsettling events, a dark presence begins to influence her. This unnatural darkness whispers temptations, urging her back towards old addictions and the past she desperately tried to leave behind. The novel follows Sydney's desperate search for the truth amidst the ruins of her former life, as the encroaching darkness grows and reveals its own terrifying desires.

Critics have praised the book for its intense atmosphere and compelling narrative. M. R. Carey, author of "The Girl with All the Gifts," describes it as offering "horrors both existential and visceral," with a "sense of dread just builds and builds." T. Kingfisher, author of "The Hollow Places," calls it "odd and dark and fascinating," a "strange, compelling, late-night page-turner." Booklist recommends it for fans of Tana French, Megan Abbott, and Zoje Stage.

Name: Demon
Description:
Scott King's podcast investigates the 1995 cold case of a demon possession in a rural Yorkshire village, where a 12-year-old boy was murdered in cold blood by two children. Book six in the chilling, award-winning Six Stories series. _______________________ In 1995, the picture-perfect village of Ussalthwaite was the site of one of the most heinous crimes imaginable, in a case that shocked the world. Twelve-year-old Sidney Parsons was savagely murdered by two boys his own age. No reason was ever given for this terrible crime, and the 'Demonic Duo' who killed him were imprisoned until their release in 2002, when they were given new identities and lifetime anonymity. Elusive online journalist Scott King investigates the lead-up and aftermath of the killing, uncovering dark and fanciful stories of demonic possession, and encountering a village torn apart by this unspeakable act. And, as episodes of his Six Stories podcast begin to air, King himself becomes a target, with dreadful secrets from his own past dredged up and threats escalating to a terrifying level. It becomes clear that whatever drove those two boys to kill is still there, lurking, and the campaign of horror has just begun... _______________________

Name: The Last Days of Jack Sparks
Description:
"Ingenious and funny . . . Magnificent." -- Alan Moore, creator of Watchmen and V for Vendetta Jack Sparks died while writing this book. It was no secret that journalist Jack Sparks had been researching the occult for his new book. No stranger to controversy, he'd already triggered a furious Twitter storm by mocking an exorcism he witnessed. Then there was that video: forty seconds of chilling footage that Jack repeatedly claimed was not of his making, yet was posted from his own YouTube account. Nobody knew what happened to Jack in the days that followed -- until now. "Wow. Seriously hard to put down." -- M. R. Carey, author of The Girl With All the Gifts

## The Rite: A Journalist's Deep Dive into Exorcism

The film "The Rite," featuring Anthony Hopkins, is based on the true experiences of journalist Matt Baglio. Baglio's reporting uncovers the intensive training of an American priest as an exorcist. His work highlights that phenomena like possession, demons, the Devil, and exorcism are not just historical tales but active forces influencing lives today.

### A Priest's Unexpected Path to Exorcism

Father Gary Thomas, a parish priest from California, received a surprising summons from his bishop. He was called to Rome to train in the rite of exorcism. Though initially taken aback, he accepted and enrolled in a new exorcism course at a Vatican-affiliated university. This demanding program taught Father Gary how to differentiate true demonic possession from mental health issues. Guided by an experienced Italian exorcist, he participated in over eighty exorcisms. These encounters fundamentally changed his outlook, shifting him from a rational doubter to an active exorcist keenly aware of the ongoing spiritual conflict between good and evil.

### Exclusive Access to a Hidden World

Matt Baglio was granted extraordinary access to Father Gary during his training. This allowed him to gather insights that often challenged ordinary understanding. "The Rite" provides striking views into the lives of exorcists and individuals believed to be possessed. The book includes direct accounts of exorcisms, Father Gary's personal encounters with the Devil, and an examination of exorcism's history, including its ceremonies, rituals, and the Catholic Church's official stance on demonic possession.

### Exploring the Spiritual Realm

Baglio's research goes beyond exorcism, venturing into a complex spiritual domain. This exploration encompasses the ranks of angels and demons, the nature of satanic groups, dark ceremonies like black masses, the effects of curses, and various scientific and anthropological theories that attempt to explain these occurrences. Written with an investigative approach, "The Rite" engages both skeptics and believers, proving that the reality of demonic possession is more unusual and unsettling than any fictional account.

In the chilling supernatural horror thriller, "The Perfect Possession," demons escalate their ancient practice of possessing individuals to a terrifying new level: seizing an entire town. This gripping narrative plunges readers into the heart of a dire threat that even seasoned demonologist and paranormal investigator Rand Casey has never encountered.

Rand is far from his best. Still reeling from his last harrowing case, he finds himself physically and spiritually depleted. Adding to his torment, the formidable demon Shindael has sworn eternal vengeance upon Rand, a cruel retribution for his consistent victories against the forces of hell. Desperate for divine intervention, Rand seeks solace and protection at a new church in the seemingly tranquil town of Finnick, Louisiana.

The church is led by the captivating Pastor Deckard Arcan. Initially, Rand is deeply skeptical of the inexplicable miracles he witnesses emanating from Deckard. The entire populace of Finnick is enthralled, worshipping the charismatic preacher. This widespread devotion fuels Rand's suspicion, compelling him to uncover the truth behind Deckard Arcan. Is he a genuine messenger of God, or a cunning deceiver?

What Rand unearths is a deception so profound and malevolent that it can only be the work of supernatural, evil spirits. The devil's insidious tactics are masterfully disguised as divine miracles, threatening to engulf Finnick in darkness. It will demand every ounce of Rand's strength, sharp intellect, and unwavering resolve to liberate the town from this encroaching evil. This time, hundreds of lives and souls hang in the balance, depending entirely on Rand's ability to confront hell's ultimate deception. Can he muster the courage and cunning to save Finnick from the abyss? "The Perfect Possession" is a must-read for fans of hauntings and epic battles against the demonic, exploring the most profound forms of evil imaginable.

Name: Come Closer
Description:
Instead of a book she had ordered by mail, Amanda receives "Demon Possession, Past and Present." Soon after, something seems to take her over, and she wonders if she has been possessed by a female demon known to students of the Kabbalah as Naamah.

Name: The Encyclopedia of Fantastic Film
Description:
Thirty-five years in the making, and destined to be the last word in fanta-film references! This incredible 1,017-page resource provides vital credits on over 9,000 films (1896-1999) of horror, fantasy, mystery, science fiction, heavy melodrama, and film noir. Comprehensive cast lists include: directors, writers, cinematographers, and composers. Also includes plot synopses, critiques, re-title/translation information, running times, photographs, and several cross-referenced indexes (by artist, year, song, etc.). Paperback.

Name: The Devils of D-Day
Description:
Unsealing the hatch of a rusty old WWII tank will unleash a demonic nightmare in this novel by “the master of modern horror” (Library Journal). Thirty-five years have passed since the Allied invasion of Normandy on D-Day turned the tide of World War II against Adolf Hitler and the Nazi Reich, and it’s been more than three decades since the residents of the tiny French village of Le Vey witnessed the horrific slaughter of hundreds of German soldiers by thirteen black tanks. One of the tanks remains on the outskirts of town—its hatch mysteriously sealed, trapping its controller inside—only to be discovered by American surveyor and cartographer Dan McCook. Driven by curiosity and an inexplicable compulsion, McCook is about to do the unthinkable and release what lives within the tank upon an unsuspecting world. And once the monstrous occupant reunites with others of its demonic kind, a new world war will begin, one that threatens to wash the earth in blood and drag every man, woman, and child through the fiery gates of hell. A chilling and ingeniously original tale of demonic possession and apocalyptic possibilities, The Devils of D-Day is classic horror at its best, from the award-winning author of The Manitou.

## "The Walk Through Hell": A Family's Fight Against Spiritual Warfare

"The Walk Through Hell" chronicles a family's intense battle to reclaim a loved one from a powerful, unseen spiritual threat. This true story highlights the reality of spiritual warfare, illustrating how even strong Christian faith can be challenged by malevolent forces. The author shares her family's harrowing experience when her brother, a pastor, became the target of an evil entity bent on his destruction.

The book details the profound impact of this spiritual attack on the family. They undertook a courageous mission to rescue their brother from the grip of a demonic force aiming to consume him. This personal narrative offers a stark look at the devastating effects of such attacks and the enduring strength of family love when confronting overwhelming spiritual adversity.

Wendy Fuller-Holden, writing as Class F. Thomas, shares her debut work, "The Walk Through Hell." Raised in a devout Christian household in Louisiana, Fuller-Holden's faith was shaped by her Baptist minister father and devoted mother. As the youngest of five, she credits her upbringing and personal spiritual experiences for her strong convictions. A nurse and a singer/songwriter, Fuller-Holden hopes her deeply personal account will provide comfort and hope to others facing similar spiritual struggles.

Name: My Best Friend's Exorcism
Description:
Soon to be a major motion picture. This ENHANCED DIGITAL EDITION features TONS of TOTALLY AWESOME ’80s bonus materials—including Satanic Panic educational pamphlets, a do-it-yourself exorcism cheat sheet, a Spotify playlist of awesome ’80s tunes, animated cover artwork, and much more!
