As featured in SELF and on Weekend Today "Steve Zim's plan helped me drop my body fat more quickly than with any other program I've ever tried. I couldn't be more pleased."--Jessica Biel, star of The Illusionist In 6 Weeks to a Hollywood Body, the top Hollywood trainer and Weekend Today show fitness expert Steve Zim unlocks the secrets of the stars and shows you how to have a Hollywood body of your own. Want to add shape and strength to your legs? Have a great butt? Make your back and arms stronger and leaner? Develop an amazing chest or breasts? After you've completed the easy-to-follow Hollywood Body program in six short weeks, you'll be runway ready for your grand entrance and you'll look amazing and feel healthier and happier than ever before. In this book, Steve Zim reveals the three keys to his Hollywood Body system: HOLLYWOOD NUTRITION: The easy and effective Hollywood Body way to supercharge your metabolism with protein, good carbs, and good fats HOLLYWOOD HEART: Ramp up your cardio health and melt away fat and inches the Hollywood Body way HOLLYWOOD SCULPT: A revolutionary set of all-new Hollywood Body exercises designed to sculpt and reshape your muscles This proven program works no matter what your body type is. So get started--your Hollywood body awaits you!

Name: Transform Your Body Transform Your Life
Description:
This book gives you the blueprint for transforming your body and your life, by guiding you through the RNT Transformation Journey. This five-phase process will arm you with the tools to finally get into the shape of your life, for life, and experience incredible benefits that transcend the physical.

## Embark on Your 21-Day Primal Transformation

Mark Sisson, the visionary behind the Primal Blueprint, offers a practical and engaging guide to help you embrace a "Primal" lifestyle in just 21 days. This program is designed to be fun and easy to follow, empowering you to reprogram your genes for a long, healthy, and energetic life.

### The Core of the Primal Blueprint

The transformation begins with understanding eight Key Concepts. These are the most crucial daily elements of living the Primal way. Following these concepts, you'll dive into five Action Items. These actions are designed to fundamentally shift your body's programming.

### Your 21-Day Challenge: A Daily Journey

The Action Items are presented as a dynamic 21-Day Challenge. Each day offers specific guidance on:

*   **Diet:** Transitioning away from the Standard American Diet (SAD), which is carb-dependent and promotes fat storage.
*   **Exercise:** Moving beyond the chronic, overly-stressful exercise patterns often recommended by conventional wisdom.
*   **Lifestyle:** Incorporating other health-promoting elements that counter the stresses of modern life.

This challenge includes daily journal exercises to help you track your progress and reflect on your journey.

### Embracing Ancestral Wisdom for Modern Health

The goal is to move away from the restrictive and often unhealthy habits of modern living. Instead, you'll adopt the evolution-tested lifestyle behaviors of our hunter-gatherer ancestors. This approach promotes optimal gene expression and a more natural state of being.

### Becoming a "Fat Burning Beast"

By eating according to Primal principles, you'll transform from a "sugar-burner" into what Sisson calls a "fat burning beast." In this state:

*   Stored body fat becomes your primary fuel source.
*   Energy levels are optimized.
*   Hormone function improves.
*   Metabolic health is enhanced.
*   Immune function is boosted.

### Efficient and Enjoyable Fitness

The Primal Blueprint incorporates intuitive workouts that allow you to achieve exceptional fitness in just a few hours per week. The emphasis is on making exercise enjoyable, not a chore.

### A Comprehensive Resource for Success

The Primal Blueprint 21-Day Total Body Transformation is packed with valuable resources to support your journey:

*   **Visual Aids:** Photos and diagrams to illustrate concepts.
*   **Concise Summaries:** Easy-to-digest overviews of each section.
*   **Workout Details:** Clear descriptions and photos of exercises.
*   **Food Guidance:** Resource lists of Primal-approved foods and those to avoid.
*   **Culinary Inspiration:** Recipe suggestions to make Primal eating delicious.
*   **Motivation:** Detailed real-life success stories to keep you inspired and focused.

### A Flexible and Personalized Approach

Unlike rigid, quick-fix programs, the Primal Blueprint offers a simple, sensibly-paced journey. It's shaped by your personal preferences and emphasizes enjoying the comforts of modern life. The program is scalable, making it suitable for:

*   **Fitness Enthusiasts:** Those who might benefit from a more relaxed approach.
*   **Beginners:** Individuals needing motivation to start their health journey.

Mark Sisson's light-hearted and irreverent tone ensures you feel comfortable and inspired, never intimidated.

### Lasting Results Beyond 21 Days

While you can expect noticeable results within the first 21 days – including fat loss, improved energy, enhanced fitness, and better blood test markers – this transformation is just the beginning. It lays the foundation for a life of ease and contentment, achieved by living in harmony with your genetic requirements for health and longevity.

**Uncover the Chilling Secrets of "The Twisted Ones"**

Dive into the unsettling world of "The Twisted Ones," a novel that masterfully blends the eerie atmosphere of "The Blair Witch Project" with the folksy charm of "The Andy Griffith Show." This gripping tale, penned by Hugo Award-winning author Ursula Vernon under the pseudonym T. Kingfisher, centers on a young woman named Mouse.

When Mouse's father tasks her with clearing out her deceased grandmother's home in rural North Carolina, she readily agrees, expecting a straightforward, albeit perhaps dusty, chore. However, the reality proves far more daunting. Her grandmother's house is not just cluttered; it's a hoarder's paradise, overflowing with an overwhelming amount of forgotten possessions.

Amidst the chaos, Mouse unearths a hidden treasure: her step-grandfather's journal. Initially, its contents appear to be the ramblings of a disturbed mind, filled with what seem like nonsensical entries. But as Mouse delves deeper, the journal's cryptic words begin to take on a terrifying significance. Her own experiences in the secluded woods surrounding the property start to mirror the disturbing accounts within its pages.

Alone with her dog, Mouse finds herself confronting a series of inexplicable and horrifying events. The chilling descriptions in the journal are not mere fantasy; they are tangible threats lurking in the shadows. The novel powerfully illustrates that sometimes, the things that go bump in the night are not just figments of imagination but real entities with sinister intentions. Mouse must summon her courage and confront these terrifying beings head-on if she hopes to survive and escape the woods with her life. "The Twisted Ones" is a suspenseful and deeply frightening narrative that will undoubtedly keep readers on the edge of their seats, torn between fear and an insatiable curiosity to discover what lies ahead.

Name: Future Folk Horror
Description:
Future Folk Horror: Contemporary Anxieties and Possible Futures analyzes folk horror by looking at its recent popularity in novels and films such as The Ritual (2011), The Witch (2015), and Candyman (2021). Countering traditional views of the genre as depictions of the monstrous, rural, and pagan past trying to consume the present, the contributors to this collection posit folk horror as being able to uniquely capture the anxieties of the twenty-first century, caused by an ongoing pandemic and the divisive populist politics that have arisen around it. Further, this book shows how, through its increasing intersections with other genres such as science fiction, the weird, and eco-criticism as seen in films and texts like The Zero Theorum (2013), The Witcher (2007–2021), and Annihilation (2018) as well as through its engagement with topics around climate change, racism, and identity politics, folk horror can point to other ways of being in the world and visions of possible futures.

**Harvest Hymns, Volume II - Sweet Fruits: A Deep Dive into Contemporary Folk Horror Music**

This installment of *Harvest Hymns*, subtitled "Sweet Fruits," immerses readers in the vibrant and evolving world of modern folk horror music. Building upon the groundwork laid in Volume One, "Twisted Roots," this collection spotlights artists, composers, and albums that are either shaping our current understanding of the genre or exploring its thematic and sonic landscapes. Whether you're a seasoned enthusiast or new to the eerie melodies, this volume offers a compelling exploration of what defines folk horror today.

The content within "Sweet Fruits" is as rich and varied as the genre itself. Through insightful interviews, in-depth articles, and critical reviews, we dissect the contributions of influential figures and projects. Prepare to discover the sonic worlds of Adam Scovell, Moon Wiring Club, Drew Mullholland, Broadcast, The Devil & The Universe, Jim Jupp, Inkubus Sukkubus, and A Year in the Country, among others. Each offers a unique perspective on the enduring appeal and creative potential of folk horror.

Beyond the music, "Sweet Fruits" delves into the potent imagery that forms the bedrock of the folk horror aesthetic. The collection highlights recurring motifs that evoke a sense of ancient dread and pastoral unease. Expect to encounter the stark symbolism of rural icons like scarecrows and corn rigs, the unsettling charm of seemingly idyllic hamlets and village fetes, and the chilling presence of supernatural entities such as Black Eyed Dogs and the spectral figures associated with hanging trees. The evocative power of mist-laden fields, blackberry brambles, and weeping willows is also explored, alongside mythic allusions to ancient rituals and the primal forces embodied by figures like the Great God Pan. This exploration of imagery underscores the genre's deep connection to folklore and the natural world, often with a disquieting twist.

Name: The White People Illustrated
Description:
"""The White People"" is a horror short story by Welsh author Arthur Machen. Written in the late 1890s, it was first published in 1904 in Horlick's Magazine, edited by Machen's friend A. E. Waite, then reprinted in Machen's collection The House of Souls (1906).The story has since been described as an important example of horror fiction, influencing generations of later writers."

## We Don't Go Back: An Exploration of Folk Horror

Step into the unsettling world of folk horror with "We Don't Go Back," a comprehensive survey of this uniquely chilling genre. This book delves into the haunted landscapes of pagan village conspiracies, the grim pronouncements of witch finders, and the terrifying awakenings of evil in unsuspecting teenagers. It explores dark fairy tales, the insidious nature of backwoods cults, and the unsettling presence of obsolete technologies within the genre.

### Defining the Genre

"We Don't Go Back" begins by examining the foundational classics of screen folk horror, including *Night of the Demon*, *Witchfinder General*, *The Wicker Man*, and *Blood on Satan's Claw*. From these cornerstones, the book broadens its scope to encompass folk horror from across the globe.

### A Journey Through Folk Horror Cinema and Television

The journey takes readers from the pastoral dread of *Watership Down* to the visceral terror of *The Texas Chain Saw Massacre*, with countless unsettling stops in between. "We Don't Go Back" offers a thoughtful, funny, and essential overview of folk horror's manifestations in both television and cinema.

### Critical Acclaim

As Robin Ince describes it, "A beautiful rumination on the dark films and television that shaped me and a generation of odd children, for good or ill, worth a year of your time, because you won't just read the book, you'll feel a burning desire to watch everything mentioned within."

Kier-La Janisse, author of *House of Psychotic Women*, praises it as "A comprehensive, accessible and often riotously funny tome weaving together folk horror in all its forms, from British television to the American backwoods, from Eastern European fairytales to the vengeful ghosts of East Asia." Janisse further notes that Ingham "explores uncanny landscapes haunted by things buried, old cultures converging with the reluctance of contemporary reason, that very tension that gives his book its name. He attempts to both define folk horror and free it from definition, creating the ultimate guide to the genre's manifestations on film and offering a convincing argument as to why the genre resonates so compellingly with people today."

Name: Thomas Hardy and the Folk Horror Tradition
Description:
Thomas Hardy and the Folk Horror Tradition takes the uncanny and unsettling fiction of Thomas Hardy as fundamental in examining the lineage of 'Hardyan Folk Horror'. Hardy's novels and his short fiction often delve into a world of folklore and what was, for Hardy the recent past. Hardy's Wessex plays out tensions between the rational and irrational, the pagan and the Christian, the past and the 'enlightened' future. Examining these tensions in Hardy's life and his work provides a foundation for exploring the themes that develop in the latter half of the 20th century and again in the 21st century into a definable genre, folk horror. This study analyses the subduing function of heritage drama via analysis of adaptations of Hardy's work to this financially lucrative film market. This is a market in which the inclusion of the weird and the eerie does not fit with the construction of a past and its function in creating a nostalgia of a safe and idyllic picture of England's rural past. However, there are some lesser-known adaptations from the 1970s that sit alongside the unholy trinity of folk horror: the adaptation for television of the Wessex Tales. From a consideration of the epistemological fissure that characterize Hardy's world, the book draws parallels between then and now and the manifestation of writing on conceptual borders. Through this comparative analysis, Thomas Hardy and the Folk Horror Tradition posits that we currently exist on a moment of fracture, when tradition sits as a seductive threat.

Name: Folk Horror
Description:
While the undisputed heyday of folk horror was Britain in the 1960s and 1970s, the genre has not only a rich cinematic and literary prehistory, but directors and novelists around the world have also been reinventing folk horror for the contemporary moment. This study sets out to rethink the assumptions that have guided critical writing on the genre in the face of such expansions, with chapters exploring a range of subjects from the fiction of E. F. Benson to Scooby-Doo, video games, and community engagement with the Lancashire witches. In looking beyond Britain, the essays collected here extend folk horror’s geographic terrain to map new conceptualisations of the genre now seen emerging from Italy, Ukraine, Thailand, Mexico and the Appalachian region of the US.

Name: Folk Horror Revival: Harvest Hymns. Volume I- Twisted Roots
Description:
The Twisted Roots of Folk Horror music. An exploration of the artists and their music who laid the foundations for future generations of Folk Horror musicians. Taking in Murder Ballads, Acid Folk, Occult Rock, The Blues and Traditional Folk Music as well as Film Soundtracks Twisted Roots is a collection of articles, interviews and album reviews from the likes of Maddy Prior, Jonny Trunk, Sharron Kraus, John Cameron and Candia McKormack and many more.

### Unearthing the Chilling World of Folk Horror

Folk horror delves into ancient fears and the unsettling power of the past. *The Mammoth Book of Folk Horror* collects stories that capture this enduring subgenre's essence. It features classic authors like Arthur Machen, Algernon Blackwood, H. P. Lovecraft, and M. R. James, alongside modern voices such as Ramsey Campbell, Storm Constantine, Christopher Fowler, Alison Littlewood, and Kim Newman. This anthology offers a complete look at folk horror's chilling development.

The genre's strength lies in its exploration of anxieties tied to the land and its hidden histories. Folk horror often uses isolated landscapes, ancient traditions, and mysterious rituals to create unease. It suggests that the supernatural is deeply connected to specific places or old objects, a theme central to early horror writing.

The stories in this collection, enhanced by Michael Marshall Smith's atmospheric photography, revitalize folkloric elements. They show how portraying these forces as "natural" doesn't lessen the dread they evoke. From spirits bound to a location to creatures in ancient wells, *The Mammoth Book of Folk Horror* presents compelling examples of this captivating subgenre.

Name: The Routledge Companion to Folk Horror
Description:
The Routledge Companion to Folk Horror offers a comprehensive guide to this popular genre. It explores its origins, canonical texts and thinkers, the crucial underlying themes of nostalgia and hauntology, and identifies new trends in the field. Divided into five parts, the first focuses on the history of Folk Horror from medieval texts to the present day. It considers the first wave of contemporary Folk Horror through the films of the ‘unholy trinity’, as well as discussing the influence of ancient gods and early Folk Horror. Part 2 looks at the spaces, landscapes, and cultural relics, which form a central focus for Folk Horror. In Part 3, the contributors examine the rich history of the use of folklore in children’s fiction. The next part discusses recent examples of Folk Horror-infused music and image. Chapters consider the relationship between different genres of music to Folk Horror (such as folk music, black metal, and new wave), sound and performance, comic books, and the Dark Web. Often regarded as British in origin, the final part analyses texts which break this link, as the contributors reveal the larger realms of regional, national, international, and transnational Folk Horror. Featuring 40 contributions, this authoritative collection brings together leading voices in the field. It is an invaluable resource for students and scholars interested in this vibrant genre and its enduring influence on literature, film, music, and culture.

Name: Dead Secret
Description:
“Call[ing] to mind the forensic mysteries of Aaron Elkins and Patricia Cornwell” (Chicago Sun-Times), Beverly Connor’s spellbinding series proves that the dead do tell tales—and reveal secrets that can threaten the living in the most chilling of ways… In the depths of an unmapped cave, forensic anthropologist Diane Fallon makes an astonishing discovery: the decades-old skeleton of a caving victim. Soon, the remains of two more bodies are found—one in an old car submerged in the waters of an abandoned quarry, another buried in the Georgia woods. At first, with nothing to link the dissimilar victims except desiccated bones, Diane can’t fathom the connection. But someone in her shadow does. It’s the key to a mystery that reaches back seventy years in a heritage of love, greed, and murder—and an unearthed family secret that still holds the power to kill.

Name: American TV Detective Dramas
Description:
The way detectives access and attain the 'truth' about a crime is an important indicator of how they relate to contemporary political developments. This book explores these methods of detection and positions the genre in a specific political, aesthetic, narrative and industrial context.

Name: Dead Past
Description:
When she sees a television show featuring the unsolved murders of her entire family, Juliet Price, who can't remember anything about the event, is plagued by terrifying nightmares that hold the key to identifying the killer and turns to Diane Fallon for help. Original.

## The Sociological Landscape of Forensic Science

This comprehensive overview delves into the sociology of forensic science, examining its intricate connections with science, technology, law, and society. Drawing on extensive international research and real-world case studies, it scrutinizes how forensic knowledge is produced and integrated into the justice system.

### Core Themes Explored

The book navigates a spectrum of crucial topics, including:

*   **Science in Policing:** The integration of scientific methods into police work and criminal investigations.
*   **Law and Science Interplay:** The complex relationship between legal frameworks and scientific advancements.
*   **Ethical and Social Dimensions:** The ethical and societal implications arising from new forensic technologies, such as DNA analysis.
*   **Media Representation:** How forensic science is portrayed in popular media.
*   **Policy and Global Agendas:** An examination of forensic policy and the international agenda shaping its future.

### Advancements in Forensic Technology

This updated edition places a significant emphasis on recent technological innovations. Key additions and revisions include:

*   **Facial Recognition Technology:** Its application and impact on investigations.
*   **Digital Forensics:** The growing role of digital evidence in policing.
*   **Algorithmic Tools:** The use of algorithms like probabilistic genotyping in forensic analysis.
*   **Genealogical Searching:** The utilization of genetic databases for identification.
*   **Phenotyping:** Predicting physical characteristics from DNA.

### Critical Appraisal and International Perspectives

The new edition critically appraises current scholarship and incorporates fresh international case studies. This provides readers with a valuable comparative perspective on forensic science practices globally.

By engaging with sociological literature, the book argues how forensic science is socially constructed and, in turn, influences the administration of justice. It serves as an excellent introduction for students, illuminating the position of forensic science within the criminal justice system, as well as within professionalization and ethical frameworks. This is essential reading for students in criminal investigation, policing, forensic science, and the sociology of science and technology.

Name: DNA In Forensic Science
Description:
An up-to-date treatment of DNA in forensic science, which contains an introduction to the underlying science, and lays the foundation for a discussion of the technology and methods used. It also addresses current applications of DNA techniques.; Topics covered include structure, function and variation in DNA, experimental techniques, hypervariant a

Name: Scene of the Crime
Description:
Part of the Howdunit Series, Scene of the Crime is written by a professional in the field. It provides the inside details that writers need to weave a credible - and salable - story. essential buys for any serious author...Will cut research time in half! - Mystery Scene Magazine

Name: The Practice Of Crime Scene Investigation
Description:
The Practice Of Crime Scene Investigation covers numerous aspects of crime scenes investigation, including the latest in education and training, quality systems accreditation, quality assurance, and the application of specialist scientific disciplines to crime. Written by authors specifically chosen for their expertise in this specialized field, the book includes discussion on fingerprinting, dealing with trauma victims, photofit technology, the role of the pathologist and ballistic expert, clandestine laboratories, and explosives. This comprehensive book is a valuable reference for everyone interested in forensic science and the broader application of the justice system.

**Detective Fiction's Roots in Early Forensics**

This book uncovers the compelling link between the emergence of forensic science in the 19th century and the birth of detective fiction in Britain and America. Ronald R. Thomas reveals how the "criminal body" became central to understanding and achieving justice within various fictional narratives.

**The Detective's Toolkit**

Literary detectives derived their authority from innovative "devices" used to reveal truth and demonstrate their skill. These groundbreaking tools included:

*   **Fingerprinting:** The unique patterns of fingerprints became a key identifier.
*   **Photography:** Visual evidence captured through photography offered new ways to document crime scenes.
*   **Lie Detectors:** Early forms of lie detection aimed to uncover deception.

Thomas explores how these scientific methods shaped perceptions of cultural authority during crucial phases in detective fiction's evolution.

**A Blend of Disciplines**

This study merges literary criticism with historical and scientific insights. It contextualizes readings of iconic literary works by examining:

*   **Criminology's Progress:** Developments in the study of crime and criminals.
*   **Evidence Law:** The changing legal standards for admitting evidence.
*   **Scientific Identity:** Modern scientific approaches to understanding who someone is.

The book features a broad spectrum of fictional works, from early innovators like Poe, Dickens, and Hawthorne to later giants such as Twain, Conan Doyle, Hammett, Chandler, and Christie.

Name: Forensic Detective
Description:
Death. It’s not only inevitable and frightening, it’s intriguing and fascinating–especially today, when science continues to make ever more stunning advances in the investigation of the oldest and darkest of mysteries. To discover the how and why of death, unearth its roots, and expose the mechanics of its grim handiwork is, at least in some sense, to master it. And in the process, if a criminal can be caught or closure found, so much the better. Enter Robert Mann, forensic anthropologist, deputy scientific director of the U.S. government’s Central Identification Laboratory, and, some might say, the Sherlock Holmes of death detectives. When the dead reveal some of their most sensational, macabre, and poignant tales, more often than not it’s Mann who’s been listening. Now, in this remarkable casebook, he offers an in-depth behind-the-scenes portrait of his sometimes gruesome, frequently dangerous, and always compelling profession. In cases around the world, Mann has been called upon to unmask killers with nothing but the bones of their victims to guide him, draw out clues that restore identities to the nameless dead, recover remains thought to be hopelessly lost, and piece together the events that can unlock the truth behind the most baffling deaths. The infamous 9/11 terror attacks, which killed thousands; the unplanned killing that inaugurated serial murderer Jeffrey Dahmer’s grisly spree; mysterious military fatalities from World War II to the Cold War to Vietnam, including the amazing case of the Vietnam War’s Unknown Soldier–all the fascinating stories are here, along with photos from the author’s personal files. Mystery hangings, mass graves, errant body parts, actual skeletons in closets, and a host of homicides steeped in bizarre clues and buried secrets–they’re all in a day’s work for one dedicated detective whose job begins when a life ends.

Name: Forensic Textile Science
Description:
Forensic Textile Science provides an introduction to textile science, emphasizing the terminology of the discipline and offering detailed coverage of the ways textile damage analysis can be used in forensics. Part One introduces textiles and their role in forensics, including chapters on fibers, yarns and fabrics, garment types and construction, and household textiles. Part Two covers analysis of textile damage in a forensic context. Key topics include textile degradation and natural damage, weapon and impact damage, textile ripping, and ballistic damage. This book is an important reference point for all those interested in textile damage and the role of textiles in forensics, including academics, post-graduate students, and forensic scientists. - Offers various perspectives on forensic textile science from an international team of contributors - Provides wide-ranging coverage of textile damage analysis in the context of forensic investigations - Includes chapters on fibers, yarns and fabrics, garment types and construction, and household textiles

Name: The Little Book of Friendship
Description:
Friendships are like flowers. If you take care of them, they grow and bloom until you have a beautiful garden! The Little Book of Friendship shows young readers what they need to know to make a friend and to be one too.

Name: ABCs of Kindness
Description:
Learning the importance of kindness is as easy as A-B-C with this sweet board book that fosters social emotional development. A is for all of us be everyone's friend. B is for believing things will turn out well in the end. C is for being caring in everything you do. D is for dear ones who mean the world to you. Learn your ABCs while learning to be kind to everyone you meet with this irresistibly illustrated board book that's perfect for Valentine's Day and all year round! Learn to love and love to learn! Complete the series with 123s of Thankfulness and Happiness is a Rainbow!

**Navigating Worry and Fostering Empathy: A Guide for Young Readers**

This heartwarming picture book, "It Will Be OK," offers a gentle and accessible introduction to understanding and managing anxiety, while highlighting the profound impact of empathy and attentive listening. It's an ideal resource for young children experiencing worry, and for the adults who care for them. The story beautifully illustrates that sometimes, the most powerful act of love is simply being present for someone in need.

The narrative unfolds with Giraffe and Zebra, two friends who share a daily ritual of walking to the watering hole beneath their favorite tree. However, one day, Giraffe is conspicuously absent. Zebra's search leads him to discover Giraffe hidden high in the branches, paralyzed by fear after spotting a spider. Instead of dismissing Giraffe's feelings, Zebra chooses a path of patient understanding. He sits with Giraffe, offering quiet support for as long as it takes for his friend to feel safe again. This simple yet profound act of solidarity underscores the book's central message.

"It Will Be OK" is a valuable tool for various educational and therapeutic settings. Preschool classrooms and educators can utilize it to explore themes of feelings, social-emotional learning, mindfulness, and social skills. School counselors and child therapists will find it a beneficial bibliotherapy resource for children grappling with their emotions. Parents seeking to instill values of kindness, courage in facing fears, empathy, and the importance of listening will also find this book to be a cherished addition to their collection. Furthermore, anyone who appreciates a beautifully illustrated story celebrating the enduring strength of friendship will be captivated by its charm. The book thoughtfully includes educational backmatter, providing practical suggestions for children experiencing worry or anxiety, guidance on how to support a worried friend, and a clear definition of empathy.

Name: Together We Can
Description:
The perfect picture book celebrating friendship, compassion, diversity and togetherness in the world. Our friends are all different, but one thing is true, each one is quite precious and that includes YOU! Award-winning Caryl Hart and Ali Pye have created a beautifully fun-filled and vibrant picture book showing children forming friendships and displaying kindness and compassion. The perfect rhyming read-aloud with joyous illustrations, this book celebrates diversity and inclusivity throughout and highlights how important togetherness is in our world.

**Title:** Navigating Shifting Friendships in Middle School

**Description:**

Frances O’Roark Dowell, an Edgar Award-winning author, delves into the complexities of middle school friendships in her latest novel, a companion to the popular *The Secret Language of Girls*.

### The Seventh-Grade Divide

Seventh grade presents a significant challenge for Kate and Marylin. They recognize that their once-unbreakable bond as best friends is changing. The pressures of middle school are pulling them in different directions.

### Divergent Paths

Marylin embraces the typical middle school scene, focusing on cheerleading, popularity, and the latest hairstyles. In stark contrast, Kate finds her interests in combat boots, learning guitar, and writing her own music. Their personalities and priorities have clearly diverged.

### The Struggle for Connection

Despite their differences, Kate and Marylin still desire to maintain a connection. They actively seek common ground to preserve their friendship. However, bridging the gap between their evolving selves proves to be a more difficult task than either of them anticipated.

**Growing Young: The Surprising Science of a Longer, Happier Life**

Science journalist Marta Zaraska's journey into longevity began with a familiar parental concern: diet. The birth of her daughter sparked a deep dive into healthy eating, leading her to experiment with fasting, the keto diet, and a half-marathon. She stocked her pantry with superfoods like goji berries and chia seeds, and embraced organic options. However, her extensive research, which involved poring over scientific papers and interviewing experts in fields like molecular biochemistry, epidemiology, and neuroscience, led to a profound shift in her understanding of aging and how to live a longer, more fulfilling life.

Zaraska's findings revealed that while diet and exercise play a role, other factors have a more significant impact on mortality risk. She discovered that a robust network of family and friends can reduce mortality risk by approximately 45 percent, a figure considerably higher than the 23 percent reduction attributed to exercise. Furthermore, engaging in volunteer work offers a 22 percent reduction in mortality risk. In contrast, some popular health trends, such as the use of turmeric, have shown no demonstrable benefit.

These revelations led Zaraska to a compelling conclusion: a longer and happier life isn't solely about nutrition and physical activity. Cultivating deep friendships, practicing empathy, and finding purpose are equally, if not more, crucial. Her book, "Growing Young," chronicles her global exploration across eleven chapters. From tracking wild mice in England to participating in flower arranging with elders in Japan, and visiting laboratories and "hugging centers," Zaraska offers an engaging and insightful look at the habits that truly influence our lifespan. This deeply researched and expertly reported work promises to transform how readers approach the pursuit of a longer, happier existence.

Name: Books of Kindness
Description:
Learning the importance of kindness is as easy as A-B-C and 1-2-3 with this sweet collection of board books, which foster social emotional development. This bestselling board book series is available in a four-book boxed set--perfect for holiday gifts, birthdays, and baby showers. Learn to love and love to learn! Support your little one's social-emotional development with these soothing, adorable board books that focus on the most important aspects of life: kindness, gratitude, joy, and friendship. This boxed set includes: - ABCs of Kindness - 123s of Thankfulness - Happiness is a Rainbow - Friendship is Forever

Name: Real Friends vs. the Other Kind
Description:
“If you’re like us, you probably have questions about friendships. Maybe you’re not sure if someone is a real friend or the other kind.” So goes the story of Jack, Jen, Chris, Abby, Mateo, and Michelle—six students trying to figure it all out in middle school. Real Friends vs. the Other Kind, the second book in the Middle School Confidential series, follows these characters as they work to forge friendships while navigating tough social situations. The book offers insider information on making friends, resolving disputes, and dealing with other common middle school concerns—like gossip, exclusion, and cyberbullying. There’s also expert advice on crushes, peer pressure, and being there for friends who need help. Filled with character narratives, quizzes, quotes from real kids, tips, tools, and resources, this book is a timely and engaging survival guide for the middle school years.

Name: Amy's Amazing Hats
Description:
“I wrote “Amy’s Amazing Hats” as a way to teach children the values of being kind and caring to a friend with cancer, bringing awareness to pediatric cancer, sharing the challenges young people have fighting this disease, and the wonderful work the “Passing Hats” organization does. For more information, look up www.passinghats.org and you too may get “hooked” on looming hats! On behalf of children fighting cancer everywhere and Passing Hats, please share this special book with your friends and family.” – Sharyn Diamond

Name: My Best Friend, Dylan!
Description:
"My best friend, Dylan!" is the story of a girl whose best friend has a skin condition called "Psoriasis". She absolutely loves him and goes above and beyond to make him smile. It is a beautiful and amazing story about friendship, kindness and acceptance!

## Navigating Grief in the Workplace

This guide offers a compassionate approach to understanding how grief impacts the workplace. It explores the profound effects grief can have on employees, their work, and the overall office atmosphere.

### Understanding Different Types of Grief

Workers may experience various forms of grief, including:

*   Loss due to death
*   Divorce or relationship breakdown
*   Job loss or layoffs

### Supporting Colleagues Through Grief

The handbook provides practical advice on how to support coworkers who are grieving. This includes:

*   Participating in group memorials
*   Understanding and negotiating bereavement leave

### Resources for Mourners and Coworkers

This resource offers valuable insights for both individuals experiencing grief and their colleagues. It aims to foster a supportive and understanding work environment.

### A Message for Employers and Managers

A special section is dedicated to employers, owners, managers, and HR professionals. It addresses the economic implications of grief in the workplace and offers practical, cost-effective strategies for:

*   Maintaining employee morale
*   Creating a productive and compassionate work environment

Name: The Journey Through Grief
Description:
This spiritual companion for mourners affirms their need to mourn and invites them to journey through their very unique and personal grief. Detailed are the six needs that all mourners must yield to and eventually embrace if they are to go on to find continued meaning in life and living, including the need to remember the deceased loved one and the need for support from others. Short explanations of each mourning need are followed by brief, spiritual passages that, when read slowly and reflectively, help mourners work through their unique thoughts and feelings. Also included in this revised edition are journaling sections for mourners to write out their personal responses to each of the six needs. This replaces 1879651114.

Name: Healing After Loss
Description:
The classic guide for dealing with grief and loss. Daily reflections to find solace in our own lives, and comfort in the connection of sharing these meditations with countless others. After the focus on planning and outpouring of love from family and friends in the immediate aftermath following the loss of a loved one, we are left to enter a new version of our lives where someone important is missing. For days, months, years, the pain of the loss can crash in all at once. It is tempting to push that wave of grief back and soldier on with our new lives, but the loss will never lose its controlling power if we don’t find the courage and love to face it. Meditating on the loss, along with the rush of love that comes with it, gives us a chance to rejoice in the life that was shared, and to look forward in which memories of our loved ones continue to bless us. The short, poignant meditations given here follow the course of the year, but it is not a necessity to follow them chronologically. They will strengthen, inspire, and give comfort for as long as they are needed.

## Understanding the Grieving Brain: A Neuroscientist's Perspective

What happens inside our minds when we experience loss? Renowned grief expert and neuroscientist Mary-Frances O’Connor, PhD, offers groundbreaking insights into this fundamental human experience in her book, *The Grieving Brain*. This compelling work, recognized as an NPR SciFri Book Club Pick and a "Top 21 Psychology Books of 2022" by Next Big Idea Club, delves into the intricate workings of the brain during grief, presenting a new framework for understanding love, loss, and the process of learning to live without someone.

### The Neuroscience of Attachment and Loss

O'Connor, a psychologist with decades of research dedicated to grief's impact on the brain, makes complex neuroscience accessible and engaging. She guides readers through how we form attachments and how our brains process the profound absence left by loss.

*   **Forming Bonds:** With love, our neurons facilitate the creation of deep attachments to others.
*   **Processing Absence:** Following loss, the brain grapples with the reality of a loved one's permanent absence and the challenge of envisioning a future without them.

### Key Questions Explored in *The Grieving Brain*

The book tackles many of the perplexing aspects of grief, offering scientific explanations for common experiences:

*   **The Difficulty of Acceptance:** Why is it so challenging to fully comprehend that a loved one is gone forever?
*   **The Spectrum of Emotions:** What causes the wide range of feelings associated with grief, including sadness, anger, blame, guilt, and intense yearning?
*   **The Duration of Grief:** Why does the grieving process often take so long?
*   **Distinguishing Grief Types:** What is the difference between typical grief and prolonged grief disorder?
*   **The Nature of Rumination:** Why do we find ourselves ruminating so frequently after losing someone dear?
*   **Rebuilding a Meaningful Life:** How do we begin to restore a sense of meaning and purpose after experiencing loss?

### A New Paradigm for Navigating Loss

Drawing upon O’Connor’s pioneering neuroimaging research, extensive studies in the field, and poignant personal anecdotes, *The Grieving Brain* seamlessly blends storytelling, accessible science, and practical wisdom. This unique approach offers readers a deeper understanding of the neurological processes involved in grief and provides valuable tools for navigating loss with greater ease and grace.

Name: Healing Grief
Description:
James Van Praagh's first two books, both New York Times bestsellers, have been a powerful healing force for millions of readers. Using his talents as a medium, Van Praagh has not only helped the bereaved reach their lost loved ones and find peace but he has also illuminated the mysteries of death, the afterlife, and rebirth. His new book, Healing Grief, will once again draw from his compelling and uplifting readings, but with a new and special purpose- to show what the spirit world can teach us about the grieving process itself. While grief is clearly a natural response to death, it should also properly accompany life's other difficult passages, including times of transition, the loss of a relationship, or even the loss of a pet. Healing Grief begins with chapters that each examine a specific kind of loss - death of a parent, a spouse, or a child, the end of a marriage, or the onset of a troubling life change, such as unemployment or grave illness - and considers the particular bereavement issues it may engender. The book also offers advice on explaining death to children, on distinguishing healthy from destructive grief, and on harnessing the powers of healing through special exercises, meditation and affirmations. Healing Grief should be, in Van Praagh's words, "a manual for grieving well," offering an inspiring new perspective on grief from a world-renowned medium who has become an expert at helping people cope with unresolvable sorrow.

"Healing Grief, Finding Peace" by Louis LaGrand offers a compassionate guide for navigating the profound pain of loss. This resource provides daily strategies to help you find solace and inner peace.

LaGrand, a grief counseling expert, delves into the complex nature of grief, offering heartfelt guidance for acknowledging and honoring your emotions. Drawing on research and personal experience, the book addresses the distinct challenges of bereavement with wisdom and empathy, focusing on nurturing your emotional well-being.

Within its pages, you'll discover practical techniques designed to help you cope with grief day by day. The book also emphasizes building resilience, exploring avenues for growing stronger and discovering inner peace amidst loss. It encourages a perspective shift, allowing for personal growth to emerge from difficult experiences. "Healing Grief, Finding Peace" serves as a supportive companion, providing encouragement and advice to help you embrace your emotions and find healing.

Name: Monkey Mind
Description:
Shares the author's personal experiences with anxiety, describing its painful coherence and absurdities while sharing the stories of other sufferers to illustrate anxiety's intellectual history and influence.

Name: Understanding Your Suicide Grief
Description:
For anyone who has experienced the suicide of a loved one, coworker, neighbor, or acquaintance and is seeking information about coping with such a profound loss, this compassionate guide explores the unique responses inherent to their grief. Using the metaphor of the wilderness, the book introduces 10 touchstones to assist the survivor in this naturally complicated and particularly painful journey. The touchstones include opening to the presence of loss, embracing the uniqueness of grief, understanding the six needs of mourning, reaching out for help, and seeking reconciliation over resolution. Learning to identify and rely on each of these touchstones will bring about hope and healing.

Name: Healing
Description:
“When I ask people who are grieving what comforts them, nearly every single person gives the same answer. ‘Someone to listen.’ They don’t want someone to ‘fix’ it. They’re not asking for answers, spiritual wisdom, or uplifting cliches. Nope, they just need you to be there.” In Healing: The Essential Guide to Helping Others Overcome Grief and Loss, Alicia King combines her own wisdom and that of others who have “been there” to offer good advice for those who feel helpless when it comes to helping the bereaved. Included here are • the best ways to get involved on behalf of the grief-stricken • how to care for young children in grief • interviews with and advice from those who have suffered a loss • 20 ways to pay tribute to the beloved • 10 things never to say

Name: Healing Your Grieving Body
Description:
Acknowledging the unique set of symptoms that accompanies a period of mourning, this guide is the ideal companion to weathering the storm of physical distress. From muscle aches and pains to problems with eating and sleeping, this handbook addresses how the body responds to the impact of profound loss. Low energy, headaches, and other conditions are also taken into account. With 100 ways to help soothe the body and calm the mind, this compassionate study is an excellent resource in understanding the connection between the two.

Name: The Mystery of the Haunted House
Description:
Beware the Haunted House on Sycamore Street! Noah just wants some peace and quiet to finish his homework. But his little brother Josh can't stop talking about the Haunted House. When they go to check it out, the brothers find out they are not the only kids on the case. Olivia is new in town. But she already has a high tech plan to spy on the Haunted House. The three kids join forces to find out the truth. But when Josh goes missing, Noah and Olivia have to decide which is worse- the risk of the Haunted House, or the risk of losing Josh! If your child reads Magic Treehouse, Eerie Elementary or Dragon Masters, then they'll love this brand new chapter book series from Willow Night!

As the autumn air grows crisp and the days shorten, a sense of unease settles over Tinker's Cove, Maine. This charming coastal town, usually a haven of cozy autumn traditions, finds itself at the center of a perplexing mystery. Part-time reporter Lucy Stone, known for her knack for uncovering local stories, is drawn into a situation far more sinister than the usual Halloween spookiness.

The focus of the town's attention is a dilapidated house, a local landmark that has long been the subject of ghost stories and whispered rumors. Despite its eerie reputation, a recent inspection has declared the house structurally sound, much to the surprise of the residents. Even more unexpectedly, a new couple, Ty and Heather Moon, are set to purchase the property and make it their home.

However, the Moons' arrival is anything but ordinary. They seem determined to remain isolated, showing little interest in integrating with the welcoming community of Tinker's Cove. Their peculiar behavior is further amplified by strange occurrences emanating from their new residence – unexplained noises and flickering lights that disrupt the quiet nights. The mystery deepens when a local boy disappears near the Moon's property, casting a shadow of fear over the town. It falls upon Lucy Stone to investigate the eccentric couple and their increasingly alarming actions, hoping to uncover the truth behind the haunted house and the unsettling events unfolding within its walls.

Winter 1917. As the First World War rages on, a chilling darkness has settled over England. At Blackwater Abbey, an isolated estate off the Devon coast, Lord Highmount, a prominent armaments manufacturer, hosts a spiritualist gathering. His hope is to connect with his two sons, both lost to the brutal realities of the Western Front.

However, this gathering is not solely for mourning. Two individuals from the intelligence service are secretly present. Kate Cartwright, a friend of the family who grieves the loss of her brother at the Somme, possesses a unique gift for the spiritual realm. Accompanying her is the enigmatic Captain Donovan, a recent returnee from the European battlefields.

The reason for their clandestine attendance is grave: top-secret weapon plans developed by Lord Highmount's company have surfaced in Berlin. Intelligence suspects enemy spies will be among the guests, seeking to exploit the situation. As the attendees arrive, it becomes apparent that each harbors secrets they wish to conceal.

Then, a fierce storm descends, trapping everyone on the island. The isolation amplifies the tension, and soon, one of the guests is found dead. Blackwater Abbey, it seems, is haunted by more than just the specters of war.

This gripping mystery, a finalist for the Irish Book Award for Crime Fiction Book of the Year, offers a classic cozy big-house atmosphere haunted by the specters of World War One. For readers who enjoy the intricate plots of Agatha Christie and the atmospheric suspense of Simone St. James, "A House of Ghosts" delivers an unrelenting narrative packed with twists, turns, and a hint of romance. It's the perfect cold-weather read.

Name: Curse of the Haunted House
Description:
At first, Luke thought it would be kind of cool to spend the summer in the old mansion his dad had inherited. But when he got there, a strange boy appeared, seemingly from nowhere, and warned him about a curse that trapped people's spirits inside the mansion forever. Luke began to worry. Was his family in danger? Would the house trap them, too?

Name: Haunted Houses
Description:
Footsteps echo down empty hallways. Ghostly shadows float across rooms. Are ghosts real? Do they haunt houses and other places? Find out about haunted houses and how science is trying to solve this mystery.

Name: The Haunting of Bechdel Mansion
Description:
A paranormal phenomenon, a mysterious curse, and an unsolved murder forty years in the making.A young couple move into their dream home only to find a dark presence lurking from within. For Curtis and Mary, the small town of Redwood, Indiana seems too good to be true. Everything is perfect, including the Victorian mansion they purchased at a great price. But they soon experience terrifying supernatural encounters tied to the deadly secrets of an unsolved mass murder. Can they solve the mystery in time? Or will they face the same doomed fate as the tenants who came before them?

Name: Secret Picture Search: Haunted House
Description:
A walk in the country with their canine companion, Buster, turns into a scary adventure for teenage twins, Joe and Joanna. Buster has run off and is heading for the old derelict mansion. The twins follow--but do they know what creepy encounters await them? Can you spot the ghosts and ghouls in the shadows? Will Joe and Joanna rescue their pet pal? Put on your Secret Glasses and all is revealed.

Name: The Haunting of Bell Mansion
Description:
Bell, Maine is a small town with rural roots. While its citizens have fallen on hard times, they've managed to survive decades of harsh, northern winters. But when drifter Sarah Pembrooke rolls into town looking for work, the frigid cold will be the least of their worries.

Name: Firegirl
Description:
This poignant novel about a boy's friendship with a burn victim is perfect for fans of R. J. Palacio's Wonder From the moment Jessica arrives, life is never quite the same for Tom and his seventh-grade classmates. They learn that Jessica has been in a fire and was badly burned, and will be attending St. Catherine's will receiving medical treatments. Despite her appearance and the fear she evokes in him and most of the class, Tom slowly develops a tentative friendship with Jessica that changes his life. This quietly powerful novel demonstrates that a small gesture can make a huge impact in someone's life.

Name: The Friendship Book
Description:
In this little book, Marcia Malzahn shares her love for friends and celebrates the irreplaceable value of friendships. This book is meant to be a gift between friends and it features 50 gorgeous paintings by artist Isa Tyler and 50 poems written by Marcia. This book will inspire you to appreciate all your friendships as gifts from God and to nurture and cherish each one of them.

Name: The Ledge
Description:
NEW YORK TIMES BESTSELLER • “The authors bring extreme climbing to life. . . . Perhaps no author can rationalize why some choose to risk their lives . . . for the thrill of conquering a mountain. The Ledge comes perilously close and tells a ripping true story at the same time.”—The Denver Post In June 1992, best friends Jim Davidson and Mike Price stood atop Washington’s Mount Rainier, celebrating what they hoped would be the first of many milestones in their lives as passionate mountaineers. Then their triumph turned tragic when a cave-in plunged them deep inside a glacial crevasse—the pitch-black, ice-walled hell of every climber’s nightmares. An avid adventurer since youth, Davidson was a seasoned climber at the time of the Rainier ascent. But the harrowing free fall left him challenged by nature’s grandeur at its most unforgiving. Trapped on a narrow frozen shelf, deep below daylight, he desperately battled crumbling ice, snow that threatened to bury him alive, and crippling fear of the inescapable chasm below—all the while struggling to save his fatally injured friend. Finally, alone, with little equipment and rapidly dwindling hope, he confronted a fateful choice: the certainty of a slow, lonely death or the near impossibility of an agonizing climb for life. A story of heart-stopping adventure, heartfelt friendship, fleeting mortality, and implacable nature, The Ledge chronicles the elation and grief, dizzying heights and punishing depths, of a journey to hard-won wisdom. “Plunges readers into a dark, icy chasm from which escape seems impossible. Then it reveals the strength it takes to look up, and to start climbing.”—Jim Sheeler, winner of the Pulitzer Prize and author of the National Book Award finalist Final Salute “How [Davidson] rescued himself is the core of The Ledge, and its most gripping part. The physical effort and will involved are astonishing.”—The Plain Dealer “A moving portrait of friendship and loss.”—The Wall Street Journal

## Cultivating Enduring Bonds: The Essence of "Big Friendship"

Deep friendships are fundamental to a rich and supportive life, yet the consistent effort needed to sustain these meaningful connections is frequently overlooked. Aminatou Sow and Ann Friedman, the creative minds behind the podcast *Call Your Girlfriend*, delve into their own "Big Friendship" within their recent book. They meticulously document its decade-long evolution, candidly addressing both the hurdles and the profound satisfactions it has brought.

### Defining a "Big Friendship"

Sow and Friedman characterize a Big Friendship as a significant, deeply felt connection capable of adapting to various life circumstances. This includes navigating:

*   **Life Stage Transitions:** Friendships that mature and shift as individuals move through different phases of life.
*   **Geographical Separation:** Bonds that remain resilient even when individuals are physically apart.
*   **Personal Growth:** Connections that stay strong as individuals evolve and change over time.

Their personal journey exemplifies this, having navigated intense joy, considerable frustration, profound intimacy, and periods of painful distance.

### Overcoming Friendship Hurdles

The authors openly discuss confronting substantial challenges, such as serious health issues, career setbacks, and memorable, though difficult, shared experiences. They highlight that such obstacles are not unusual and that the bedrock of a Big Friendship is the ongoing dedication to fostering the relationship.

The book *Big Friendship* provides a valuable perspective on a type of relationship that is often taken for granted. It prompts readers to consider their own friendships: their origins, the trials they face, and the methods used to maintain them. Ultimately, it serves as an inspiration to value friendships, to intentionally invest in them, and to actively strive for their preservation.

Does your child get caught up in constant thinking, missing out on life's fun? "A Friendship Story: Heart & Mind" introduces your child to the dynamic duo of Heart and Mind, best friends who help each other find balance. Heart fills each moment with joy and love, while Mind guides actions.

Discover what happens when these two best friends spend a day apart. This book beautifully illustrates how a child's heart and mind can collaborate. When the heart's joyful sparks meet the mind's practical logic, a truly magical and fulfilling life unfolds.

"A Friendship Story: Heart & Mind" encourages children to tune into their hearts and take positive steps forward. Through its charming illustrations and engaging narrative, this book teaches that anyone, young or old, can embrace the present, letting go of worries and anxious thoughts. The core message is the powerful combination of "heartfulness" and "mindfulness" for a joyful, present life.

At the back of the book, you'll find thoughtful questions and fun activities designed to help children cultivate a strong friendship between their heart and mind, leading to a more joyful existence. It's perfect for reading aloud or for independent readers aged 4 to 8, and is also a wonderful resource for children with special needs.

Author Nishi crafted this story to emphasize the importance of listening to one's heart and taking positive action for a happier life. For more adventures with Heart and Mind, explore "Heart & Mind: Meet A New Friend." This book aims to introduce the concept of awareness, showing children how to use it daily. As awareness grows, so does our understanding of emotions, helping us move beyond hurtful behaviors. Children will learn that their awareness can reveal there's nothing to fear and nothing wrong with experiencing feelings.

## Embracing Sacrifice: Finding Room for Love and Joy

Minister Walker's words about Jesus' profound sacrifice resonated deeply with Tamara, filling her with awe. Contemplating the immense act of giving His life for individuals, for people just like her, left her in a state of wonder. This reflection naturally led to a deeper consideration of love, as the minister posed a challenging question to the congregation: "Could you love like that? Could you sacrifice anything that you truly cherished?"

### Confronting Personal Selfishness

The minister's sermon struck a profound chord with Tamara, compelling her to examine her own inner feelings. She couldn't ignore the recurring resentment she felt regarding Sienna's presence in her life. While she didn't harbor any deep affection for the girl, the shame of her own selfishness became undeniably palpable in that moment.

### The Transformative Power of Love and Joy

Reverend Walker continued his earnest preaching, his voice resonating with unwavering conviction. "Church," he declared with powerful emphasis, "your sadness cannot keep you crying when you are walking in love, and your problems cannot make you lose your joy when you are walking in love." These pronouncements served as a potent reminder of the truly transformative nature of love.

### A Profound Spiritual Awakening

As Tamara closed her eyes, her Bible clutched tightly in her hands, she felt her emotions stir once more within the sacred sanctuary. The timeless lyrics of an old Negro spiritual began to touch her heart, bringing a familiar and comforting warmth. Tears welled in her eyes, not tears of sorrow, but tears born from a profound spiritual awakening. Overwhelmed, Tamara embraced herself, gently rocking back and forth, allowing the music to envelop her completely, filling the very space within her soul.

Name: In My Heart
Description:
New York Times Bestseller! “A must-have for any storytime.” ―Kirkus Reviews “Childlike drawings evoke each feeling with a playful style.” ―Publishers Weekly “Evocative imagery, popping with bright colors.” —School Library Journal Happiness, sadness, bravery, anger, shyness . . . celebrate feelings in all their shapes and sizes in this full-color picture book from the Growing Hearts series! Our hearts can feel so many feelings! Some make us feel as light as a balloon, others as heavy as an elephant. In My Heart explores a full range of emotions, describing how they feel physically, inside, with language that is lyrical but also direct to empower readers to practice articulating and identifying their own emotions. With whimsical illustrations and an irresistible die-cut heart that extends through each spread, this gorgeously packaged and unique feelings book is sure to become a storytime and bedtime favorite. It will not only help your little boy or girl understand his or her feelings, but you may also hear, “Read it again! Read it again!” when you think it’s time for bed. The Growing Hearts series celebrates the milestones of a toddler’s emotional development, from conquering fears and expressing feelings to welcoming a new sibling. Read them all! Hello in There: A Big Sister’s Book of Waiting In My Heart: A Book of Feelings Brave As Can Be: A Book of Courage All My Treasures: A Book of Joy In My Room: A Book of Creativity and Imagination With My Daddy: A Book of Love and Family My Little Gifts: A Book of Sharing My Tree and Me: A Book of Seasons

Name: A Friend for Henry
Description:
In Classroom Six, second left down the hall, Henry has been on the lookout for a friend. A friend who shares. A friend who listens. Maybe even a friend who likes things to stay the same and all in order, as Henry does. But on a day full of too close and too loud, when nothing seems to go right, will Henry ever find a friend—or will a friend find him? With insight and warmth, this heartfelt story from the perspective of a boy on the autism spectrum celebrates the everyday magic of friendship.

Name: Molly and Mae
Description:
When Molly and Mae meet at the train station, two journeys begin: a trip through the countryside and an expedition through the highs and lows of friendship. At first the way is scenic and smooth—and then something goes off track. Can Molly and Mae build a bridge of kindness back to each other? Capturing the playfulness, laughter, disagreements, and reconciliations familiar to all relationships, Molly and Mae is a loving portrayal of friendship in its sweetest form.

Name: Penguin and Pinecone
Description:
Geisel Honor-winning author/illustrator Salina Yoon's beloved character Penguin has been giving love -- and growing -- for ten years! This special new edition is the perfect gift for Penguin fans new and old! When you give love... it grows. When curious little Penguin finds a lost pinecone in the snow, their friendship grows into something extraordinary! But Grandpa reminds Penguin that pinecones can't live in the snow--they belong in the warm forest far away. Can Penguin help Pinecone get home? And can they stay friends, even if they're miles apart? Prolific author/illustrator Salina Yoon's spare text and bright, energetic illustrations bring to life this endearing story celebrating friendships lost and found, and overcoming the odds to be with the one you love. This sweetly quirky friendship story will warm your heart! Don't miss these other books from Salina Yoon! The Penguin series Penguin and Penelope Penguin on Vacation Penguin in Love Penguin and Pumpkin Penguin's Big Adventure Penguin's Christmas Wish The Bear series Found Stormy Night Bear's Big Day The Duck, Duck, Porcupine series Duck, Duck, Porcupine My Kite is Stuck! And Other Stories That's My Book! And Other Stories Be a Friend

Name: The Veiled Heart
Description:
A CHANCE ENCOUNTER, A FORBIDDEN TASTE OF PASSION... IT SHOULD HAVE ENDED THERE... When Lady Miriam Rothbury has a fleeting brush with a handsome stranger at the scandalous Velvet Basement, it creates an invitation to sin. But what happens when this dangerous game promises to extend into a passion that threatens the foundation of her world?

Name: Second Chance Ranch
Description:
After his deployment, injured and discharged Major Squire Ackerman returns to Three Rivers Ranch, anxious to prove himself capable of running the cattle operation os his parents can retire. Things would be easier if the ranch wasn't missing $1.6 million dollars, which forces Squire to hire Kelly, the girl who rejected his high school prom invitation, as his accountant. She's back in town with her four-year-old son, living in her parent's basement until she can get her life back together. With fresh ink on her divorce papers and open gashes on her heart, she's not ready for much beyond her new job on the ranch. With old wounds opening and a ranch on the brink of financial collapse, it will take patience and faith to make their second chance possible.--Back cover.

## Anchored Hearts: A Tropical Tale of Second Chances

"A big-hearted, beautiful book about first love, second chances, and finding one’s place in the world. An exceptional getaway of a book!" —Emily Henry, New York Times bestselling author of Beach Read

Sparks are set to fly in the Florida Keys with the mass market release of *Anchored Hearts*, the latest from USA Today bestselling author Priscilla Oliveras. This acclaimed novel, the latest in the Keys to Love Series, offers an effervescent blend of humor, romance, and vibrant Latinx culture, all set against the unique island energy of Key West, Florida. It's the perfect summer read for those seeking an escape.

### The Return of the Prodigal Son

Award-winning photographer Alejandro Miranda finds himself back in Key West after years away, a return prompted by an injury that necessitates months of recuperation. His homecoming is fraught with the drama of a prodigal son facing his familia, but the island paradise also forces him to confront the girl he left behind – the one who was meant to be by his side.

### Rekindling a Lost Flame

Anamaría Navarro was devastated when Alejandro departed, leaving behind their shared dreams of traveling the world. Her own plans were derailed by her father's heart attack, making it impossible for her to leave. Now a dedicated firefighter and paramedic, with a personal training side hustle, Anamaría is surprised to find that Alejandro's mere presence reignites the feelings she's worked so hard to suppress.

### Navigating Old Hurts and New Futures

As family meddling subtly pushes Alejandro and Anamaría together, the heat of their rekindled attraction intensifies. Can these two Key West natives overcome past hurts and embrace a new future under the tropical sun? *Anchored Hearts* explores whether they can learn to trust each other again before their chance at happiness fades with the Key West sunset.

## A Dog's Hope: Exploring the Unbreakable Bond and Unconditional Love

"A Dog's Hope" delves into the profound and often life-altering connection between a boy and his dog. This novel offers a deeply moving, at times heartbreaking, yet ultimately inspiring narrative that resonates with readers who cherish stories of animal companionship. If you enjoyed titles like "A Dog’s Purpose," "The Art of Racing in the Rain," or "Marley and Me," you will find yourself captivated by this tale.

### Finding Light in a Dark World

The story unfolds in the serene farming community of Riverside, Washington, focusing on young Toby Fuller. Burdened by his father's constant disapproval and a growing sense of inadequacy, Toby's world is irrevocably fractured when his father suddenly leaves him and his mother. Just as despair begins to set in, a scruffy golden retriever enters Toby's life. Toby names him Buddy.

Buddy, despite facing his own physical challenges, becomes Toby's steadfast companion. His unwavering gaze suggests Toby is his entire universe. From that moment on, Buddy is Toby's shadow, offering a level of loyalty Toby had never before known.

### Navigating Loss and Discovering Strength

The narrative takes a sharp turn when a devastating event shatters Toby's life, leaving him to confront unimaginable hardship. The central question becomes whether Buddy can provide the unwavering support Toby needs to endure. "A Dog's Hope" powerfully illustrates how unconditional love can emerge during our darkest hours, serving as a vital beacon of hope.

### Reader Reactions: A Tapestry of Emotion

The profound emotional impact of "A Dog's Hope" is consistently reflected in reader feedback:

#### A Deeply Moving Experience

Many readers report being moved to tears, describing the book as "heart-warming, sweet, and beautiful." The novel's emotional depth has left some readers feeling "completely demolished" in the best possible way.

#### An Unforgettable Connection

From the very first page, readers find themselves drawn into the story, with many noting how it "grabbed" them and "stole their hearts." The novel is widely praised for its authentic portrayal of the deep bond between humans and their canine companions.

#### A Compelling, Page-Turning Read

Described as a "gut-wrenching" and "heart-wrenching" tale, the novel is difficult to put down. Its compelling narrative keeps readers invested from beginning to end.

#### Lessons in Love, Loss, and Resilience

The story is celebrated for its exploration of universal themes such as heartbreak, love, loss, forgiveness, and the triumph over adversity. It beautifully captures the peace and profound understanding that comes from sharing life with a beloved dog.

#### Essential Reading for Dog Lovers

Enthusiastic reviews consistently recommend "A Dog's Hope" to anyone who loves dogs, promising a deeper understanding of why these animals hold such a special place in our lives.

#### A Journey of Mutual Empathy

The novel offers a unique perspective, allowing readers to glimpse the world through a dog's eyes. This highlights the reciprocal learning and emotional growth that characterizes human-animal relationships.

#### Beautifully Crafted Prose

The writing itself is frequently lauded as "beautifully written" and "enchanting." This skillful storytelling creates a memorable and impactful reading experience that resonates long after the final page is turned.

#### An Emotional Rollercoaster Ride

Readers often find themselves needing tissues, pausing to process intense emotions, and experiencing the book as a true "experience" rather than just a passive read.

#### A Cherished and Enduring Story

"A Dog's Hope" is consistently described as "incredibly special." Many anticipate rereading it and consider it a future favorite, a testament to its lasting impact.

"A Dog's Hope" transcends a simple narrative; it is an exploration of the remarkable capacity for love and resilience that can be found in the most unexpected of places. Will Toby find the inner strength he so desperately needs? Immerse yourself in this breathtaking tale to discover the enduring power of a dog's unwavering love.

Name: Second Chance Summer
Description:
Taylor Edwards family might not be that close - everyone is a little too busy and overscheduled, but for the most part, they get along just fine. Then Taylor's dad gets some devastating news, and her parents decide that the family will spend on last summer together at their old lake house in the Pcocono Mountains. Crammed into a place much smaller than they are used to, they begin to get to know each other again, but as the summer progresses they're more aware than ever that they're battling a ticking clock. And as Taylor tries to deal with the drama at home, she is faced with the fact that the friends she thought she'd left behind haven't actually gone anywhere. Her former summer best friend is still living across the lake and still as mad with Taylor as she was five years ago, and her first boyfriend has moved in next door… but he's much cuter at seventeen than he was at twelve. Can one summer be enough time to get a second chance - with family, friends, and love?

Name: The City of Second Chances
Description:
'Full of heart and hope' Heidi Swain. Has she already met The One? What if Mr Right had come along at the wrong time...? Evie Grant is forty-five years old, a widow, and single mum of two children about to leave the nest. Suddenly alone in the family home, Evie realizes she hates her job, hardly goes out and hasn't had a date since who knows when... So it feels like fate when the opportunity arises for a girls trip to New York City. Staying with her sister on the Upper East Side, Evie is enchanted by a snow-covered city consumed by preparing for Christmas. Bobble hat firmly on, Evie is walking through the city one day when she bumps into Daniel Roberts, Hollywood heartthrob and one-time boyfriend of hers. It's now or never for Evie – but she open her heart to the possibility of a new beginnings and true happiness once again...? Funny, real and wonderfully romantic, this is the perfect feel-good read to keep you warm this winter! Praise for Jane Lacey-Crane: 'A beautifully written, emotional tale that will have you captivated from the off!' Rach. 'A story which made me feel all fuzzy inside and smiley on the outside from the first page right up until the end' SC. 'Fantastic read, good storyline, great twists and flowed well... My only complaint is that i got no housework done today as i couldn't put my kindle down!' 'An outstanding debut from a writer we're sure to hear more from! By turns funny, and heart-wrenching, it rarely takes anything for granted, and it gives us some laugh-out-loud moments too!' Mycroft.

Name: His Hollow Heart
Description:
Bella A pact made. Two orphans who vowed to never part. I was selfish-for once, I had to be. The last time I saw Callum he was standing at the dormer window as I sat in the back seat of a car. His dark eyes foreign to me. The hatred that radiated from his glare shot through me with more force than a silver bullet. In a matter of minutes he had become a stranger. No longer my best friend. He was now a boy with a hollow heart who was headed down a dark path. A path I knew I'd never cross again. Or, so I thought. CallumWe had a deal-always stay together. The day Bella left, I vowed I would find her. And when I did, she would feel what I felt as I watched her pull away into a new life, with a new family. Leaving me behind to endure a life that was wrapped inside the hands of a monster.It's been twelve years, but I've finally found her. Now it's time for her to bask in the darkness she's created inside of me.

## Mad About Ewe: A Second Chance Romance

Dawn Botstein is enjoying her independence after a divorce, managing her yarn store and her home. She's decided she's finished with men, until a striking silver fox enters her shop. It's her high school crush, the same man who rejected her thirty years ago.

Despite his undeniable charm and rugged beard, Dawn is determined not to get swept away this time. He's the opposite of her ex, and his attention ignites a thrill she thought was lost. However, she's hesitant to risk her heart again.

Meanwhile, Mike Pilota is undergoing his own mid-life reset. After his second divorce, he quit his job to be closer to his widowed mother, never expecting to see Dawn again. The moment he sees her, he's captivated and determined to make amends for past mistakes, proving he can be the man she deserves.

The core question is whether it's too late for a second chance. Can these two lonely hearts find their way back to each other?

**About the Book:**

"Mad About Ewe" is a full-length contemporary romance that can be enjoyed as a standalone story. It is Book #1 in the Common Threads series, which is part of the Seduction in the City World and the Penny Reid Book Universe.

Name: Wake My Heart
Description:
From bestselling and award-winning author Lydia Michaels comes a crossover small-town, second-chance romance series that welcomes readers back into the world of McCullough Mountain! Ryan Clooney moved to the outskirts of his small town, and the last thing he expected to find was love, but when he meets Maggie, the beautiful young widow next door, his plans change. Maggie O’Malley is not looking for romance. Since losing her husband, she’s withdrawn from her social life and prefers to be left alone. But her new neighbor doesn’t understand personal space, and the more he intrudes on her solitary world, the less control she has over her heart. Determined to charm Maggie, Ryan pulls out all the stops. Will Maggie choose to let go of her past to have a future, or is there another way to move on?

## A Fashion Editor's Second Chance at Love

Fiona Monaghan, a high-powered fashion magazine editor, lived a life of international glamour, filled with demanding designers, supermodels, and her bulldog, Sir Winston. Her meticulously organized world was turned upside down by the arrival of John Anderson, a widower raising two daughters. John's quiet, conservative nature was a stark contrast to Fiona's fast-paced lifestyle, yet an undeniable spark ignited between them.
