Michael Portillo, author and presenter, calls it "A thoroughly good read," while Terry Waite CBE, author of *Taken on Trust*, describes it as "A compelling story of courage, determination and skill."

**The Somali Piracy Crisis**

During the height of the Somali piracy crisis, several ships faced a dire fate. Three vessels, belonging to Malaysia, Thailand, and Taiwan, were hijacked. Their employers, unable to afford the ransom demands, abandoned them to their fate. These ships and their crews would have remained in peril were it not for the intervention of Colonel John Steed.

**A One-Man Mission**

Colonel Steed, a retired British military attaché, decided to take matters into his own hands. At 65 years old, he was an unlikely hero. He had no prior experience in hostage negotiations and no financial backing for his ambitious plan.

**Raising the Ransom**

Colonel Steed's mission involved a monumental task: raising millions of dollars to pay the ransom. He embarked on this challenge from scratch, operating his entire operation from his spare room. The logistics were extraordinary, including personally transporting million-dollar ransom payments in the boot of his car.

**An Inside Look at Hostage Negotiation**

Written by Colin Freeman, former chief foreign correspondent of The Sunday Telegraph, this book offers an unparalleled glimpse into the complex world of hostage negotiation. Freeman, who has personal experience being held hostage by Somali pirates, draws on first-hand interviews to bring readers an inside track on Colonel Steed's heroic rescue mission. The narrative explores:

*   The challenges of negotiating with pirates.
*   The immense pressure faced by those involved.
*   The personal sacrifices made during the operation.
*   The remarkable determination of Colonel Steed.

Name: Rescue Mission
Description:
Kidnappers have taken their baby. Their only mission is to find him. The moment his ex-fiancée tells him that he’s a father, military contractor Mike Spade is ready to make the commitment he’s always feared. Intelligence operative Summer Daniels is willing to give him another chance. Then their child is abducted. Mike and Summer both have enemies — and they both have unhealed hurts from their tempestuous romance. Can they overcome the past to save their son? Mills & Boon Intrigue — Seek thrills. Solve crimes. Justice served.

Name: On Wings of Eagles
Description:
#1 bestselling author Ken Follett tells the inspiring true story of the Middle East hostage crisis that began in 1978, and of the unconventional means one American used to save his countrymen. . . . When two of his employees were held hostage in a heavily guarded prison fortress in Iran, one man took matters into his own hands: businessman H. Ross Perot. His team consisted of a group of volunteers from the executive ranks of his corporation, handpicked and trained by a retired Green Beret officer. To free the imprisoned Americans, they would face incalculable odds on a mission that only true heroes would have dared. . . .

Name: Impossible Odds
Description:
A harrowing and heart-wrenching memoir of humanitarian aid worker Buchanan's kidnapping by Somali land pirates, her three months in captivity, her rescue by the Navy SEALs, and her husband's extraordinary efforts to help bring her home.

Name: Operation Jacknap
Description:
The New York Times described what happened to New York businessman Jack Teich as a “front page horror.” Two hundred FBI agents and Nassau County police officers combined forces to form a dragnet, hunt for his kidnappers, and rescue him. Teich lay handcuffed and chained to the walls of a closet in the Bronx with a medical bandage wrapped around his head to cover his eyes. His captors demanded that his wife, Janet, drop a bag with $750,000 (the equivalent of four million dollars in today’s currency) in a locker at Penn Station, making the Jack Teich ransom one of the highest in U.S. history at the time. FBI and Nassau County police detectives spent over a year before finally uncovering the meticulously planned kidnapping ploy hatched by radical mastermind Richard Warren Williams. The FBI internally dubbed the Jack Teich kidnapping operation “Jacknap.” The real-life crime drama that followed proved stranger than fiction, involving a tense across-the-country manhunt, a trailer in California stuffed with tens of thousands of ransom dollars hidden inside, a contentious jury trial that dominated NYC headlines for months; a guilty verdict that was overturned twenty-one years later on a controversial technicality; a retrial stymied by a mysterious fire that incinerated court records; and a civil verdict ruling that the kidnapper pay Jack Teich back the ransom money, plus interest. Operation Jacknap tells the incredible true crime story that continues even now. Indeed, as of this writing, no one knows where the majority of the ransom money is located. Inside, Teich also details his offer of a reward to anyone helping track down the still missing money and kidnappers.

## Live Younger, Longer: A Cardiologist's Guide to a Healthier, More Purposeful Life

Mayo Clinic cardiologist Stephen Kopecky, drawing from his extensive research and personal journey, presents a comprehensive, evidence-based strategy for disease prevention and enhancing life's quality and purpose.

Over the past century, the primary threats to human health have dramatically changed. We've moved from battling infectious diseases like the flu and tuberculosis to confronting chronic illnesses such as heart disease and cancer. These chronic conditions are now the leading causes of death globally, claiming over 1.2 million American lives annually. Interestingly, these very chronic diseases are, in part, a byproduct of our increased longevity. While we are living longer than ever before, the prevalence of chronic illness suggests that many are not living *better*. Dr. Stephen Kopecky, M.D., after personally overcoming two battles with cancer, embarked on a mission to identify actionable behaviors that promote longevity and freedom from chronic disease.

Dr. Kopecky's research revealed that the key to a longer, healthier life lies in adopting just six fundamental habits. These are not drastic overhauls, but rather small, consistent changes to your daily routine that yield significant long-term benefits. These habits encompass dietary choices, physical activity, sleep hygiene, stress management, substance use, and developing strategies to make these healthy habits last a lifetime. The true secret to success, as Dr. Kopecky emphasizes, isn't solely in the habits themselves, but in the *method* of their implementation. With over 30 years dedicated to cardiovascular disease prevention, Dr. Kopecky shares his accumulated knowledge from both his clinical practice and personal experiences, offering a roadmap to staying healthy, preventing chronic illnesses, and ultimately, living younger for longer.

### Divergent Longevity: Why Americans Age 50+ Live Shorter Lives Than Peers

Life expectancy at age 50 has seen an increase in the United States over the past 25 years. However, this growth lags behind that of many other high-income nations, including Japan and Australia. This disparity is particularly striking considering the U.S. invests more in healthcare than any other country.

#### Examining the Causes of Longevity Gaps

In response to this concern, the National Institute on Aging commissioned the National Research Council to investigate the potential reasons behind this divergence. The resulting report, "Explaining Divergent Levels of Longevity in High-Income Countries," highlights several key factors.

##### The Lingering Impact of Smoking

A significant contributor to shorter lifespans in the U.S. compared to other high-income nations is the nation's historical prevalence of heavy smoking. The long-term damage from this habit continues to affect longevity.

##### The Role of Obesity

Current high levels of obesity in the United States are also identified as playing a substantial role in reducing life expectancy. The report suggests this is a significant ongoing factor.

##### Healthcare Access and Mortality

The lack of universal healthcare access in the U.S. has also contributed to increased mortality and reduced life expectancy. While this is a less pronounced factor for individuals over 65 due to Medicare, it impacts younger demographics more significantly.

#### Strengths and Weaknesses in U.S. Healthcare for Older Adults

For the primary causes of death in older age groups – cancer and cardiovascular disease – the U.S. healthcare system does not appear to be failing in ways that would lead to preventable deaths compared to other nations.

*   **Cancer Detection and Survival:** The U.S. demonstrates strong performance in cancer detection and survival rates, often exceeding those in most other high-income countries.
*   **Cardiovascular Disease Survival:** Similarly, survival rates following a heart attack are favorable in the United States.

#### Research Gaps and Methodological Challenges

The report also identifies numerous areas where further research is needed. Unlike lung cancer deaths, which serve as a clear indicator of smoking's impact, there is no single, definitive marker for the effects of obesity, physical inactivity, social integration, or other risk factors examined. Furthermore, the evaluation of these risk factors often relies on observational studies, which are prone to various biases and differ from the more controlled environment of randomized controlled trials.

Name: Healthy Living
Description:
Want to spend more of your time doing things that you want to do with the help of lifestyle changes?Overcome Procrastination NOW. This book will show you how you can take a series of steps or make small changes to guide you towards your goal of having a healthy lifestyle and changing your life.This works because it only takes around 15 to 30 minutes of your time each day. By making small changes on daily aspects of your life, these changes can make you healthy and will positively affect your life, strengthen your relationships, and boost your health.Inside you will discover:6 little habits that can change your life8 lifestyle changes that can make you healthy and positively affect your lifeBest exercises to keep you fitAnd much, much more!Would you like to know more?Simply scroll up and click the buy button to get started.

## Unlock a Longer, Healthier Life with The Longevity Diet

Discover the groundbreaking approach to aging gracefully and living vibrantly with "The Longevity Diet," the international bestseller by renowned researcher Dr. Valter Longo. Hailed as a key to staying young and a pioneer in the fasting movement, Dr. Longo's work is backed by extensive scientific research.

### The Science Behind Cellular Regeneration

After three decades of dedicated research, biochemist Professor Valter Longo has identified a powerful link between diet and longevity. His findings reveal that a specialized diet can trigger cellular regeneration, a process crucial for slowing down aging, combating disease, and managing weight. This revolutionary approach, known as the fasting-mimicking diet, empowers you to harness the healing potential of food.

### Your Lifelong Health-Boosting Plan

"The Longevity Diet" offers a clear and actionable path to a healthier future. The plan includes:

*   **An Easy-to-Adopt Lifetime Strategy:** Integrate sustainable dietary habits into your daily life.
*   **Periodic Fasting-Mimicking Diet:** Undertake a short, 5-day fasting-mimicking diet 3-4 times annually. This approach delivers the benefits of fasting without the associated hunger.
*   **Delicious and Simple Recipes:** Enjoy 30 easy-to-prepare meals inspired by Longo's "Five Pillars of Longevity." These plant-and-fish-based recipes are designed for optimal health.

### Experience the Benefits

By embracing "The Longevity Diet," you can anticipate a range of positive outcomes:

*   **Enhanced Heart Health:** Recipes like black rice with courgette and shrimp are rich in antioxidants, supporting cardiovascular well-being.
*   **Improved Nutrient Intake:** Dark chocolate and yogurt provide a good source of iron, while dried cranberries and walnuts make for a tangy, healthy dessert.
*   **Disease Prevention:** This diet is designed to help build resistance to age-related conditions such as diabetes, cardiovascular disease, Alzheimer's, and cancer.
*   **Weight Management:** Maintain your ideal weight and effectively reduce abdominal fat.
*   **Preserved Muscle and Bone Health:** Combat age-related muscle and bone loss for greater mobility and strength.

Make simple, impactful changes to extend your healthy lifespan and live a more fulfilled life.

**The Blue Zones: Secrets to a Longer, Healthier Life**

Discover the secrets to living a longer, more fulfilling life. National Geographic Explorer Dan Buettner has identified "Blue Zones" – regions around the world where people consistently live to be 100 and beyond. This book reveals the lifestyle strategies that contribute to remarkable longevity in these unique communities.

Buettner's extensive research, spanning diverse cultures from Costa Rica to Italy and Japan, highlights a common thread: the way people connect, manage stress, eat, and perceive their world directly impacts their lifespan. He shares inspiring stories of centenarians, like a 94-year-old Costa Rican farmer and a 102-year-old Okinawan grandmother, showcasing the power of everyday choices.

By observing these vibrant societies, Buettner's teams have pinpointed key habits that align with the latest longevity research. These simple yet potent practices can be adopted by anyone looking to add healthy years to their life.

**Key Lifestyle Factors for Longevity:**

*   **Connection and Community:** Strong social ties and a sense of belonging are vital.
*   **Stress Management:** Effective ways to shed daily stress contribute to well-being.
*   **Nourishing the Body:** Diet plays a crucial role, with an emphasis on whole, plant-based foods.
*   **Purposeful Living:** Having a reason to wake up each morning is linked to a longer life.

This book offers a practical guide, blending these time-tested Blue Zone principles with scientific insights. It provides an accessible roadmap for making lasting changes that can significantly enhance your health and extend your years.

## Fueling a Lifetime of Well-being: Nutrition's Crucial Role in Healthy Aging

A pivotal workshop convened in September 2016 by the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine delved into the intricate relationship between nutrition and the journey of healthy aging within the United States. This comprehensive exploration aimed to illuminate current trends and patterns in the aging population, with a specific emphasis on how strategic nutritional choices can actively support and enhance well-being throughout an individual's entire lifespan. The discussions underscored the critical importance of considering nutritional needs from the earliest stages of life, beginning with pregnancy and early childhood, and extending through every phase of adulthood.

The workshop served as a vital platform for leading experts to engage in in-depth discussions on several interconnected themes central to understanding and promoting healthy aging. A primary focus was the nuanced role of nutrition across distinct life stages, examining how dietary intake influences the aging process at various junctures. This included a thorough consideration of the physiological transformations that occur within organ systems over a lifetime, particularly as individuals advance in age. Furthermore, the experts dedicated significant attention to the complex interplay between nutrition, brain health, and mental well-being, exploring age-related shifts in cognitive function and psychological health. Ultimately, the collective goal was to identify and advance actionable strategies that can effectively promote healthy aging nationwide, ensuring a higher quality of life for an increasing number of Americans. This publication distills the key insights and valuable perspectives shared during these impactful presentations and dialogues.

## Unlock a Healthier, Longer Life with Longevity Decoded

Achieving a long and vibrant life is within reach, and **Longevity Decoded** offers a practical guide to help you get there. This resource focuses on simple lifestyle changes that empower you to take charge of your well-being and actively shape your journey toward a healthier future.

Dr. Stephen Schimpff, a physician with extensive experience, highlights seven essential pillars for healthy aging. These principles are effective and require only your dedication:

*   **Nutrition:** Make smart food choices to properly fuel your body.
*   **Physical Activity:** Incorporate effective exercise into your daily routine.
*   **Stress Management:** Develop techniques to reduce and manage everyday stress.
*   **Quality Sleep:** Prioritize restorative sleep for better overall health.
*   **Tobacco-Free Living:** Avoid tobacco for significant health benefits.
*   **Mental Engagement:** Keep your mind active and stimulated.
*   **Social Connections:** Cultivate strong relationships for emotional and physical well-being.

**Longevity Decoded** provides clear, actionable advice that combines the science of aging with proven strategies. By understanding how your daily decisions impact your health now and in the future, you can proactively plan for a more fulfilling tomorrow. Healthcare and business leaders have praised the book for its insightful and accessible approach. For instance, James (Jim) M. Anders, Jr. notes how it illuminates the impact of daily choices, while R. Alan Butler calls it an "uplifting read" offering a prescription for a healthier life. Hiren Doshi commends Dr. Schimpff's "brilliant work" for making complex science accessible, and David B. Nash MD, MBA recommends it as a vital guide for active later years. E. Albert Reece, MD, PhD, MBA, also praises Dr. Schimpff for offering simple steps and an optimistic outlook. By embracing the seven keys in **Longevity Decoded**, you invest in your health and set a positive example for others.

## Businesses as Drivers of Community Well-being

This document distills the key takeaways from a July 2014 workshop convened by the Institute of Medicine's Roundtable on Population Health Improvement. The central theme explored was the transformative potential of businesses to actively foster healthier communities, extending their influence far beyond conventional workplace wellness initiatives.

This pivotal discussion was informed by prior roundtable dialogues that underscored the critical imperative of integrating health considerations into all facets of decision-making, irrespective of industry sector. Furthermore, it strongly advocated for robust inter-industry collaboration as an essential element for advancing the health of entire populations.

The workshop proceedings were enriched by the valuable perspectives shared by:

*   **Prominent business leaders** who have demonstrated a tangible commitment to elevating community health outcomes.
*   **Delegates representing business coalitions** specifically dedicated to advancing health-focused initiatives.

The workshop's core objectives were to:

*   **Articulate a compelling business rationale** for strategic investments in population health enhancement.
*   **Pinpoint actionable strategies** that empower businesses to spearhead community health endeavors.
*   **Investigate a spectrum of innovative approaches** through which businesses can meaningfully contribute to population health.

This report serves as a comprehensive record of the significant presentations and insightful discussions that characterized this influential event.

Name: The Blue Zones Solution
Description:
Bestselling author Dan Buettner reveals how to transform your health using smart nutrition, lifestyle, and fitness habits gleaned from longevity research on the diets, eating habits, and lifestyle practices of the communities he's identified as "Blue Zones"—those places with the world's longest-lived, and thus healthiest, people, including locations such as Okinawa, Japan; Sardinia, Italy; Costa Rica's Nicoya Peninsula; Ikaria, Greece; and Loma Linda, California. With the audacious belief that the lifestyles of the world's Blue Zones could be adapted and replicated in towns across North America, Buettner launched the largest preventive health care project in the United States, The Blue Zones City Makeovers, which has impacted the health of millions of Americans since 2009. In The Blue Zones Solution, readers can be inspired by the specific stories of the people, foods, and routines of our healthy elders; understand the role community, family, and naturally healthy habits can play in improving our diet and health; and learn the exact foods—including the 50 superfoods of longevity and dozens of recipes adapted for Western tastes and markets—that offer delicious ways to eat your way to optimum health. Throughout the book are lifestyle recommendations, checklists, and stories to help you create your own personal Blue Zones solution. Readers will learn and apply the 80/20 rule, the plant slant diet, social aspects of eating that lead to weight loss and great health naturally, cultivating your "tribe" of friends and family, and your greater purpose as part of your daily routine. Filled with moving personal stories, delicious recipes, checklists, and useful tips that will transform any home into a miniature blue zone, The Blue Zones Solution is the ultimate blueprint for a healthy, happy life.

Name: Jacob's School Play
Description:
Jacob—star of one of the most banned books of the decade according to the American Library Association—is back in his third book and ready to put on a school play! While learning their lines and making their costumes, Jacob’s class finds itself unexpectedly struggling with identity, and what it means to be “he,” “she,” or “they.” Jacob’s School Play is an engaging way to introduce young readers to non-binary people and the pronoun options available to us all. Learning that individuals are more nuanced than how others see them is a developmentally important milestone, and helps foster respect of one’s self and one’s peers. “An empowering and uplifting tale…perfect icebreaker for young students to have meaningful conversations about gender identity and community.”—The Advocate

Name: Wings of Fire Collection
Description:
A thrilling new series soars above the competition and redefines middle-grade fantasy fiction for a new generation! The seven dragon tribes have been at war for generations, locked in an endless battle over an ancient, lost treasure. A secret movement called the Talons of Peace is determined to bring an end to the fighting, with the help of a prophecy--a foretelling that calls for great sacrifice. Five dragonets are collected to fulfill the prophecy, raised in a hidden cave and enlisted, against their will, to end the terrible war. But not every dragonet wants a destiny. And when the select five escape their underground captors to look for their original homes, what has been unleashed on the dragon world may be far more than the revolutionary planners intended... This must-have collection includes the first three novels in the Wings of Fire series: * Wings of Fire Book One: The Dragonet Prophecy *Wings of Fire Book Two: The Lost Heir *Wings of Fire Book Three: The Hidden Kingdom

## Hawaii's Imperial Past: The Story of "Lost Kingdom"

"Lost Kingdom" by [Author's Name] offers a compelling look at Hawaii's struggle for sovereignty against the backdrop of Big Sugar's imperial ambitions. As noted by Douglas Brinkley, this New York Times bestseller vividly chronicles the conflict between the indigenous Polynesian people and the expanding influence of American capitalism. The book introduces a cast of characters, from Hawaiian royalty and opportunistic figures to powerful sugar barons and dedicated missionaries, all of whom shaped the Hawaiian Kingdom's destiny.

The narrative prominently features Queen Lili‘uokalani, Hawaii's last reigning monarch. Her reign, beginning in 1838, occurred during a period of significant economic transformation. The lucrative sugar industry, driven by white planters known as the "Sugar Kings," gradually consolidated control over much of the islands' land. This economic power made Hawaii a coveted strategic location in the Pacific, attracting the attention of America, Britain, and France, who vied for military and trade dominance.

Queen Lili‘uokalani recognized the growing threat to her nation's independence. She attempted to introduce a new constitution to bolster the monarchy's authority. However, U.S. intervention ultimately led to Hawaii's annexation, marking a significant moment in American imperialism. The New York Times Book Review hails "Lost Kingdom" as essential reading, calling it "an important chapter in our national history that most Americans are unaware of but should know." Fortune praises the book's "captivating and personal account of the rise and tragic end of Hawaii’s royal family," while the Los Angeles Times commends its "thoroughly researched, well-placed history," accurately describing the events as "[Indeed] ‘one of the most audacious land grabs of the Gilded Age.’"

Name: The Lost Kingdoms of the Nakshatras
Description:
I am practicing astrology from my childhood and with multiple decades of experience I am presenting in this book a story of 27 nakshatras in story telling format to understand it more deeply. Astrology is a divine science and everybody should learn it to understand the divine code of this universe. How our karma of past life manifest in to the present life and it reflects in our horoscope. Horoscope is nothing but snapshot of our karma of past life. I hope people will love reading this and I welcome everyone to dive in to this divine knowledge. Regards, Saket Shah

Name: Cavelli's Lost Heir
Description:
Normally Prince Nico Cavelli would never waste his time visiting the prison cell of a tourist. Except this particular alleged criminal has stolen something very personal to him-- his son, heir to the Montebianco throne Lily Morgan always knew it was a mistake coming to the Mediterranean kingdom, but she'd had no choice. First she was thrown into jail for a crime she didn't commit...now she's been bailed out by the prince--though in return she must become his royal wife

Name: Swimming in the Deep End
Description:
"In this moving, masterful saga from best-selling novelist Christina Suzann Nelson, four women's lives collide. Their tangled courses and attempts to find grace in the midst of crisis combine in one heartfelt story showing the many faces of motherly love"--

Name: The Lost Heir
Description:
NY Times Bestselling Author E.G. Foley brews up a magical potion for fantasy readers of all ages! Take a dash of Harry Potter and a splash of Oliver Twist, add a pinch of steampunk and a sprinkle of Victorian fairy lore, and what do you get? The Gryphon Chronicles! A rollicking fantasy adventure series that's as much fun for grownups as it is for kids. Strange new talents... Jake is a scrappy orphaned pickpocket living by his wits on the streets of Victorian London. Lately he's started seeing ghosts and discovers he can move solid objects with his mind! He has no idea why. Next thing he knows, a Sinister Gentleman and his minions come hunting him. On the run for his life, Jake is plunged headlong into a mysterious world full of magic and deadly peril. A world that holds the secret to who he really is: the long-lost heir of an aristocratic family - with magical powers! But with treacherous enemies closing in, it will take all of his wily street instincts and the help of his friends - both human and magical - to solve the mystery of what happened to his parents, and defeat the foes who never wanted the Lost Heir of Griffon to be found... (270 pages / 100,409 words)

Name: The Lost Heir (Wings of Fire #2)
Description:
The WINGS OF FIRE saga continues with a thrilling underwater adventure -- and a mystery that will change everything! The lost heir to the SeaWing throne is going home at last.She can't believe it's finally happening. Tsunami and her fellow dragonets of destiny are journeying under the water to the great SeaWing Kingdom. Stolen as an egg from the royal hatchery, Tsunami is eager to meet her future subjects and reunite with her mother, Queen Coral.But Tsunami's triumphant return doesn't go quite the way she'd imagined. Queen Coral welcomes her with open wings, but a mysterious assassin has been killing off the queen's heirs for years, and Tsunami may be the next target. The dragonets came to the SeaWings for protection, but this ocean hides secrets, betrayal--and perhaps even death.

Name: Lost Heirs
Description:
Prophecy and destiny combine to tear peasant-born twins, Ilan and Adra from their home and return them to the throne their grandfather refused. Compelled to stay by the needs of their people; driven to go by their resentful nobles and hostile neighbors, Ilan can't forget his duty while Adra only wants her freedom back. Regardless of how they choose, they stand to lose but if they can't agree, they are going to lose each other and the country has its own plans for them.

## Unveiling the Middle East's Hidden Faiths: A Legacy of Diversity

The Middle East, often viewed as religiously monolithic, is in fact a vibrant tapestry woven with a rich diversity of ancient and unique faiths. These often-overlooked traditions are the enduring legacy of once-powerful civilizations, including Persia, Babylon, and Pharaonic Egypt. Despite centuries of invasions and assimilation pressures, their adherents have persisted. However, the current climate of regional turmoil presents unprecedented challenges to these distinctive religions.

### Exploring Ancient Traditions

In "Heirs to Forgotten Kingdoms," former diplomat Gerard Russell embarks on a journey into the remote regions where these mysterious faiths continue to survive. His exploration highlights communities such as:

*   **Mandaeans and Ezidis in Iraq:** Russell delves into their unique cosmologies and practices.
*   **Zoroastrians in Iran:** He uncovers the rich history and rituals of this ancient Persian faith.
*   **Copts in Egypt:** He examines the enduring Christian traditions of this ancient community.
*   **Other lesser-known groups:** Russell sheds light on a spectrum of faiths striving to exist.

Through extensive travel and research, Russell documents their histories, participates in their rituals, and gains a profound understanding of the existential threats they face.

### Facing Modern Challenges

While Islam historically fostered tolerance, the rise of militant sects since the early 20th century, coupled with the destabilizing effects of Western interventions, now poses a grave threat to these minority faiths.

Furthermore, a significant exodus of young people seeking greater freedoms and economic opportunities in the West is depleting these communities of their future generations. This demographic shift brings these ancient religions perilously close to extinction.

"Heirs to Forgotten Kingdoms" meticulously documents the past, present, and precarious future of these remarkable, yet vulnerable, religious traditions, prompting reflection on what will be lost if these voices fall silent and whether these ancient faiths can truly endure in the face of modern pressures.

Name: The Moor's Account
Description:
PULITZER PRIZE FINALIST • A NEW YORK TIMES NOTABLE BOOK • The imagined memoirs of the first black explorer of America—this "stunning [book] sheds light on all of the possible the New World exploration stories that didn’t make history” (Huffington Post). In these pages, Laila Lalami brings us the invented memoirs Mustafa al-Zamori, called Estebanico. The slave of a Spanish conquistador, Estebanico sails for the Americas with his master, Dorantes, as part of a danger-laden expedition to Florida. Within a year, Estebanico is one of only four crew members to survive. As he journeys across America with his Spanish companions, the Old World roles of slave and master fall away, and Estebanico remakes himself as an equal, a healer, and a remarkable storyteller. His tale illuminates the ways in which our narratives can transmigrate into history—and how storytelling can offer a chance at redemption and survival.

**Healing from Betrayal: A Guide to Rebuilding Your Relationship**

Betrayal can deeply wound the bond between partners. In her book, *Courageous Love*, Dr. Stefanie Carnes offers a practical, step-by-step approach to mending relationships fractured by infidelity, pornography use, or compulsive sexual behaviors.

Dr. Carnes guides couples through the process of:

*   **Responding with Compassion:** Learning to approach each other with empathy and understanding during a difficult time.
*   **Maintaining Hope:** Cultivating a sense of optimism for the future of the relationship.
*   **Detailed Repair Process:** Following a clear roadmap to restore the relationship and foster new growth.

*Courageous Love* is an essential resource for any couple navigating the challenging aftermath of betrayal.

Name: The Authentic Heart
Description:
The richest, most fulfilling love of your life is yet to come! "The Authentic Heart offers practical, wise, and compassionate guidance for midlife love."--Jack Kornfield, author of A Path with Heart and After the Ecstasy, the Laundry "The Authentic Heart is a groundbreaking, insightful, warmly written book that I highly recommend to anyone wanting more loving, joyful relationships. John Amodeo addresses with great clarity, wisdom, and practicality the key steps that are necessary for building authentic, mature, loving connections--not only with others, but also with oneself."--John Bradshaw, New York Times bestselling author of Healing the Shame that Binds You "Just what millions want to know--not only how to make love last but how to make lasting love new again and again. This warmhearted and clearheaded book is full of practical wisdom."--Gay Hendricks, Ph.D., and Kathlyn Hendricks, Ph.D., authors of Conscious Loving and The Conscious Heart As you enter midlife, you may feel that something you've always longed for has never happened. Frustrated, you may give up on love or cling to young images of romantic love, hoping that another person will furnish happiness. You may experience a growing sense of depression, anxiety, or cynicism. But as psychotherapist and relationship expert Dr. John Amodeo explains, authentic love takes time and maturity. At midlife, you hold the extraordinary potential to become more fully awake and alive in your relationships than ever before. In this groundbreaking book, Dr. Amodeo helps you rediscover love at its best. You'll learn how to overcome the psychological obstacles that have kept you from developing satisfying relationships. And you'll learn the eight enriching steps that release your authentic self for the fullness of genuine connection. Whether you're seeking true love for the first time or wish to deepen the joy and meaning in your current relationship, The Authentic Heart guides you along the path to a more vibrant partnership in the prime of your life.

## Unbound: A Shanghai Saga of Resilience

**Unbound**, a finalist for the 2021 Next Generation Indie Book Award, tells a powerful multigenerational story. It follows two determined women, a grandmother and her granddaughter, against the backdrop of twentieth-century Shanghai. This richly textured novel captures the city's turbulent spirit, where survival meant fighting for a place amidst political change and wartime chaos.

### Mini's Shanghai Struggle

Mini Pao lives in pre-war Shanghai, a city divided by foreign powers and its own citizens. Known as the "Paris of the East," it offered vibrant nightlife alongside strict social rules. At twenty-three, Mini was already considered an old maid. She defied expectations, striving to support her family while yearning for romance and personal freedom. Her story of love, betrayal, and resolve unfolds in Shanghai's Western cafes, bustling markets, and smoky jazz clubs.

### Ting's Quest for Truth

Decades later, Mini's granddaughter, Ting, begins her own path to independence in the same city. Ting grew up behind the "iron curtain" during Chairman Mao's Cultural Revolution. This era was marked by scarcity, hardship, and enforced conformity. Consequently, Ting's imagination was fueled by a fascination with fashion, America, and especially her grandmother Mini's seemingly glamorous past and enigmatic present.

### Uncovering a Hidden History

As Ting's curiosity about the world beyond 1970s Shanghai intensifies, she becomes driven to uncover her family's tragic history. She must also confront the harsh reality of her own future if she remains in China.

Name: Betrayal of Love and Freedom
Description:
Two powerful men-one at the mercy of bipolar disorder, the other facing life imprisonment-struggle to regain their freedom. The life of Luke Powers has long been punctuated by abrupt changes in fortune, but nothing could prepare him for the possibility of life imprisonment. Facing charges of murdering the love of his life from thirty years earlier, the influential media mogul is powerless to escape his predicament. Is he a murderer? Even he cannot say for sure. Meanwhile, entrepreneur and family man Rick Dellich finds himself stripped of all his rights as a citizen, the result of a mental breakdown. Separated from those he loves, he faces the prospect of confinement to a chemical straightjacket for the rest of the life. Desperate for a true recovery, he commits himself to a psychiatric clinic. Rick struggles to put his shattered life back together, but his deepening search for answers leads him to revelations that threaten to turn his world upside down. Through it all, two women, each offering the possibility of the love that seems to have betrayed him all his life, weave their way into Luke's destiny. Will each man succeed in his quest for love and freedom? Will each man find what he is seeking? Both will face challenges that will destroy or transform them, eventually placing the two on a collision course with one another. Set against a backdrop of four continents, this is a story of power, love, and ultimate freedom.

Name: Love and Betrayal
Description:
Violette and Thomas are in France for Camdens coronation into French nobility. Married for seventeen years, their happiness is threatened by the arrival of a suspicious letter from the past, which threatens to destroy their love and marriage. Mary, queen of Scots, seeks refuge in England, hoping that Queen Elizabeth will restore her to the Scottish throne. Instead, Mary finds herself Queen Elizabeths prisoner. She is entangled in a murderous plot to assassinate Queen Elizabeth. Marys life hangs by a thread.

## Capote's Women: The Scandalous True Story Behind "Answered Prayers"

**Discover the captivating real-life drama that inspired FX's FEUD: CAPOTE VS. THE SWANS, now streaming on Hulu!** This original series is brought to life by Laurence Leamer's New York Times bestselling book, which meticulously unravels the intricate relationships and explosive true stories at the heart of Truman Capote's unfinished masterpiece, "Answered Prayers."

### The Elite Circle: Capote's "Swans"

Truman Capote famously observed, "There are certain women... who, though perhaps not born rich, are born to be rich." He was referring to an exclusive group of women who defined midcentury New York society: Barbara "Babe" Paley, Gloria Guinness, Marella Agnelli, Slim Hayward, Pamela Churchill, C. Z. Guest, and Lee Radziwill, sister of Jacqueline Kennedy. These were the women who graced the covers of magazines and the pages of society columns, the undisputed queens of their era.

### A Confidant and a Betrayer

Capote, a master of social maneuvering, cultivated deep friendships with these influential women. He became their confidant, privy to their most intimate secrets and woven into the fabric of their glamorous lives. He gained their trust, and in return, they shared their confidences freely. However, this intimate access would ultimately lead to a shocking and profound betrayal.

### The Genesis of "Answered Prayers"

Following the immense success of "Breakfast at Tiffany's" in 1958 and "In Cold Blood" in 1966, Capote found himself grappling with a severe case of writer's block. Despite enjoying the accolades and material comforts of his fame, an idea began to form for what he envisioned as his most celebrated work. This ambitious novel would draw inspiration from the remarkable, often scandalous, lives of his wealthy female friends.

### The Unfinished Magnum Opus

For years, Capote dedicated himself to writing "Answered Prayers," believing it would be his magnum opus. He meticulously crafted the narrative, intending to capture the essence of these extraordinary women. However, when he eventually published a few select chapters in Esquire magazine, the thinly veiled portrayals of his "swans" and their secrets were exposed to the public.

### The Fallout and the Legacy

The publication of these excerpts had devastating consequences. The thinly fictionalized accounts of their lives and scandals were laid bare, leading to Capote's swift and permanent banishment from the high-society world he had so skillfully infiltrated. Laurence Leamer's compelling work meticulously reconstructs the lives of these fascinating women, their complex friendships with Capote and each other, and the ill-fated pursuit of a novel that promised to be one of the greatest literary achievements of the twentieth century, but ultimately led to its author's social downfall.

Name: Love & Betrayal
Description:
"A book of profound insight and wisdom, written not just for those who have been betrayed, but for anyone who wants to build deeper, more trusting relationships." SUSAN M. CAMPBELL, Ph.D. Author of THE COUPLES JOURNEY LOVE & BETRAYAL explores the many forms betrayal can take, from keeping secrets and negative gossip to breaking promises and sexual infidelity. In the process, you can discover how to heal the wounds of past betrayals, what steps to take to forgive both yourself and your betrayer, and how to cultivate a climate of love and trust in your current relationships.

## Navigating the Aftermath of Betrayal: A Journey of Healing and Transformation

Experiencing betrayal in a culture that often dismisses the profound impact of such trauma and rushes the grieving process presents a unique and challenging path to recovery. The author's personal encounter with the sudden departure of her long-term partner, mere weeks before their wedding, revealed a depth of pain she was entirely unprepared for. This shock was particularly disorienting, even for someone who had previously navigated the profound loss of her husband's death. The erosion of trust in one individual triggered an existential and spiritual crisis, fundamentally questioning her understanding of human nature and prompting a deep inquiry into the "why" behind such devastation.

### Documenting the Inner Landscape

As she grappled with what proved to be a significant trauma, the author began meticulously documenting her internal experiences. Her aim was to capture the complex interplay of love, grief, longing, bewilderment, and the lingering effects of post-traumatic stress. This introspective endeavor, undertaken with remarkable fearlessness and unvarnished honesty, resulted in a profound exploration of the human heart.

### A Tapestry of Research and Personal Revelation

"Love and the Mystery of Betrayal" masterfully weaves together scholarly research with deeply personal reflections. It navigates the intricate threads of love and heartbreak, rage and eventual transformation, connecting individual experiences to broader human themes. The writing is both profound and engaging, offering a rare form of solace that can only come from a kindred spirit who has traversed similar emotional terrain.

### Finding Strength in Shattered Worlds

This moving chronicle and meditation on the enigmatic nature of love and betrayal ultimately illustrates how faith and love can not only endure but also flourish, even in the wake of life-altering revelations and profound loss. The book's honest portrayal of heartbreak is a testament to its authenticity.

### Endorsements from Esteemed Voices

*   "This story of heartbreak has a rare quality: it is absolutely honest." - **Ginette Paris, PhD,** author of "Heartbreak"
*   "...a powerful book that will serve many." - **Tara Brach, PhD,** author of "Radical Acceptance," "True Refuge"
*   "Sandra Dennis does not sugar-coat the experience of abandonment and betrayal with easy tips on getting over it or with spiritual bypass sleight of hand.... A much needed contribution to our collective healing..." - **Francis Weller,** Founder of Wisdom Bridge, author of "Entering the Healing Ground"
*   "What Sandra Dennis tells us about the transformative power of suffering is so important and so true. I hope many read this book; many surely are in need of it." - **Fr. Richard Rohr,** author of "Silent Compassion," "Breathing Underwater"
*   "...a rare and beautiful book...invaluable for anyone interested in harnessing the deepest human heartbreak as a crucible for spiritual awakening....a triumph of spirit." - **Miranda Macpherson,** author of "Boundless Love"
*   "...a powerful and thoughtful book right from the heart that will be a source of comfort and assistance to a lot of hurting people." - **Lundy Bancroft,** author of "Why Does He Do That?"
*   "...probes the subject of betrayal in an almost kinesthetic way, like a dance that is also superbly intelligent." - **Charlie Fisher, PhD,** author of "Meditation in the Wild" and "Dismantling Discontent"
*   "What a remarkable book Sandra Dennis has written! I celebrate her courage and discoveries, and welcome her home!" - **Gangaji,** author of "Hidden Treasure," "A Diamond in Your Pocket"

Name: Fear and Courage
Description:
We have all felt fear, whether it’s our racing heart as we make a speech or the profound awareness of our own mortality as we await medical results. Of course, the flip-side of fear is courage: as Nelson Mandela famously said, ‘I learned that courage was not the absence of fear but the triumph over it.’ The 25 true stories showcased here capture the full range of the fear and courage experience. At times humorous, often poignant, they shine a light on just what it means to be human.

## Navigating Love and Loss: A Therapist's Perspective

The profound impact of love and loss is a universal human experience, shaping our greatest joys and deepest sorrows. Relationships, by their very nature, evolve and often end, whether through departure, distance, or death. Loss is an inescapable facet of life. In her book, *Love and Loss*, Linda B. Sherby explores the essential human process of mourning and integrating loss, enabling growth and a continued vibrant life.

### The Interplay of Love and Loss in Therapy

Sherby posits that while love isn't guaranteed, a life devoid of deeply meaningful connections lacks true satisfaction. The core of *Love and Loss* lies in examining how the individual experiences of love and loss by both patients and therapists, alongside the dynamics within the therapeutic relationship itself, converge and influence one another.

### The Analyst's Personal Journey

Crucially, Sherby highlights that the consulting room is occupied by two individuals, each navigating their own lives and the unique bond formed in therapy. The personal experiences of love and loss for either party inevitably ripple through the therapeutic space, impacting the process on both conscious and unconscious levels.

*Love and Loss* distinguishes itself in two key ways. Firstly, it delves into the analyst's present life circumstances and their inherent influence on the patient and the treatment. Secondly, Sherby bravely shares her personal memoir of loss, weaving it with rich clinical examples to vividly demonstrate how an analyst's current life situation shapes the therapeutic encounter.

### A Unique Insight for a Broad Audience

Writing with the dual perspective of a psychoanalyst and a widow, Sherby offers readers an intimate glimpse into the emotional world of a therapist. This makes *Love and Loss* a compelling read for a diverse audience, including psychoanalysts, psychotherapists, bereavement specialists, mental health students, and the general public. The book's heartfelt and direct approach promises resonance and valuable learning for all.

Name: Grieving the Loss of Someone You Love
Description:
Few losses are as painful as the death of someone close. No valley is as vast as grief, no journey as personal and life changing. Compassionate and wise guides Raymond Mitsch and Lynn Brookside shine a light on the road through grief. They can help you endure the anguish and uncertainty; understand the cycles of grief; sort through the emotions of anger, guilt, fear, and depression; and face the God who allowed you to lose the one you love. A series of thoughtful daily devotions, Grieving the Loss of Someone You Love shares wisdom, insight, and comfort that will help you through and beyond your grief.

Name: In Love
Description:
NEW YORK TIMES BESTSELLER • A powerful memoir of a love that leads two people to find a courageous way to part—and a woman’s struggle to go forward in the face of loss—that “enriches the reader’s life with urgency and gratitude” (The Washington Post) “A pleasure to read . . . Rarely has a memoir about death been so full of life. . . . Bloom has a talent for mixing the prosaic and profound, the slapstick and the serious.”—USA Today ONE OF THE TEN BEST BOOKS OF THE YEAR: Publishers Weekly ONE OF THE BEST BOOKS OF THE YEAR: The New York Times Book Review, Time, Entertainment Weekly, NPR, The Washington Post, The Boston Globe, USA Today, Real Simple, Prospect (UK), She Reads, Kirkus Reviews Amy Bloom began to notice changes in her husband, Brian: He retired early from a new job he loved; he withdrew from close friendships; he talked mostly about the past. Suddenly, it seemed there was a glass wall between them, and their long walks and talks stopped. Their world was altered forever when an MRI confirmed what they could no longer ignore: Brian had Alzheimer’s disease. Forced to confront the truth of the diagnosis and its impact on the future he had envisioned, Brian was determined to die on his feet, not live on his knees. Supporting each other in their last journey together, Brian and Amy made the unimaginably difficult and painful decision to go to Dignitas, an organization based in Switzerland that empowers a person to end their own life with dignity and peace. In this heartbreaking and surprising memoir, Bloom sheds light on a part of life we so often shy away from discussing—its ending. Written in Bloom’s captivating, insightful voice and with her trademark wit and candor, In Love is an unforgettable portrait of a beautiful marriage, and a boundary-defying love. Shortlisted for the Rathbones Folio Prize

Name: Grief Dialogues
Description:
Grief Dialogues: The Book harnesses the power of words and images to combat grief. Sometimes the stories are funny. Sometimes hopeful. Sometimes inspiring. Always heartfelt, honest, and reflective. Proceeds of this book are donated to People's Memorial Association (PMA) since 1939, and to the Funeral Consumers Alliance (FCA).

Name: Finding Love After Loss
Description:
Guides readers through the emotions and practical concerns of finding love after the death of a partner. Romantic love, in all its permutations, forms one of the most fascinating of human interactions. It also can be one of life’s thorniest challenges, especially in a world where relationships often unfold online and, recently, where a pandemic barred face-to-face contact with people outside one’s immediate household. Among those seeking romance in increasing numbers is a group that stands apart: the women who, slammed by the death of a spouse, bravely pursue new love. Finding Love After Loss: A Relationship Roadmap for Widows goes to the trenches to interview widows who have embarked, nervously but with hope, on this quest. Their frank and revealing interviews, along with wisdom from relationship experts, provide guidance to other women trying to navigate the relationship scene when their last date might have been decades ago. Where do widows find new partners? How much should they share in their online profile? What do they tell their friends and family? What about getting naked for the first time with a new man? Who pays when the bill appears at a restaurant? More than any time in U.S. history, the country’s widows are seeking another chance at romance. The sheer number of widows—11 million, with an average age in the fifties—makes them a formidable force. They are living longer and have broader views on sex and money. Yet it is difficult for them to find their footing. Many of them have been away from the courtship arena for decades. They may make their return to dating with children and in-laws in tow. They are confused by the new rules and unclear on the expectations but convinced that they are capable of loving again. This book, written by a widow and a co-author who dated a widower, details just how powerful, sometimes daunting, and exhilarating the journey to new love can be. It also unveils the extraordinary ways that widows are reshaping the romance landscape: by tossing traditional marriage vows by the roadside, by skipping marriage entirely, or even by committing to a new partner but living apart. This isn’t your grandmother’s widowhood scene, not by a long shot. Finding Love After Loss examines the crazy, sad, and even zany contributions that people left behind by the death of a partner bring to new relationships. At the same time, it reveals both the amazing resilience of women who have lived through great loss and the irresistible pull of human connection.

Navigating the profound and often overwhelming experience of grief can feel like an insurmountable challenge without adequate support. The emotional and spiritual toll of loss can leave individuals feeling depleted, questioning deeply held beliefs, and struggling to find a sense of peace. In these difficult times, mindfulness meditation emerges as a powerful tool, offering a sanctuary of compassion and a pathway toward healing and self-reflection.

While the journey of grief is unique to each individual, this book provides a gentle and supportive guide to help you move forward. Through simple, accessible mindfulness-based exercises, you will be encouraged to reconnect with your mind, body, and spirit. These easy-to-follow meditations are designed to help you effectively cope with the pain of loss, fostering a sense of resilience as you embark on your personal healing journey.

Each chapter delves into a distinct facet of the grieving process, offering targeted meditations to calm a turbulent mind and cultivate greater clarity and focus. By engaging with these practices, you can begin the essential work of reconstructing your sense of self, which may feel shattered in the wake of a significant loss. This approach empowers you to find solace and strength, ultimately guiding you toward a more integrated and hopeful future.

**Love, Loss, and What We Ate: A Culinary Memoir of Family and Identity**

Padma Lakshmi's memoir, "Love, Loss, and What We Ate," offers a compelling journey through her life, intricately weaving together themes of food, family, and personal growth. This vivid account explores how our relationship with food mirrors our connections to love, comfort, and the very essence of home. It's a story that resonates with the rich storytelling of Ruth Reichl's "Tender at the Bone" and the sharp wit of Nora Ephron's "Heartburn."

**An Immigrant's Journey and the Taste of Home**

Long before her television career, Padma Lakshmi understood that food was more than sustenance. It was a language of love, a source of comfort, and a way to connect with the world. Her childhood was marked by frequent travel between continents, a sense of displacement that would follow her into adulthood. Despite this rootlessness, one constant remained: the simple, comforting taste of rice, reminiscent of her grandmother's kitchen in South India.

**From South India to the Camera's Lens**

"Love, Loss, and What We Ate" chronicles Lakshmi's extraordinary path, beginning in that humble South Indian kitchen, presided over by strong, unforgettable women. Her story moves from these formative experiences to her prominent role on "Top Chef" and beyond.

The memoir highlights the profound influence of the remarkable individuals who shaped her life:

*   **Her Headstrong Mother:** Who defied conservative Indian traditions to build a new life in New York.
*   **Her Brahmin Grandfather:** A brilliant engineer with a notable fondness for sweets.
*   **A Surprising Ally:** A man who, despite initial appearances, became her most steadfast supporter.

**A Feast for the Senses**

Rich with sensual prose and interspersed with evocative recipes, "Love, Loss, and What We Ate" immerses the reader in the scents, tastes, and textures of a life lived across diverse landscapes, both geographical and emotional. It's an intimate and surprising exploration of food and family – the families we inherit and the ones we build – and the lasting impact they have on us.

Name: Love and Loss
Description:
Loving and grieving are two sides of the same coin: we cannot have one without risking the other. Only by understanding the nature and pattern of loving can we begin to understand the problems of grieving. Conversely, the loss of a loved person can teach us much about the nature of love. Love and Loss, the result of a lifetime's work, has important implications for the study of attachment and bereavement. In this volume, Colin Murray Parkes reports his innovative research that enables us to bring together knowledge of childhood attachments and problems of bereavement, resulting in a new way of thinking about love, bereavement and other losses. Areas covered include: patterns of attachment and grief loss of a parent, child or spouse in adult life social isolation and support. The book concludes by looking at disorders of attachment and considering bereavement in terms of its implications on love, loss, and change in a wider context. Illuminating the structure and focus of thinking about love and loss, this book sheds light on a wide range of psychological issues. It will be essential reading for professionals working with bereavement, as well as graduate students of psychology, psychiatry, and sociology.

Name: Return to the Forbidden Planet
Description:
Inspired by Shakepeare's The Tempest, this juke box musical is packed with rock 'n' roll classics such as Heard it Through the Grapevine, Young Girl, Good Vibrations, and Gloria. Blast off on a routine flight and crash into the planet D'Illyria where a sci fi version of The Tempest set to rock and roll golden oldies unfolds with glee. The planet is inhabited by a sinister scientist, Dr. Prospero; his delightful daughter Miranda; Ariel, a faithful robot on roller skates; and an uncontrollable monster, the product of Prospero's Id, whose tentacles penetrate the space craft.

Name: Metropolis (大都會)
Description:
This remarkable novel, the basis for the world's greatest science-fiction movie, has long been a rare but ardently sought-after collector's item. It is an unforgettable vision of the 21st century and the awe-inspiring city of the future. Metropolis has been compared to such classics as George Orwell's 1984, H. G. Wells's The Time Machine, Samuel Butler's Erewhon, and Karel Capek's R.U.R. Science fiction writer and editor Forrest J Ackerman called it "a work of genius," noting, "The language of the novel is sometimes as thesauric as Shiel, as kaliedoscopic as Merritt, as bone-spare as Bradbury, as poetic as Poe, as macabre as Machen. . . . You will have an experience in reading that will last you all the rest of your life."

**Broken Metropolis: Queer Tales of the City That Never Was**

Dive into the vibrant world of *Broken Metropolis: Queer Tales of the City That Never Was*, an anthology edited by Dave Ring. This collection pushes the boundaries of urban fantasy, offering a fresh perspective on queer narratives. It follows in the footsteps of influential works like *Swords of the Rainbow* (Alyson Publications, 1996) and *Bending the Landscape* (Overlook Books, 1997).

**Exploring Urban Fantasy Through a Queer Lens**

*Broken Metropolis* features ten unique stories, each shining with its own distinct brilliance. These tales delve into the magic of possibility within queer communities, using fantasy as a medium to explore complex themes.

**A Glimpse into the Stories:**

*   **Claire Rudy Foster's contribution** follows a scientist as she navigates self-acceptance and the surprising influence of astrology on her romantic life.
*   **Caspian Gray's story** centers on a young man's quest to find an urban legend within a hospital ward. His goal is to save the matriarch of his chosen family.

These narratives, among others in the collection, showcase the diverse experiences and rich inner lives of queer individuals. *Broken Metropolis* invites readers to explore a fantastical city where anything is possible.

**Metropolis: How Cities Forged Human Civilization**

Ben Wilson's acclaimed book, *Metropolis*, presents a sweeping, millennia-spanning narrative that highlights how urban living has been the driving force behind humankind's most significant innovations. This captivating exploration delves into both celebrated and overlooked cities, revealing the profound impact of urban environments on our collective development. As *The Wall Street Journal* aptly describes it, "A towering achievement.... Reading this book is like visiting an exhilarating city for the first time—dazzling."

### The City: A Crucible of Innovation

For over two hundred millennia of human existence, no force has shaped our trajectory more profoundly than the city. From their nascent stages, urban centers have been fertile ground for an explosion of human creativity and endeavor. This dynamic environment fostered the emergence of:

*   **New Professions:** Specialization and diverse roles arose from concentrated populations.
*   **Novel Art Forms:** Artistic expression flourished, influenced by diverse interactions and patronage.
*   **Evolved Religious Practices:** Collective worship and philosophical inquiry found new expressions.
*   **Expanded Trade Networks:** Cities became hubs for the exchange of goods, ideas, and cultures.

These developments collectively ignited the very process of civilization.

### Urban Hubs and Groundbreaking Advancements

Wilson expertly guides readers through history, illustrating the groundbreaking innovations that thrived due to the unique energy of people living in close proximity. The book showcases how cities have been the birthplace of:

*   **Civic Life:** The foundations of democracy and public discourse were laid in the agoras of ancient Athens.
*   **Global Commerce:** The intricate networks of global trade took root and flourished in ninth-century Baghdad.
*   **Financial Systems:** The complex world of finance and banking emerged from the bustling coffeehouses of London.
*   **Domestic Refinements:** Innovations in comfort and daily living were perfected within the well-ordered environments of Amsterdam.
*   **Social Expression:** The art of social display and cultural performance reached new heights in Belle Époque Paris.

### Modern Aspirations and a Sustainable Tomorrow

In more recent times, the iconic skyscrapers of New York City inspired utopian visions for community design and urban planning. Looking towards the future, the emphasis on green spaces in twenty-first-century cities like Seattle and Shanghai offers compelling glimpses of a sustainable urban existence in the face of climate change.

*Metropolis* is an irresistible, richly detailed, and page-turning account that powerfully underscores a fundamental truth: the story of human civilization is intrinsically interwoven with the story of its cities.

## Love in the Big City: A Korean Millennial's Search for Connection

Sang Young Park's *Love in the Big City*, a runaway bestseller in Korea and a highly anticipated English-language debut, offers a funny, transporting, and poignant glimpse into the life of a young gay man navigating the complexities of love and happiness in the bustling, often lonely, metropolis of Seoul. This novel, which achieved remarkable success with twenty-six printings and widespread critical acclaim for its unique literary voice, is now set to captivate a global audience.

### Young's Seoul Sojourn

At its heart, the story follows Young, a Korean student whose cynicism is tempered by a fun-loving spirit. His life in Seoul is a dynamic cycle of home, classes, and fleeting connections forged through Tinder. He shares his apartment and his anxieties with Jaehee, his closest friend and roommate. Together, they seek solace in local bars, using soju and chilled Marlboro Reds to momentarily push away their worries about love, family, and finances.

### Shifting Dynamics and New Beginnings

As life progresses, Jaehee eventually moves on to settle down, leaving Young to confront his solitude. He finds himself increasingly responsible for his ailing mother and seeking companionship through a series of romantic encounters. These relationships range from a man whose striking handsomeness is overshadowed by his emotional distance, to another who may represent the potential for true love.

### A Wry Portrait of Modern Life

*Love in the Big City* masterfully captures the glittering allure of Seoul's nightlife and the raw vulnerability of the mornings that follow. Through humor and deep emotion, Sang Young Park delivers a wry and insightful portrait of millennial loneliness, while simultaneously celebrating the abundant joys and resilience found within queer life.

## Imagining 2SQ Futures: Love After the End

*Love After the End* is a Lambda Literary Award-winning fiction anthology that highlights emerging Two-Spirit and queer Indigenous (2SQ) writers from across Turtle Island. These authors create utopian visions, emphasizing the strength and vitality of 2SQ communities and their capacity to thrive despite the persistent impacts of settler colonialism.

The collection features imaginative speculative fiction that centers 2SQ identities. Stories include bio-engineered AI rats, interstellar transplanted trees, and the powerful emergence of a 2SQ resistance camp. The anthology also provides practical advice, such as a guide on how to thrive "Indigiqueerly."

*Love After the End* pushes the boundaries of imagination with narratives involving virtual reality, motherships traversing oceans, and the manipulation of space-time. These stories are queered through the concept of NDN time, offering a unique perspective on temporal and spatial experiences. The anthology embodies the imaginative Two-Spirit futurisms envisioned since 1492.

The collection includes works by acclaimed writers such as Darcie Little Badger, Mari Kurisato, Kai Minosh Pyle, David Alexander Robertson, and jaye simpson. *Love After the End* is dedicated to accessibility, meeting EPUB Accessibility requirements and WCAG-AA guidelines. It is designed for screen-reader compatibility and individuals with disabilities, featuring a clean design with minimal imagery and clear structural markup. The book includes features like image alt text, a table of contents, page-list, landmark navigation, reading order, and semantic structure.

## London's Socialist Legacy: A Blueprint for Progressive Urbanism

While London is often recognized for its global financial standing and its role as a political capital, a significant and often overlooked chapter of its history reveals a different, profoundly progressive identity: that of a pioneering social democratic metropolis. This "third London," shaped by the principles of municipal socialism, introduced groundbreaking initiatives that continue to influence the city today, from its extensive council housing and public enterprises to its commitment to socialist design, radical local democracy, and vibrant multiculturalism.

### The Ascendancy of Municipal Socialism

The foundation for this transformative socialist vision was laid through the burgeoning power of local government. Visionary leaders, including figures like Herbert Morrison and Ken Livingstone, championed socialist ideals that left an indelible mark on London throughout the 20th century, resulting in tangible improvements that benefited all its inhabitants.

### Pillars of Socialist London's Success

*   **Affordable Housing for All:** A central tenet of this era was the unwavering commitment to providing high-quality, affordable housing, ensuring that all Londoners had access to safe and decent homes.
*   **Public Enterprise for Collective Benefit:** Essential public services were established and managed with the primary goal of serving the community, prioritizing public good over private profit motives.
*   **Thoughtful Socialist Design:** The city's public spaces and infrastructure were meticulously planned, emphasizing functionality, accessibility, and an enduring aesthetic quality that enhanced the urban experience.
*   **Empowering Local Democracy:** Communities were actively involved in the governance of their neighborhoods, fostering a culture of robust citizen participation and local decision-making.
*   **Celebrating Multiculturalism:** London embraced and celebrated the rich tapestry of diverse populations that have always been fundamental to its identity and dynamism.

### The Erosion of Municipal Authority

The significant dismantling of this powerful municipal structure in 1986 represented a pivotal shift. Although subsequent Greater London Authorities, under leaders such as Ken Livingstone, Boris Johnson, and Sadiq Khan, have made efforts to address the resulting vacuum, the consequences of this decline in local socialist power remain evident.

### Revitalizing London's Socialist Heritage

This profound socialist heritage offers a potent counter-narrative to the challenges facing contemporary London. It directly challenges the concept of an isolated "metropolitan elite" and makes a compelling argument for embracing London's socialist past with renewed pride. This legacy provides a strong foundation for advocating against policies that may exploit Londoners for the benefit of external interests.

This exploration aims to:

*   **Reframe** London as a city with a deep-rooted social democratic identity.
*   **Highlight** its remarkable achievements in public services and democratic governance.
*   **Examine** the historical forces that contributed to the decline of municipal socialism.
*   **Advocate** for a revitalized socialist vision for London's future, drawing inspiration from its progressive historical achievements.

A thorough understanding of this socialist history is indispensable for effectively envisioning and shaping London's future potential.

Name: The Love Orb The Final Book in the B.E.R. Series & the Dawn of a New Age
Description:
The Love Orb is the authors evolution beyond constitutional government. It draws heavily on the works that came before it and then passes that evolution on to humanity. Thus, it represents the end of the series, but the beginning of an era.

## The Metropolis Case: A Novel of Music, Fate, and Connection

Matthew Gallaway's debut novel, **The Metropolis Case**, offers a sweeping narrative that connects lives across centuries and continents through the enduring power of Richard Wagner's opera, *Tristan and Isolde*. This story, beginning in the vibrant music halls of 1860s Paris and concluding in the soaring skyscrapers of 21st-century New York, follows four individuals whose destinies become intertwined with the opera's remarkable history.

The novel introduces Martin, a successful lawyer navigating post-9/11 Manhattan, who questions his path and seeks a more authentic existence. Decades earlier, Anna, on the cusp of international stardom in the 1960s New York, discovers the unexpected challenges of fame. In 1970s Pittsburgh, Maria, an ostracized teenager with a remarkable operatic voice, finds her escape and a new world of possibilities at Juilliard. Meanwhile, Lucien, a young Parisian at the dawn of the modern era, embarks on a journey of discovery after a tragic event, ultimately learning a vital lesson about life's richness.

**The Metropolis Case** is a grandly operatic exploration of music, magic, love, death, betrayal, and fate. This riveting story promises to captivate readers with its intricate plot and profound themes.

Name: Dark Metropolis
Description:
Sixteen-year-old Thea Holder's mother is cursed with a spell that's driving her mad, and whenever they touch, Thea is chilled by the magic, too. With no one else to contribute, Thea must make a living for both of them in a sinister city, where danger lurks and greed rules. Thea spends her nights waitressing at the decadent Telephone Club attending to the glitzy clientele. But when her best friend, Nan, vanishes, Thea is compelled to find her. She meets Freddy, a young, magnetic patron at the club, and he agrees to help her uncover the city's secrets???even while he hides secrets of his own. Together, they find a whole new side of the city. Unrest is brewing behind closed doors as whispers of a gruesome magic spread. And if they're not careful, the heartless masterminds behind the growing disappearances will be after them, too. Perfect for fans of Cassandra Clare, this is a chilling thriller with a touch of magic where the dead don't always seem to stay that way.

Name: Takedown
Description:
A NYPD detective describes his work as an undercover cop, in which role he infltrated a lethal mob cartel to uncover evidence of a conspiracy among the various mob families to extort billions of dollars from the nation's most influential corporations.

## Organized Crime: A Deep Dive into the Underworld

Organized crime, a shadowy world often glimpsed through popular culture like "The Sopranos," is explored in depth by Lunde. His work delves into the structure and reach of these criminal enterprises, offering a perspective most readers will be glad to observe from a distance.

### Defining Organized Crime

The book begins with a clear definition of organized crime, setting the stage for a comprehensive examination.

### Key Areas of Criminal Activity

Lunde identifies four primary domains where organized crime operates:

*   **Exploiting the Human Condition:** This section likely covers crimes that prey on vulnerable individuals.
*   **Supplying the Illicit:** This area focuses on the distribution of illegal goods and substances.
*   **Extortion and Protection:** This involves demanding payment through threats or offering "protection" services.
*   **Manipulating Money:** This category addresses financial crimes and money laundering.

### Global Crime Syndicates

The majority of the book is dedicated to specific crime groups, categorized by their geographic or cultural origins. These include:

*   The Sicilian Mafia
*   Criminal organizations in Britain
*   Russian organized crime
*   Japanese syndicates (Yakuza)
*   Chinese Triads
*   Crime groups in the U.S.
*   Mexican cartels
*   South American criminal networks

### Visualizing the Underworld

The text is richly complemented by a range of illustrations. These include:

*   Photos of contemporary white-collar criminals like Nick Leeson and Michael Milken.
*   Mug shots of notorious mobsters such as Al Capone.
*   Grim images depicting victims of gangland violence.

**Associate Author:** James Morton (Gangland International) is credited as the associate author.

### An Insider's Perspective

"Organized Crime" examines the characteristics, resources, and strategies of criminal groups worldwide. It also explores the social, political, and economic environments in which they thrive. The book offers a captivating and detailed look at the criminal underworld and its key figures. From iconic figures like Al Capone and Pablo Escobar to less recognized Russian, Chinese, and Southeast Asian crime leaders, this work serves as an insider's guide. It details the origins of these organizations, their internal codes of conduct, their control over illegal markets, and the law enforcement efforts aimed at dismantling them.

Name: Manhattan Mafia Guide
Description:
The New York City historian and author of The Bowery takes readers on a tour of New York’s infamous underworld in this revealing guide. During the early twentieth century, Sicilian and Southern Italian immigrants poured into New York City looking for a better life. But while they escaped the kind of poverty and persecution they experienced in the old country, they soon discovered that certain criminal enterprises followed them to America. Over the years, the island of Manhattan would become a hotbed of organized crime and underworld intrigue. It’s a version of the city that remains invisible to most visitors—until now. In this revealing tour of New York City’s mafia history, Eric Ferrara gives readers an insider’s look at how the mob lived—and where they died. Ferrara goes inside mafia hangouts from the Copacabana to Milady’s Bar and the Thompson Street Social Club. He vividly recounts infamous episodes in the lives of famous mafia men, like Charlie “Lucky” Luciano and Joey Gallo, as well as more obscure players who will be new to most readers. From the beginnings of Black Hand criminal networks to the reign of an all-powerful organized crime syndicate, Manhattan Mafia Guide offers a fascinating look down New York City’s mean streets.

Name: Double Cross
Description:
One of the most feared Chicago mobsters Sam Giancana clawed his way to the top of the Mafia hierarchy by starting as a hit man for Al Capone. He was known as one of the best vehicle escape artists, a tenacious business man, and a ruthless killer. He partied with major stars such as Frank Sinatra and Marilyn Monroe and did business with agents ranging from the CIA to the Vatican to the shah of Iran. When politician Joe Kennedy gave Giancana the chance to use mob muscle to get his son John elected, Giancana jumped at the task. But the Kennedy brothers double-crossed him, waging full-out war on organized crime throughout the United States. And Giancana went after them. Written with suspense and conviction, we learn about how the CIA asked Giancana to assassinate Fidel Castro. The book includes Giancana's testimony about the truth of his involvement in the deaths of Monroe and others, among others. Chuck Giancana, Sam's brother, contributes a unique perspective of the mobs relationship with the Bay of Pigs and many other pivotal events of the 60's and beyond. Double Cross is an eye-opening account of the interworking of the government and the mob and how this relationship has impacted American history.

Name: Five Families
Description:
