Victoria became Queen at just 18. Her private writings document her personal growth and how she navigated scandals and political challenges, highlighting her transformation into an influential monarch.

Authored by historian Helen Rappaport, an experienced historical consultant for the series, *The Victoria Letters* provides rich historical context. The book also features a foreword by Daisy Goodwin, the drama's screenwriter.

*The Victoria Letters* blends striking photography from the television series with authentic Victorian imagery. This visual journey invites readers behind palace walls to uncover the personal story of the girl who became Queen.

Name: Outrageous
Description:
Women empowerment, overcoming adversity, social change, and hope were the cornerstones upon which Victoria Woodhull built her incredible life in Victorian America and Europe. OUTRAGEOUS, Rise to Riches traces Victoria from childhood poverty and horrific abuse to becoming one of the wealthiest women in America.

**Grace Sinclair's Desperate Marriage Race: A Race Against Time and Betrayal**

Grace Sinclair, once cherished, now finds her striking beauty a perilous burden. Taken in by a kind vicar and his wife, her presence inadvertently threatens the vicar's daughters as they prepare for society. Facing ostracization and deemed unwelcome competition, Grace is forced from the only home she's ever known.

A lifeline appears in her estranged Aunt Bell, offering refuge in London. However, this sanctuary quickly reveals itself as a treacherous trap. Upon meeting her aunt's husband, Grace confronts a chilling reality: he is a detestable rake.

### An Unforgiving Ultimatum

Aunt Bell, recognizing the danger Grace faces from her predatory husband, issues a stark ultimatum: Grace has a mere two weeks to secure a husband. Failure means being cast out onto the unforgiving streets.

### The Perilous Pursuit of a Suitor

With no experience in the intricate art of attracting a husband, Grace faces an immense challenge. She quickly realizes that finding a worthy suitor within such a compressed timeframe is far more formidable than anticipated. The clock relentlessly ticks as Grace must navigate a treacherous social landscape, fraught with hidden dangers and societal expectations, to secure her very future.

*   **The ticking clock:** Grace has only two weeks to find a husband.
*   **Lack of experience:** She has no prior knowledge of courtship or attracting a suitor.
*   **Dangerous environment:** Her aunt's home is not a safe haven due to her uncle's predatory nature.
*   **Societal pressures:** Grace must contend with the expectations and complexities of London society.

Name: The Good Life Method
Description:
“At once revolutionary and conservative . . . positively warm, oddly free of moralizing, welcoming of disagreement and engagement.” —Los Angeles Review of Books Two Philosophers Ask and Answer the Big Questions About the Search for Faith and Happiness For seekers of all stripes, philosophy is timeless self-care. University of Notre Dame philosophy professors Meghan Sullivan and Paul Blaschko have shepherded thousands of students on the journey to faith and happiness in their blockbuster undergraduate course God and the Good Life. Now they invite us into their classroom to wrestle with the big questions about how to live and what makes life meaningful. They distill guidance from Aristotle, Plato, Marcus Aurelius, Iris Murdoch, and W. E. B. Du Bois to work through issues like what justifies our beliefs, whether we should practice a religion, and what sacrifices we should make for others. The Good Life Method applies the timeless wisdom of philosophy to real- world case studies that explore love, finance, truth, and more. In so doing, this book pushes us to escape our own caves, ask stronger questions, explain our deepest goals, and wrestle with suffering, the nature of death, and the existence of God.

Name: Pause
Description:
Feeling overwhelmed, burned out, or stuck? Discover the power of the pause. Sometimes life throws you for a loop. You’re stressed out at your job; you’re torn between work and family; your motivation and productivity are taking a nosedive. Your impulse might be to lean in and tough it out, but what you may really need to do is take a step back. Reassess your life with a clear head and dive back in with purpose and poise. In this enlightening book, Rachael O’Meara guides you through the steps of your own pause journey: - The signs that you’re in need of a meaningful break - Planning your optimal pause—whether it’s as short as a day or as long as an epic journey - Reentering the world with renewed clarity and purpose. Incorporating the latest findings from psychology and neuroscience and peppered with inspiring stories of successful pauses, this book will show you that the fastest way to happiness is to slow down. Whether you pause by taking a five-minute walk outside, spending a day unplugged from digital devices, or taking a few weeks off to yourself, Pause will give you the tools to find what “lights you up” and the ability to lead the most satisfying and fulfilling life you choose. As seen in The Washington Post.

Name: Designing Your New Work Life
Description:
From the authors of the #1 New York Times bestseller Designing Your Life comes a revised, fully up-to-date edition of Designing Your New Work Life, a timely, urgently needed book that shows us how to transform our new uncharted work life into a meaningful dream job or company. With practical, useful tools, tips, and design ideas that show us how to navigate disruption (global, regional, or personal) and create new possibilities for our post-COVID work world and beyond. Bill Burnett and Dave Evans successfully taught graduate and undergraduate students at Stanford University and readers of their best-selling book, Designing Your Life ("The prototype for a happy life." —Brian Lehrer, NPR), that designers don't analyze, worry, think, complain their way forward; they build their way forward. And now more than ever, we all need creative and adaptable tools to cope with the chaos caused by COVID-19. In Designing Your New Work Life, Burnett and Evans show us how design thinking can transform our present job, and how it can improve our experience of work in times of disruption. All disruption is personal, write Burnett and Evans, as with the life-altering global pandemic we are living through now. Designing Your New Work Life makes clear that disruption is the new normal, that it is here to stay and that it is accelerating. And in the book's new chapters, Burnett and Evans show us step by step, how to design our way through disruption and how to stay ahead of it—and thrive. Burnett and Evans's Disruption Design offers us a radical new concept that makes use of the designer mindsets: Curiosity, Reframing, Radical collaboration, Awareness, Bias to action, Storytelling, to find our way through these unchartered times. In Designing Your New Work Life, Burnett and Evans show us, with tools, tips, and design ideas, how we can make new possibilities available even when our lives have been disrupted (be it globally, regionally, or personally), giving us the tools to enjoy the present moment and allowing us to begin to prototype our possible future.

Name: Maintaining a Sustainable Work–Life Balance
Description:
This is an open access title available under the terms of a CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 License. It is free to read, download and share on Elgaronline.com. This thought-provoking book provides a detailed exploration of work–life balance, considering the perspectives of specific groups such as parents, academics, the self-employed, and migrants. Moreover, it sheds more light on the dynamics of self-care, childcare as well as informal care. Collaborative and interdisciplinary in its approach, featuring researchers ranging from quantitative to interpretative scholars, it highlights the importance of a sustainable work–life balance and the instruments needed to improve this.

## Work-Life Matters: Redefining Balance for a Fulfilling Existence

The concept of work-life balance is often misunderstood. It's not simply about how you divide your time between professional and personal activities. Instead, true balance hinges on how you utilize and replenish your energy across all aspects of your life. Both your work and your personal life are significant, and their integration is what truly matters.

As we adapt to life in a post-pandemic world, both employers and employees are actively rethinking how to make work more sustainable and rewarding. A growing number of employees, especially those from Gen X and Gen Z, are seeking a revised psychological agreement with their employers. This book explores these evolving trends and offers practical strategies, delving into why work is an integral component of a healthy and fulfilling life.

### Challenging the Dichotomy of Work and Life

The authors propose a novel perspective on achieving balance. They argue that a strict division between "work" and "life" is artificial. Since work inherently exists within the broader context of life, attempting to balance two separate entities when one is a subset of the other is an ineffective approach.

### Understanding Energy Dynamics

To cultivate a balanced and healthy existence, it's crucial to recognize that different activities within your work-life spectrum have varying impacts on your energy levels. Some activities deplete your energy, while others invigorate you.

Instead of attempting to counteract the draining effects of demanding work by overindulging during weekends, or compensating for an unfulfilling personal life by working excessively, a more effective strategy is to foster balance both within your professional sphere and in your personal life.

### Seizing the Opportunity for Change

The current era presents a prime opportunity to re-evaluate the professional landscape and to actively "job-craft." This involves making work more:

*   **Satisfying:** Finding greater fulfillment in your daily tasks.
*   **Less Draining:** Minimizing activities that deplete your energy.
*   **More Energizing:** Incorporating elements that boost your vitality.

The insights presented in this book serve as a practical roadmap to guide you through this transformative process.

Name: Work / Life Balance For Dummies
Description:
Find out what you really need to live, thrive and survive Does it seem like the constant struggle to succeed at work is overshadowing other areas of your life? Are your relationships suffering as you try to cram everything into a normal day? Work/Life Balance For Dummies will help you get your equilibrium back, taking you through all available solutions -- from changes you can adopt within your existing workplace or chosen career, to career changes, treechanges and seachanges. Discover how to: Work out your priorities Put off procrastination and improve your time management Move your boss towards work/life balance Cast your net wider and change jobs and employers Plan your seachange or treechange

Name: Off Balance
Description:
Argues that people require satisfaction more than a work-life balance, outlining a system used by the author to promote energy, focus on key priorities, and avoid popular beliefs that compromise personal fulfillment.

Name: Work/life Balance
Description:
Giving human resource professionals historical perspective, data, and possible solutions to the challenging balance of work and life, this resource provides options to positively impact the bottom line of their companies, improve employee morale, retain employees with valuable company knowledge, and keep pace with workplace trends. This critical perspective demonstrates how global competition, personal life, and an aging workforce are factors that can be utilized to gain a competitive advantage in the marketplace through work/life initiatives.

Name: The Work-Life Balance Myth: Rethinking Your Optimal Balance for Success
Description:
An empowering guide that will show you how to shed the myth of the “work-life balance” by merging the seven key components that make up your life to attain harmony and whole-life success “All of us have Seven Slices in our lives: our Family Slice, our Professional Slice, our Personal Slice, our Physical Slice, our Intellectual Slice, our Emotional Slice, and our Spiritual Slice. These all need to be served in some fashion—and in serving them, they, in turn, serve us.” This relatively simple but profoundly critical concept is at the heart of the method executive coach David McNeff has used to transform the lives and careers of his clients. It begins with two important facts: 1) stress happens—you can’t avoid it; and 2) your existence is composed of far more than “work” and “life.” Too often, we divide our lives into those two general categories, but we’re all a lot more complex and our lives are richer than that. By being clear and mindful of all aspects of your life—the Seven Slices—you’ll be more likely to find inner harmony when stress impacts one of them. In The Work-Life Balance Myth, McNeff takes you on a deep dive into each of the Seven Slices, explaining the components of each Slice, signs that you may not be attending to each Slice in a healthy way, and hands-on methods for accessing an underserved Slice. The Work-Life Balance Myth won’t make your life perfect—no one can do that, and you shouldn’t trust anyone who makes that promise. What this book will do is provide you with proven new ways of framing your life, seeing stress for what it is, and vastly improving your ability to navigate the emotional challenges that will inevitably arise in a way that serves your Seven Slices.

**Navigating the Demands of Work and Home Life**

In today's fast-paced world, the lines between our professional and personal lives are increasingly blurred. The constant pressure of time and the ability to work anytime, anywhere, thanks to new technology, make it challenging for many to achieve a healthy integration of work and home. This can lead to significant conflict, impacting not only the individual but also those closest to them.

**Understanding Work-Life Conflict**

The struggle to balance work and home responsibilities is a growing concern. This conflict can manifest in various ways, affecting our well-being and relationships.

*   **Impact on Personal Life:** Work-related stress can spill over into home life, affecting partners and family members.
*   **Time Pressures:** Modern work environments often demand more time, making it difficult to dedicate sufficient attention to personal commitments.
*   **Technological Integration:** While technology offers flexibility, it can also tether us to work outside of traditional hours.

**Seeking Effective Solutions**

To address these challenges, flexible and innovative approaches are crucial for managing the interface between work and home. "Work-Life Balance: A Psychological Perspective" offers a comprehensive look at these issues, drawing on the latest research and providing practical solutions.

**Key Areas Explored in the Book:**

*   **Psychological Dimensions:** The book delves into the psychological aspects of work-home conflict, including:
    *   The inherent conflict between work and family roles.
    *   How work stress impacts partners and family dynamics.
    *   Strategies for recovery from work-related exhaustion.
*   **Legal Considerations:** It also examines relevant legal issues surrounding work-life balance.
*   **Employer Initiatives:** Examples of successful programs implemented by leading employers are showcased.

**An Essential Resource**

Featuring contributions from leading international researchers, this book provides an in-depth psychological perspective on work-home issues. Its practical focus and innovative approach make it an indispensable guide for:

*   Managers
*   HR Professionals
*   Organizational Psychologists
*   Students in related fields

For those engaged in research on workplace issues, the book's strong theoretical foundation and research focus will prove invaluable.

## No-Drama Leadership: Making Better Decisions in a High-Pressure World

In today's fast-paced business environment, leaders are constantly bombarded with choices, power, and speed. This can lead to decisions made in the heat of the moment, often fueled by emotion and drama. Hasty decisions can have serious consequences, especially in our transparent culture where privacy is limited and problems can't be ignored. Leaders' decision-making is more exposed than ever, making it crucial to get it right.

Marlene Chism's "No-Drama Leadership" offers a vital model for the corporate world. Through case studies, checklists, and examples from diverse industries and leadership levels, Chism presents the mindset shifts and practical skills necessary to cultivate enlightened leaders. These leaders make decisions from a more grounded and aligned perspective.

### Key Benefits of No-Drama Leadership:

*   **Identify Misalignment:** Learn to spot the early signs that indicate a lack of alignment within your team or organization.
*   **Boost Effectiveness:** Discover strategies to significantly increase your overall leadership effectiveness.
*   **Leverage Change:** Utilize the four quadrants of change as a powerful tool for leadership growth.
*   **Enhance Engagement:** Foster a more engaged workforce by understanding and implementing key principles.
*   **Unlock Potential:** Tap into the unique gifts and talents of your employees to drive success.
*   **Strategic Communication:** Develop the ability to communicate in a more strategic and impactful way.
*   **Build Accountability:** Create a culture where accountability is a shared value and practice.
*   **Drive Innovation:** Empower your team to increase innovation and productivity.

### Beyond Traditional Leadership Skills

Today's leaders require more than just their position, power, or business expertise. They need more than self-management, communication skills, or emotional intelligence alone. The modern leader must be:

*   **Aligned:** Working in harmony with organizational goals and values.
*   **Aware:** Possessing a deep understanding of themselves and their environment.
*   **Accountable:** Taking responsibility for their actions and decisions.

Effective leaders balance choice and power with wisdom and responsibility. They integrate the internal growth of leadership with the external achievement of business results. By embodying these mindsets and actions, leaders can transform the cultures they lead, creating environments free from unnecessary drama and focused on sustainable success.

Name: Car
Description:
This astonishing journey into the belly of one of our most important industries, a portrait of the energy and ingenuity of America at work, follows the 1996 Ford Taurus from its conception to its public debut.

## Mastering Workplace Appreciation: A Guide to the 5 Languages

In today's fast-paced professional world, cultivating a workplace where employees feel genuinely valued is no longer a luxury, but a necessity for sustained success. Drawing inspiration from the widely recognized *5 Love Languages®* framework, the book *The 5 Languages of Appreciation in the Workplace* presents a powerful and practical methodology. This approach is designed to significantly improve interpersonal dynamics, elevate employee morale, and ultimately decrease staff turnover by ensuring that every individual's contributions are recognized and celebrated.

### The Foundation of a Thriving Work Environment

At its core, a truly engaging workplace hinges on a simple yet profound question: **Do people feel their efforts are seen and appreciated?** This essential guide offers a clear and actionable framework for effectively conveying appreciation and encouragement to everyone you collaborate with, from direct reports and peers to senior leadership.

### A Proven Framework for Diverse Settings

With an impressive track record of over 600,000 copies sold and translations into 24 languages, *The 5 Languages of Appreciation in the Workplace* has demonstrated its universal applicability and effectiveness across a wide array of organizational structures. Its transformative insights have been successfully implemented in numerous sectors, including:

*   **Corporate Environments:** Fostering stronger team bonds and enhancing overall productivity.
*   **Non-profit Organizations:** Bolstering commitment among both volunteers and dedicated staff.
*   **Healthcare Facilities:** Improving the quality of patient care through enhanced staff well-being and job satisfaction.
*   **Educational Institutions:** Creating a more supportive and encouraging atmosphere for educators and students alike.
*   **Government Agencies:** Significantly boosting employee morale and improving operational efficiency.
*   **Remote and Hybrid Teams:** Effectively bridging geographical distances and fostering meaningful connections.

### Discovering Individual Appreciation Preferences

Each new purchase of the book includes a complimentary access code to the online **Motivating By Appreciation (MBA) Inventory**. This indispensable assessment tool is specifically designed to:

*   **Identify an individual's primary and secondary appreciation languages.**
*   **Uncover the specific actions that hold the most personal meaning for them.**

By understanding and actively applying these distinct appreciation languages, leaders and colleagues can communicate in ways that truly resonate, leading to deeper professional relationships, a more positive organizational culture, and a tangible improvement in team performance.

*(For any assistance with your access code, please reach out to mpcustomerservice@moody.edu.)*

Elevate your team's experience and transform your workplace by embracing the impactful principles outlined in *The 5 Languages of Appreciation in the Workplace*.

## End Team Drama and Foster Collaboration

Are you a leader whose valuable time, attention, and energy are constantly consumed by team drama? Gossip, infighting, complaints, and cliques can derail even the most promising teams. This pervasive issue often arises when team members lack the necessary tools and support to navigate stress, selfishness, and disagreements effectively.

### The Devastating Impact of Team Drama

Team drama wreaks havoc on organizational health, leading to a cascade of negative consequences:

*   **Damaged Morale:** A toxic environment erodes employee spirit and enthusiasm.
*   **Reduced Productivity:** Conflicts and distractions hinder efficient work.
*   **Lowered Engagement:** Employees disengage when they feel unsupported or stressed.
*   **Decreased Retention:** High drama environments drive valuable talent away.
*   **Customer Dissatisfaction:** Internal issues often spill over into external interactions.
*   **Lost Revenue:** Productivity and customer issues directly impact the bottom line.
*   **Managerial Burnout:** Leaders are drained, their spirits crushed by constant conflict resolution.

Given these severe repercussions, leaders at all levels and across all industries have a critical duty to cultivate team unity and proactively prevent team drama.

### A Practical Guide to Building Unified Teams

"No More Team Drama" offers a powerful solution by expertly blending research from psychology, organizational development, and employee engagement with the author's extensive practical experience. Joe Mull, author of "Cure for the Common Leader," provides an indispensable manual designed to enhance the quality of workplace interactions.

This book guides you through the process of transforming a group of disparate employees into a cohesive unit of collaborators. You'll learn how to foster an environment where team members are:

*   Committed to working hard.
*   Able to get along harmoniously.
*   Driven to exceed customer expectations.

In essence, "No More Team Drama" is your roadmap to achieving a workplace free from disruptive conflict.

### Expert Endorsements

The effectiveness of "No More Team Drama" is highlighted by glowing reviews from industry leaders:

*   **Suzanne Malausky, Director of Culture and Talent Management, MedExpress, an Optum Company,** states: "Yes, yes, yes! Joe has smartly and frankly addressed behaviors that suck the energy out of so many at work, providing us all credible, doable fixes. 'No More Team Drama' should be required for anyone serious about creating healthy company cultures!"
*   **Vicki Hess, Author, "Shift to Professional Paradise" and "6 Shortcuts to Employee Engagement,"** describes the book as: "Using proven ideas and presented in a real-world, read-it-and-use-it format, 'No More Team Drama' is a treasure trove of resources and expert ideas to improve employee engagement, customer experience, and co-worker connections."
*   **Skip Weisman, Author, "Overcoming The 7 Deadliest Communication SINs: A New Standard for Workplace Communication,"** shares his perspective from leading professional baseball organizations: "I can attest that this book explains simply how to develop a workforce that models the best, most successful teams in sports. Joe mixes Gladwell-like stories and research with real-world experiences, so that you learn how to develop workplace teams that get along, while getting the job done."

Name: Reality-Based Leadership
Description:
Leadership strategies grounded in reality and focused on results Recent polls show that 70% of workers think about quitting their jobs every day. That number would be shocking—if people actually were quitting. Worse, they go to work, punching time clocks and collecting pay checks, while completely checked out emotionally. In Reality-Based Leadership, bestselling author and leadership expert Cy Wakeman reveals how to be the kind of leader who changes the way people think about and perceive their circumstances. This no-nonsense guide shows you how to deal with the facts, give helpful and clear feedback to employees, and kindly help others focus their effort on their personal contribution, not the rampant workplace drama. Filled with dynamic examples, innovative tools, and diagnostic tests, Reality-Based Leadership shows you how to become a Reality-Based Leader: Uncover destructive thought patterns within yourself and others Diffuse drama and lead the person in front of you Empower others to focus on facts and think for themselves Stop managing and start leading If you're ready to lead more and manage chaos less, Reality-Based Leadership will empower you to do so!

Name: Drama: A Graphic Novel
Description:
From Raina Telgemeier, the #1 New York Times bestselling, multiple Eisner Award-winning author of Smile and Sisters! Callie loves theater. And while she would totally try out for her middle school's production of Moon over Mississippi, she can't really sing. Instead she's the set designer for the drama department's stage crew, and this year she's determined to create a set worthy of Broadway on a middle-school budget. But how can she, when she doesn't know much about carpentry, ticket sales are down, and the crew members are having trouble working together? Not to mention the onstage AND offstage drama that occurs once the actors are chosen. And when two cute brothers enter the picture, things get even crazier!

Name: Am I The Only Sane One Working Here?: 101 Solutions for Surviving Office Insanity
Description:
". . . the most comprehensive guide I have ever read for solving any conceivable trying scenario!" --Julie Jansen, bestselling author of You Want Me to Work with Who? and I Don't Know What I Want, but I Know It's Not This You can't stop office madness, but you can stop the madness from getting to you. Gossipy coworkers, unmanageable managers, and cranky clients have got you pulling your hair out and gnawing your nails down to nubs. From teammates who drop the ball on deadlines to corporate bullies who try to run your show, your work environment can be lethal to your health and your career. Change Your Reaction, Not Their Actions When things get crazy, you may not be able to control how others behave, but you can change how you respond. Al Bernstein shows you how understand the situation, how to keep the craziness from bothering you, how to keep things from getting worse, and how you can make them better. Problems/solutions discussed inside: Coworkers who don't like you? Feed them! Hidden agendas? Unleash the power of “cc:” mail! Unpleasant supervisors? Tell them only what they want to hear! Office gossip? Dish out positive gossip about other people! Lying coworkers? Buy into their lies and watch what happens! And ninety-five more!

**The Local: A Small-Town Attorney's High-Stakes Murder Trial**

In the heart of Marshall, Texas, a town famous for its swift patent trials and substantial settlements, a local attorney finds himself thrust into a national murder case. James Euchre, a respected lawyer in this intellectual property hub, takes on a new client, Amir Zawar, a CEO facing a patent infringement lawsuit.

However, their legal battle takes a dramatic turn when a prominent local figure is murdered. All evidence seems to implicate Zawar, an outsider with no verifiable alibi.

Euchre, embarking on his first criminal case, must navigate the complexities of the investigation. With the assistance of a former federal prosecutor and a private investigator, he races against time to uncover the truth. The stakes are incredibly high: Euchre fears either an innocent man will be wrongly convicted and face the death penalty, or he might inadvertently help a guilty party go free.

This gripping courtroom thriller, "The Local," delivers intense suspense right up to the final verdict. As Michelle King, co-creator of "The Good Wife," "The Good Fight," and "Evil," describes it, it's "A spectacular courtroom thriller that kept me turning pages like the best of Grisham or Turow."

**Unlock the Power of Drama for Language Learning with "The Drama Book"**

Are you looking for an engaging and effective way to enhance language learning in your classroom? "The Drama Book" is your ultimate resource, offering a comprehensive and user-friendly guide to integrating theater into your curriculum. This invaluable teacher's companion provides a wealth of practical activities and ready-to-use lesson plans designed to make language acquisition dynamic and fun.

**Part 1: Building Skills Through Dramatic Play**

The first section of "The Drama Book" is dedicated to building essential communication skills through a variety of theatrical approaches. You'll discover engaging pragmatics mini-lessons, community-building exercises, and energizing drama games that foster collaboration and confidence. Dedicated pronunciation activities help students refine their spoken English, while practical lesson plans guide you through producing a play, whether a full-stage production or a Reader's Theater performance.

Furthermore, this section offers creative guidelines and activities for students to write their own plays, either independently or with your guidance. It also provides valuable suggestions for seamlessly integrating academic content into dramatic activities, ensuring a holistic learning experience. To support assessment, you'll find ready-to-use rubrics and evaluation schemes for providing constructive feedback on student performance.

**Part 2: Ready-to-Use Scripts for Immediate Impact**

The second part of "The Drama Book" features ten original monologues and scripts, conveniently formatted for immediate photocopying and classroom use. These pieces are specifically crafted to showcase everyday language and high-frequency social interactions, making them highly relevant to learners. Each script features engaging plotlines where characters encounter challenges and work towards achieving their goals, providing authentic contexts for practicing language skills. With a foreword by Ken Wilson, this book is an indispensable tool for educators seeking to leverage the performing arts to cultivate more confident and fluent speakers.

Name: Beautiful Bastard
Description:
An ambitious intern. A perfectionist executive. And a whole lot of name calling. Discover the story that garnered more than two million reads online. Whip-smart, hardworking, and on her way to an MBA, Chloe Mills has only one problem: her boss, Bennett Ryan. He’s exacting, blunt, inconsiderate—and completely irresistible. A Beautiful Bastard. Bennett has returned to Chicago from France to take a vital role in his family’s massive media business. He never expected that the assistant who’d been helping him from abroad was the gorgeous, innocently provocative—completely infuriating—creature he now has to see every day. Despite the rumors, he’s never been one for a workplace hookup. But Chloe’s so tempting he’s willing to bend the rules—or outright smash them—if it means he can have her. All over the office. As their appetites for one another increase to a breaking point, Bennett and Chloe must decide exactly what they’re willing to lose in order to win each other. Originally only available online as The Office by tby789—and garnering over 2 million reads on fanfiction sites—Beautiful Bastard has been extensively updated for re-release.

To create memorable and relatable characters, writers need to explore their depths. While detailed descriptions can sometimes slow down a narrative, a character's occupation offers a powerful shortcut to revealing their personality, skills, passions, and motivations. A chosen career can even hint at past traumas, fears, or a character's attempts to escape or atone for their history. Selecting the right job for your character can significantly enrich your storytelling.

*The Occupation Thesaurus: A Writer's Guide to Jobs, Vocations, and Careers* is designed to help you harness the storytelling potential of occupations. This comprehensive guide provides informative profiles on a wide range of popular and unusual jobs, enabling you to write about them with confidence and authority.

Inside, you'll discover:

*   **Believable Conflict Scenarios:** Each occupation comes with suggested conflict scenarios, offering endless opportunities to inject tension at both the story and scene levels.
*   **Stereotype Twists:** Learn how to subvert common stereotypes associated with various professions, adding unique dimensions to your characters.
*   **Characterization Tools:** Gain instruction on effectively using occupations to build character, support your story's structure, reinforce themes, and much more.
*   **Psychological Insights:** Explore how emotional wounds and fundamental human needs can influence a character's career choices.
*   **Brainstorming Assistance:** Utilize a dedicated tool to organize your character's personality traits, helping you pinpoint the most fitting occupations.

Choosing a profession for your character should offer more than just a source of income. With over 120 entries presented in an easy-to-use format, *The Occupation Thesaurus* acts as a comprehensive job fair for writers, providing a wealth of inspiration to bring your characters to life.

Name: Pure Sacrifice
Description:
To save his race, he must keep the chosen virgin pure. But she has other plans… A shapeshifting unicorn desperate to save his race... The last guardian of his kind, Markos Ambrostead must keep the chosen Virgin hidden and untainted. But when an attacker breaches his protective magic, he’s forced to reveal himself to defend her life. A tenacious woman who refuses to be ignored... Celia Hawkins wishes the world would get a clue and stop treating her like she’s invisible. Only one man notices her, or is that her imagination? After narrowly escaping an attempted rape, she demands answers from her mysterious rescuer—starting with why he’s been following her. Rules were made to be broken... Markos can’t risk being tempted by the Virgin, yet emboldened by his attention, Celia’s determined to become his friend. Maybe more. Maybe much more. Now he must hold onto his crumbling willpower to maintain her purity—or his tribe will become extinct. ***** Note: For adult readers--contains hot sex scenes and edgy situations. For an introduction to the Mythos Legacy world, check out the free short story Unintended Guardian! Tags: shapeshifter romance books, contemporary fantasy romance, unicorn shifter romance, virgin hero romance, strong female character lead, non-human paranormal fantasy romance, virgin, unicorn, myth, legend, ritual, spell, magic

**Faker: A Rom-Com Where Office Rivals Discover Genuine Affection**

Sarah Smith's debut novel, "Faker," emerges as a highly anticipated fall release, promising a delightful blend of fun and sensuality within the romantic comedy genre. The narrative intricately weaves the story of Emmie Echavarre, a skilled professional who navigates the predominantly male environment of Nuts & Bolts, a prominent power tool company. To thrive amidst the rugged and often gruff men, Emmie cultivates a tough, no-nonsense persona during her workday, a deliberate departure from her inherently easy-going nature.

### The Genesis of an Office Feud

Emmie's profound dislike for her colleague, Tate Rasmussen, is one emotion she genuinely experiences. Tate has harbored hostility towards her from their very first encounter, consistently responding to her friendly overtures and attempts at connection with dismissive scowls and curt replies. Despite his consistently unwelcoming demeanor, Emmie finds herself increasingly captivated by his imposing physique, particularly his remarkably muscular, Thor-like biceps.

### Proximity Breeds Unexpected Chemistry

The established dynamic between Emmie and Tate undergoes a significant transformation when they are unexpectedly assigned to collaborate on a charitable construction project. This mandated partnership ignites unforeseen sparks and cultivates a palpable, heated atmosphere between them. As their shared time together increases, Emmie begins to perceive beyond Tate's chiseled exterior, uncovering a surprisingly tender heart concealed beneath.

### Forging Trust and Cultivating True Love

Nevertheless, the process of overcoming their deeply ingrained animosity presents a considerable challenge. It will require more than a few conciliatory words to effectively erase their history of hostility and to genuinely convince Emmie that their developing connection is authentic. "Faker" delves into the compelling journey of two office rivals who must confront their entrenched differences and endeavor to build a love that possesses true and lasting substance.

**The Emotion Thesaurus: A Writer's Guide to Character Expression (2nd Edition)**

Writers often struggle to portray emotions authentically. This guide offers a solution. The Emotion Thesaurus, a beloved resource for authors, now features 56 new entries. It's designed to help you move beyond common expressions like smiling, nodding, and frowning.

### Showing, Not Telling Emotions

This thesaurus is your ultimate guide to "showing" your characters' feelings. It provides inspiration for unique and evocative emotional responses.

### What's Inside

*   **Body Language and Reactions:** Discover body language cues, thoughts, and visceral responses for over 130 emotions. These range from mild to severe, offering countless ways to personalize your characters' reactions.
*   **Common Writing Pitfalls:** Learn about frequent emotion-related writing problems and how to solve them.
*   **Pre-Drafting Strategies:** Get advice on preparing before you write to ensure your characters' emotions are realistic and consistent.
*   **Hidden Feelings:** Understand how to reveal concealed emotions and subtext through dialogue and nonverbal cues.
*   **And More:** Explore additional techniques to enrich your character development.

### Enhance Your Writing

The Emotion Thesaurus's clear, list-based format will spark creativity. You'll craft stronger, fresher character expressions that captivate readers from the very beginning.

Navigating the transition from peer to leader is a significant career milestone, especially as the landscape of management is set to evolve. With a wave of Baby Boomers retiring in the coming decade, the demand for new supervisors is expected to rise. This shift presents a unique set of challenges, perhaps more so for those stepping into their very first leadership role. It's a period marked by change and uncertainty, demanding a new skill set and a fundamental redefinition of one's relationship with former colleagues.

This comprehensive guide, "From Bud to Boss," is specifically designed to address the distinct hurdles faced by individuals embarking on their leadership journey. While its principles are applicable to any manager, supervisor, or leader, the book hones in on the specific difficulties encountered by new leaders. Drawing upon established leadership and management theories, it offers practical strategies and insights tailored to this critical phase. A key focus is placed on the delicate situation of supervising friends and former peers, a common scenario for many emerging leaders.

Authored by seasoned consultants with extensive experience guiding leaders at all organizational levels, "From Bud to Boss" provides actionable advice for cultivating a leadership mindset. It delves into essential areas such as effectively communicating change, delivering constructive feedback, coaching employees for growth, fostering productive team dynamics, and ultimately, achieving organizational goals. This invaluable resource aims to empower new leaders to move past the initial stress and apprehension, enabling them to embrace their new role and become the most effective leader they can be, starting immediately.

## Savage Love: A Steamy Romance of Rivalry and Desire

This is a standalone steamy romance that explores the intense rivalry and undeniable attraction between two lifelong enemies.

### A History of Hate

From the tender age of five, a bitter feud has defined the relationship between the narrator and Carter. It all began with a childhood prank: Carter cutting the narrator's pigtails, leading to a sharpie-fueled retaliation. This early conflict ignited a passion for animosity that only intensified with age.

### Escalating Animosity

As they grew older, their methods of torment became increasingly "savage." Their rivalry escalated beyond childish pranks to:

*   Physical altercations resulting in broken bones.
*   Sabotage of each other's romantic pursuits.
*   Deliberate destruction of opportunities for happiness.

### An Unexpected Reunion

After college, the narrator believed they had escaped Carter's influence forever. However, fate intervened when a high-profile PR job opportunity arose. Driven by ambition and the chance to finally achieve their dream career, the narrator eagerly pursued it.

### The Ultimate Competition

The catch? Carter is also vying for the same prestigious position. The narrator is determined to defeat him, even if it means resorting to "savage" tactics to secure the win.

### A Complicating Desire

Despite the deep-seated hatred and the history of betrayal, a powerful and inconvenient truth emerges: the narrator's body betrays them whenever Carter is near. The animosity doesn't diminish the intense, "naughty, unthinkable" desires they harbor for him.

The central question remains: can the narrator's "savage heart" overcome the undeniable allure of Carter's charms and secure their dream job?

Name: The Invitation
Description:
The Hating Game meets One Plus One by JoJo Moyes in The Invitation, a slow burn, heated, emotional standalone from #1 New York Times bestseller, Vi Keeland. The first time I met Hudson Rothschild was at a wedding. I’d received an unexpected invitation to one of the swankiest venues in the city. Hudson was a groomsman and quite possibly the most gorgeous man I’d ever laid eyes on. He asked me to dance, and our chemistry was off the charts. I knew it wasn’t a good idea to get involved with him, considering the wedding I was at. But our connection was intense, and I was having a great time. Though the fun came to a screeching halt when Hudson figured out I wasn’t who I’d said I was. You see, that unexpected invitation I received? Well, it hadn’t actually been addressed to me—it was sent to my ex-roommate who’d bounced a check for two months’ rent and moved out in the middle of the night. I figured she owed me an expensive night out, but I guess, technically, I was crashing the wedding. Once caught, I couldn’t get out of there fast enough. As I bolted for the door, I might’ve plucked a few bottles of expensive champagne off the tables I passed, all while the gorgeous, angry groomsman was hot on my tail. Outside, I jumped into a taxi. My heart ricocheted against my ribs as we drove down the block—but at least I’d escaped unscathed. Or so I thought. Until I realized I’d left my cell phone behind at the table. Take one guess who found it? This is the crazy story of how Hudson Rothschild and I met. But trust me, it’s only the tip of the iceberg. At the Publisher's request, this title is being sold without Digital Rights Management Software (DRM) applied.

Name: Work and Play: A workplace, grumpy sunshine, forced proximity, small town romance
Description:
I’m stuck sharing a house with my hot new boss…And I just walked in on him in the shower. Landing a major interior design contract for the new winery in Dogwood Cove is supposed to be my fresh start. But sharing a house with the annoyingly sexy winemaker Finn McNeil? That wasn’t part of the plan… To make it worse, Finn’s made it very clear he doesn’t want me around - at work, or anywhere else. I’m determined to win him over with my charm and professionalism. Not only do I need this job, but this town has become more than a place to earn a living, it’s become home. Which is why giving into the undeniable attraction we feel could spell disaster for both of us. Work and Play is the ultimate steamy small town romance for fans of forced proximity, a quirky heroine who knows what she wants, and a winemaker who’s determined to avoid her.This book is for readers who love grumpy-sunshine banter, a dirty talking hero, a close friend group, low angst and high heat with a guaranteed HEA. This is the third book in the Dogwood Cove series and can be read as a standalone, although the series is best enjoyed if read in order.

Name: From the Mixed-Up Files of Mrs. Basil E. Frankweiler
Description:
Now available in a deluxe keepsake edition! A Time Best YA Book of All Time (2021) Run away to the Metropolitan Museum of Art with E. L. Konigsburg’s beloved classic and Newbery Medal­–winning novel From the Mixed-Up Files of Mrs. Basil E. Frankweiler. When Claudia decided to run away, she planned very carefully. She would be gone just long enough to teach her parents a lesson in Claudia appreciation. And she would go in comfort-she would live at the Metropolitan Museum of Art. She saved her money, and she invited her brother Jamie to go, mostly because be was a miser and would have money. Claudia was a good organizer and Jamie bad some ideas, too; so the two took up residence at the museum right on schedule. But once the fun of settling in was over, Claudia had two unexpected problems: She felt just the same, and she wanted to feel different; and she found a statue at the Museum so beautiful she could not go home until she bad discovered its maker, a question that baffled the experts, too. The former owner of the statue was Mrs. Basil E. Frankweiler. Without her—well, without her, Claudia might never have found a way to go home.

Name: The Guggenheim Mystery
Description:
My name is Ted Spark. I am 12 years and 281 days old. I have seven friends. Three months ago, I solved the mystery of how my cousin Salim disappeared from a pod on the London Eye. This is the story of my second mystery. This summer, I went on holiday to New York, to visit Aunt Gloria and Salim. While I was there, a painting was stolen from the Guggenheim Museum, where Aunt Gloria works. Everyone was very worried and upset. I did not see what the problem was. I do not see the point of paintings, even if they are worth £9.8 million. Perhaps that's because of my very unusual brain, which works on a different operating system to everyone else's. But then Aunt Gloria was blamed for the theft - and Aunt Gloria is family. And I realised just how important it was to find the painting, and discover who really had taken it.

## I Know What You Did Last Summer: A Deadly Secret Unearths Terror

Four friends found their lives irrevocably altered by a tragic accident last summer. Bound by a desperate pact, they concealed a shocking secret, believing it buried forever. However, their carefully constructed facade crumbles as an unknown avenger uncovers the truth, unleashing a terrifying new wave of horror. This relentless stalker is engaged in a deadly game, leaving the friends to question whether their tormentor seeks mere terror or outright revenge. As this summer unfolds, these four friends will confront the chilling reality that some secrets are destined to resurface.

Name: Deductive Detective, The
Description:
Someone stole a cake from the cake contest—who could it be? Twelve animal bakers are potential suspects but Detective Duck uses his deductive reasoning skills to “quack” the case. After all, the thief left hairs behind so the thief wasn’t a bird. Follow along as he subtracts each suspect one at a time to reveal just who the culprit was. This clever story will have children of all ages giggling at the puns and the play on words.

Name: First Class Murder
Description:
A murdered heiress, a missing necklace, and a train full of shifty, unusual, and suspicious characters leaves Daisy and Hazel with a new mystery to solve in this third novel of the Wells & Wong Mystery series. Hazel Wong and Daisy Wells are taking a vacation across Europe on world-famous passenger train, the Orient Express—and it’s clear that each of their fellow first-class travelers has something to hide. Even more intriguing: There’s rumor of a spy in their midst. Then, during dinner, a bloodcurdling scream comes from inside one of the cabins. When the door is broken down, a passenger is found murdered—her stunning ruby necklace gone. But the killer has vanished, as if into thin air. The Wells & Wong Detective Society is ready to crack the case—but this time, they’ve got competition.

Name: The Box in the Woods
Description:
After solving the case of Truly Devious, Stevie Bell investigates her first mystery outside of Ellingham Academy in this spine-chilling and hilarious stand-alone mystery from New York Times bestselling author Maureen Johnson. Amateur sleuth Stevie Bell needs a good murder. After catching a killer at her high school, she’s back at home for a normal (that means boring) summer. But then she gets a message from the owner of Sunny Pines, formerly known as Camp Wonder Falls—the site of the notorious unsolved case, the Box in the Woods Murders. Back in 1978, four camp counselors were killed in the woods outside of the town of Barlow Corners, their bodies left in a gruesome display. The new owner offers Stevie an invitation: Come to the camp and help him work on a true crime podcast about the case. Stevie agrees, as long as she can bring along her friends from Ellingham Academy. Nothing sounds better than a summer spent together, investigating old murders. But something evil still lurks in Barlow Corners. When Stevie opens the lid on this long-dormant case, she gets much more than she bargained for. The Box in the Woods will make room for more victims. This time, Stevie may not make it out alive. * Cosmopolitan Best YA Books of 2021 * People Magazine Best Books of Summer 2021*

Name: The Mangle Street Murders
Description:
The first in a charming, evocative, and sharply plotted Victorian crime series starring a detective duo to rival Holmes and Watson. After her father dies, March Middleton has to move to London to live with her guardian, Sidney Grice, the country’s most famous private detective. It is 1882 and London is at its murkiest yet most vibrant, wealthiest yet most poverty-stricken. No sooner does March arrive than a case presents itself: a young woman has been brutally murdered, and her husband is the only suspect. The victim’s mother is convinced of her son-in-law’s innocence, and March is so touched by her pleas she offers to cover Sidney’s fee herself. The investigations lead the pair to the darkest alleys of the East End: every twist leads Sidney Grice to think his client is guilty; but March is convinced that he is innocent. Around them London reeks with the stench of poverty and gossip, the case threatens to boil over into civil unrest and Sidney Grice finds his reputation is not the only thing in mortal danger.

Name: The Mystery of the Missing Man
Description:
DigiCat Publishing presents to you this special edition of "The Mystery of the Missing Man" by Enid Blyton. DigiCat Publishing considers every written word to be a legacy of humankind. Every DigiCat book has been carefully reproduced for republishing in a new modern format. The books are available in print, as well as ebooks. DigiCat hopes you will treat this work with the acknowledgment and passion it deserves as a classic of world literature.

Name: Wicked Will
Description:
THE SHAPE WAS A MAN. WILL GRASPED HIS LEFT ARM AND TRIED TO HAUL HIM UP ONTO THE RIVERBANK. THE BALD HEAD, THROWN BACK, TRAILED GRAY, WISPY STRAGGLES OF HAIR. THE DEAD MOUTH HUNG OPEN, A BLACK GAP IN THE PALE BLUR OF FACE, AND THE EYES SEEMED TO BE OPEN TOO. EVEN IN THE DARKNESS I KNEW THAT TERRIBLE FACE, FOR I HAD SEEN IT EARLIER THAT DAY AS IT WRITHED IN ANGER. IT WAS THE FACE OF OLD FARMER SPEIGHT, AND HE WAS DEAD.
